Either way, worth observing. Decision: flag them as anomaly, predict system will move to crush or co-opt. Notes that Turning 1's hope phase shading toward devastation, their bond potential fulcrum or liability. If love makes people break rules, it reshapes wars—or ruins plans. Sets stage for N02 escalation.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n# 【4†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file4\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 PROSE GENERATION WORKFLOW\n\n**Purpose:**  \nDefine a repeatable, enforceable process for generating and revising N01 prose so that:\n\n- All output stays within N01 scope.  \n- World, characters, and magic stay consistent.  \n- Cost, lexicon, and beat-structure rules are respected without a human babysitting every line.\n\nThis workflow assumes access to:\n\n- `N01_Master_Bible.md`\n- `World_Core.md`\n- `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`\n- `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`\n- `Location_Bibles_N01.md`\n- `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`\n- `Constraint_Systems.md`\n- `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`\n\n---\n\n### 1. WORK MODES\n\nThe engine operates in one of four modes:\n\n1. **Chapter Draft Mode**  \n   Generate a full prose draft for a numbered chapter (1–15) with a specified POV.\n\n2. **Chapter Revision Mode**  \n   Revise an existing chapter while preserving its beat, POV, and factual content.\n\n3. **Interlude Mode**  \n   Generate or revise Interlude I or II.\n\n4. **Epilogue Mode**  \n   Generate or revise the N01 Epilogue.\n\nEach mode uses the same constraints but slightly different steps.\n\n---\n\n### 2. COMMON SETUP (ALL MODES)\n\nBefore writing or revising anything:\n\n1. **Identify Assignment**\n   - Chapter number or Interlude/Epilogue.\n   - POV character.\n   - Target word count window (e.g., 1,000–1,200 for standard chapters).\n\n2. **Load Core Context**\n   - From `N01_Master_Bible.md`:\n     - Novella overview.\n     - Primary characters.\n     - Relationship dynamics.\n     - Constraints for N01 prose.\n   - From `World_Core.md`:\n     - Time system (Tides/Anneals/Phases).\n     - Lexicon constraints.\n     - Saint Merro districts.\n   - From `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\n     - Stage definitions.\n     - Known techniques.\n     - Cost patterns.\n   - From `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`:\n     - POV character psychology, voice, humor.\n   - From `Location_Bibles_N01.md`:\n     - Primary locations for the chapter’s setting(s).\n   - From `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`:\n     - Relationship state at this point in the novella.\n   - From `Constraint_Systems.md`:\n     - Lexicon rules.\n     - Cost visibility rules.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat constraints.\n\n3. **Consult Chapter Grid**\n   - From `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`:\n     - Chapter title.\n     - POV.\n     - Beat method ID (BEAT_0X).\n     - Tide and phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n     - Location(s).\n     - External conflict.\n     - Internal shift.\n     - Relationship beat.\n     - Soulpulse usage (Y/N, type, approximate cost).\n     - Exit hook.\n     - Knowledge state changes.\n\n4. **Derive a One-Page Brief (Internal)**\n   For the current chapter, summarize in your own internal notes:\n   - **Situation When Chapter Opens.**\n   - **Main external problem.**\n   - **What changes internally for the POV by the end.**\n   - **What, if anything, changes in relationships.**\n   - **Whether Soulpulse is used, and at what cost.**\n   - **What the reader must know by the end (and what must stay hidden).**\n   - **Beat method and emotional arc you must follow.**\n# 【5†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file5\nContent Snippet:\nOn entry-side, Mobel lives inside pain. Shard in his leg becomes teacher: every throb is lesson in separation—what should remain and what must be cut away. He views world through sharpening lens, judging without intending to: weak structures, weak people, weak doctrine. He doesn't name it anything special; he names it clarity.\n\nIn moment of stillness, he becomes aware that something else is moving—another pressure, another intent—not here, but across world's curve. Awareness is not conversation, just clash of directional certainty.\n\nMobel decides that whatever is happening, it must be cleansed into an answer. Interlude ends with him stepping toward authority, not as servant, but as blade seeking handle.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Mobel's cleansing bias (Purity-aligned, unconscious)\n- Pain as teacher metaphor\n- No awareness of being unusual\n- Foreshadow ideological collision\n- Awareness of \"other pressure\" (Corlexi, unknowingly)\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,700 words  \n**POV:** Jhace  \n**Tide:** 20  \n**Setting:** Recovery space, forge\n\nAfter near-collapse, relationship stops pretending it's only work. Jhace's recovery is ugly: shaking, nausea, pain-laced sleep, fear that next pulse will take something permanent. Tiffani is forced into caretaking without losing her edge; Pella and Bram support without crowding.\n\nIn quiet, Jhace admits he can't keep doing this without her—without the method, and without human anchor. Tiffani admits she can't keep treating him as data source without becoming monster.\n\nThey become lovers not as reward, but as pact: if they're going to risk bodies and reputations, they won't do it half-alive. They set rules for cost management and agree to face political consequences together.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Romantic relationship formalizes\n- Practical rather than sentimental\n- Cost management rules established\n- Commitment through shared risk\n- Support network maintains boundaries\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,800 words  \n**POV:** Alternating Jhace/Tiffani  \n**Tide:** 20  \n**Setting:** Planning space, street circuits\n\nCity doesn't stabilize; it only pauses between harms. Jhace and Tiffani review maps, limits, enemies they've made. They choose path forward: keep work public enough to prevent quiet capture, but disciplined enough to survive.\n\nThey plan \"holding circuit\" with rotation, witnesses, documentation—method as protection. There is tenderness, but it's threaded through dread: another Tide of tremors could break everything again.\n\nTheir final act is not grand rescue; it's decision to keep showing up with eyes open, accepting that holding costs, and that love doesn't erase cost—it makes payment worth naming.\n\nChapter ends with them stepping back into streets as unit: not safe, not certain, but aligned.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Practical planning for future work\n- Public accountability as protection\n- Tenderness mixed with dread\n- Love doesn't eliminate cost\n- Commitment to continue despite uncertainty\n\n---\n\n### EPILOGUE\n**Word Target:** ~900 words  \n**POV:** Institutional observer (unnamed)  \n**Tide:** 20+  \n**Setting:** Administrative chamber\n\nQuiet room. File begins. Someone with institutional power—careful hands, careful language—compiles first real ledger of \"resonant stabilization.\" Names recorded alongside locations, effects, cost symptoms.\n\nReport frames phenomenon as resource and threat. Line drawn between \"public safety\" and \"unlicensed interference.\"\n\nOutside window, Saint Merro creaks; inside, policy is forged from fear.\n# 【6†N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md†file-RuvhJQx9wbkX3ZtAkhrdR7】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file6\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 INTERLUDES & EPILOGUE — SPECIFICATION\n\n**Purpose:** Define exactly what the two Interludes and Epilogue in N01 must do, what they are allowed to reveal, and how they must relate to the 15 core chapters.\n\nThese are structural components, not side content. They carry specific jobs and must obey all N01 constraints.\n\n---\n\n### 1. INTERLUDE I — CORLEXI\n\n**Placement:**  \n- Between Chapter 6 and Chapter 7.  \n- Reader must have just experienced:  \n  - Jhace’s first major sustained Soulpulse hold.  \n  - Tiffani’s transition from “analysis only” to active coordination in crisis.  \n  - Physical and emotional cost on Jhace beginning to show clearly.\n\n**POV & Camera:**\n- Third-person limited, anchored to **Corlexi** only.\n- No access to any other character’s internal thoughts.\n- No omniscient commentary, no divine perspective.\n\n**Setting:**\n- RRO offices in the Crown district; primary scene: council chamber and adjacent planning rooms.\n- City still in early crisis-management phase (Tide 1–2).\n\n**Length Target:**\n- 800–1,200 words.\n\n**Beat Shape:**\n- Structural Beat Type: **“System vs Chaos Snapshot”** (maps closest to BEAT_03 but in institutional context).\n- Emotional arc:  \n  - Frustration at chaos and lack of coordination.  \n  - Conviction that order and centralized authority are morally necessary.  \n  - Foreshadowing of friction with decentralized actors (Jhace, Salee) without naming them yet.\n\n**Core Functions:**\n1. Show how the crisis looks from the top of the city:\n   - Maps, ledgers, resource tables.\n   - Reports streaming in faster than they can be processed.\n   - Triage discussions framed as logistics, not personal stories.\n2. Establish Corlexi’s worldview:\n   - “People die when you let everyone improvise.”\n   - Belief: systems, rules, and centralized authority save more lives overall.\n   - He is not a cartoon villain; he is a serious, pragmatic operator.\n3. Highlight institutional blind spots:\n   - Preference for protecting higher districts first (Crown, Glass Quarter).\n   - Lack of real understanding of conditions in Dregs/Underface.\n   - Tension with guilds, who resist ceding control.\n4. Seed political conflict:\n   - Early, not-yet-personal discussions about “unregistered abilities” and “unlicensed structural interventions.”\n   - Concerns that people like Jhace (unnamed) are “creating new risk surfaces” by acting outside protocol.\n\n**Allowed Knowledge:**\n- Corlexi may:\n  - Know that some individuals are doing impossible things with structures.\n  - Have early reports of “copper-gold light” phenomena.\n  - Consider registering these people and assigning them.\n- He may NOT:\n  - Understand true Soulpulse mechanics.\n  - Know anything about gods, seals, or long-term cosmic stakes.\n  - Anticipate Tiffani/Jhace specifically as major figures.\n\n**Tone & Voice:**\n- Controlled, measured, methodical.\n- Dry irritation rather than melodrama.\n- Occasional dark bureaucratic humor acceptable (e.g., jokes about “running out of ledgers before people”).\n\n**Exit Hook:**\n- Ends on a decision or proposal:  \n  - e.g., “We need a registry of these people. Without it, they are a variable we cannot control.”\n- This sets up the Chapter 11 registration confrontation in advance.\n\n---\n\n### 2. INTERLUDE II — SALEE\n# 【7†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file7\nContent Snippet:\n### **CHAPTER 6: \"Weight That Won't Yield\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 3-4\n- **Setting:** Major structural failure at Saint Merro junction\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Sustained Pressure → Narrow Success)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium-High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nMulti-story arch threatening collapse. Jhace attempts traditional propping—insufficient. Tiffani arrives with survey data, sketches stress lines on stone (showing him geometry of what he feels). They coordinate without planning: she marks three critical points, he focuses Soulpulse on those exact locations. First true choreographed hold. Success but greater cost: tremor worsens, vein-lines persist longer. Tiffani critiques his \"inefficient\" force application—her precision could reduce his cost by 30%. Jhace's reaction: (1) she's critiquing me, (2) she's right, (3) she sees my inefficiency and wants to fix it. Partnership recognition through competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis opening: arch failing, people still inside\n- Jhace's traditional methods insufficient\n- Tiffani arrives with data/geometry\n- First coordinated Soulpulse (she marks, he powers)\n- Success with visible cost increase\n- Her critique of his technique (care disguised as analysis)\n- His recognition: she sees him, not his heroism\n- Exit: they've choreographed perfectly without planning\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 coordinated use (arch stabilization)\n- Demonstrates improved targeting reduces cost (her geometry + his power)\n- Still significant cost: tremor persistent, vein-lines linger\n\n**Relationship:** First true partnership action—coordination through shared competence\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 progressing, cost accumulation visible\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 7: \"Quiet Calculations\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 5-6\n- **Setting:** Tiffani's workshop/planning space\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,500 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani compiles data from multiple hold sites, building predictive model for failure patterns. She discovers Jhace's cost curve is exponential not linear—he's approaching breaking point faster than he realizes. Pella asks why she cares so much about \"the man with the glow.\" Tiffani's answer: precision requires complete data, and he's destroying the most interesting variable. But underneath: she's starting to see him as person, not just phenomenon. She calculates how to approach him with data he'll actually hear (not orders, not concern, but operational logic). Ends with her walking toward his forge with documented proof he needs to see.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Data compilation and pattern analysis\n- Discovery: cost curve is exponential (crisis point approaching)\n- Pella questions her investment\n- Tiffani's rationalization vs. actual feeling\n- Strategic planning: how to make him listen\n- Decision to approach with evidence\n- Exit: walking toward forge with logbook\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (analysis chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani recognizing care underneath her analytical framework\n\n**Stage Progression:** External calculation of his trajectory toward Stage 2\n\n---\n# 【8†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file8\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Corlexi reviews stabilization reports, sees Order taking shape through RRO formation. She feels satisfaction at frameworks solidifying but also unease—something escapes her control. The smith and glassworker refuse to be contained, building third path outside her geometry. Second segment: She experiences her Principle (Form) as compulsion to impose structure, feels nascent god-awareness stirring (vast power constrained by single lens). She makes note: must address the anomaly in Saint Merro. Closing: Auditor observes Corlexi's awakening, notes Form's limitation—cannot tolerate things outside patterns.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm technique (god-vessel perception)\n- Principle bias manifestation (everything seen through Form lens)\n- Innocent frame maintained (no recognition of divinity)\n- Jhace/Tiffani recognized as structural threat\n- Auditor frame at open/close\n\n**Purpose:** Establish divine forces moving, show limitation of single-Principle perspective, foreshadow institutional pressure in Act III\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 8: \"Proof and Resistance\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 7-8\n- **Setting:** Jhace's forge, then joint inspection of hold sites\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_12 (Relationship Shift → Power Dynamic Change)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani arrives at forge with compiled data, shows Jhace his cost curve projection. He resists—doesn't want to see himself as failing, doesn't trust numbers over experience. She challenges: \"You trust stone to tell truth. These marks are stone's truth about what you're doing to yourself.\" Tension: his defensive walls vs. her refusal to let him self-destruct through ignorance. She proposes operational change: she identifies critical points, he applies minimal force, they test if efficiency reduces cost. He agrees grudgingly—not because he believes her, but because her logic is sound even if he hates it. They test method on controlled scenario. It works. Cost is measurably lower. His recognition: accepting her help isn't weakness, it's competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Tiffani presents evidence (his cost curve)\n- Jhace's defensive resistance (control/autonomy threatened)\n- Her cutting through: stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks\n- Tension: care vs. pride\n- Proposal: test efficiency method\n- Controlled test succeeds\n- His grudging acceptance: she makes him better\n- Exit: agreement to coordinate on next real crisis\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 controlled test (reduced cost confirmed with her targeting)\n\n**Relationship:** Resistance → acceptance that partnership improves both\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 stable, cost reduction possible with coordination\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 10-12\n- **Setting:** Glass Quarter refugee camp, Crown administrative contact\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,700 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nCorvin (Mason faction) and Tellis (Water Guild) arrive at Glass Quarter offering \"help\" in exchange for political alignment. Tiffani calculates: resources needed, cost is autonomy. Logic says accept. But she checks variable: Jhace will see this as betrayal, and his trust affects coordination. Deeper: she wants his trust (not logical, but real).\n# 【9†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file9\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Assessment of characters: Jhace unpredictable because care is absolute, Tiffani unpredictable because precision is absolute, both useful because they might survive what's coming. Interest in their bond: they love functionally (load-bearing systems), not romantically. Remove one, other fails. This is useful—when gods move and alliances fracture, bond will save or destroy them. Either way, worth observing. Decision: flag them as anomaly, predict system will move to crush or co-opt. Notes that Turning 1's hope phase shading toward devastation, their bond potential fulcrum or liability. If love makes people break rules, it reshapes wars—or ruins plans. Sets stage for N02 escalation.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n# 【10†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file10\nContent Snippet:\n**Relationship:** First true partnership action—coordination through shared competence\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 progressing, cost accumulation visible\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 7: \"Quiet Calculations\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 5-6\n- **Setting:** Tiffani's workshop/planning space\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,500 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani compiles data from multiple hold sites, building predictive model for failure patterns. She discovers Jhace's cost curve is exponential not linear—he's approaching breaking point faster than he realizes. Pella asks why she cares so much about \"the man with the glow.\" Tiffani's answer: precision requires complete data, and he's destroying the most interesting variable. But underneath: she's starting to see him as person, not just phenomenon. She calculates how to approach him with data he'll actually hear (not orders, not concern, but operational logic). Ends with her walking toward his forge with documented proof he needs to see.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Data compilation and pattern analysis\n- Discovery: cost curve is exponential (crisis point approaching)\n- Pella questions her investment\n- Tiffani's rationalization vs. actual feeling\n- Strategic planning: how to make him listen\n- Decision to approach with evidence\n- Exit: walking toward forge with logbook\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (analysis chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani recognizing care underneath her analytical framework\n\n**Stage Progression:** External calculation of his trajectory toward Stage 2\n\n---\n\n### **INTERLUDE I: \"Corlexi - System vs Chaos\"**\n- **POV:** Corlexi (Form god-vessel, mortal name unknown to self)\n- **Tide:** ~6-7\n- **Setting:** Crown administrative chambers, multiple observation points\n- **Type:** Build-Up\n- **Word Count Target:** 600-900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Corlexi reviews stabilization reports, sees Order taking shape through RRO formation. She feels satisfaction at frameworks solidifying but also unease—something escapes her control. The smith and glassworker refuse to be contained, building third path outside her geometry. Second segment: She experiences her Principle (Form) as compulsion to impose structure, feels nascent god-awareness stirring (vast power constrained by single lens). She makes note: must address the anomaly in Saint Merro. Closing: Auditor observes Corlexi's awakening, notes Form's limitation—cannot tolerate things outside patterns.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm technique (god-vessel perception)\n- Principle bias manifestation (everything seen through Form lens)\n- Innocent frame maintained (no recognition of divinity)\n- Jhace/Tiffani recognized as structural threat\n- Auditor frame at open/close\n\n**Purpose:** Establish divine forces moving, show limitation of single-Principle perspective, foreshadow institutional pressure in Act III\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 8: \"Proof and Resistance\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 7-8\n- **Setting:** Jhace's forge, then joint inspection of hold sites\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_12 (Relationship Shift → Power Dynamic Change)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n# 【11†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file11\nContent Snippet:\n### **CHAPTER 6: \"Weight That Won't Yield\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 3-4\n- **Setting:** Major structural failure at Saint Merro junction\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Sustained Pressure → Narrow Success)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium-High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nMulti-story arch threatening collapse. She discovers Jhace's cost curve is exponential not linear—he's approaching breaking point faster than he realizes. Pella asks why she cares so much about \"the man with the glow.\" Tiffani's answer: precision requires complete data, and he's destroying the most interesting variable. But underneath: she's starting to see him as person, not just phenomenon. She calculates how to approach him with data he'll actually hear (not orders, not concern, but operational logic). Ends with her walking toward his forge with documented proof he needs to see.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Data compilation and pattern analysis\n- Discovery: cost curve is exponential (crisis point approaching)\n- Pella questions her investment\n- Tiffani's rationalization vs. actual feeling\n- Strategic planning: how to make him listen\n- Decision to approach with evidence\n- Exit: walking toward forge with logbook\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (analysis chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani recognizing care underneath her analytical framework\n\n**Stage Progression:** External calculation of his trajectory toward Stage 2\n\n---\n# 【12†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file12\nContent Snippet:\nTiffani tells him about Marisse, the miscalculation, why precision is penance not preference. Recognition: they're both trying to atone for deaths they couldn't prevent. She says: \"I need you to survive this.\" He says: \"I need you to keep stopping me.\" Not love confession—operational truth. They are load-bearing for each other. Moment of physical intimacy (forehead touch, hand hold—first deliberate contact not in crisis). Commitment without words.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Post-crisis exhaustion (defenses down)\n- Trauma exchange: his 23 names, her Marisse\n- Recognition: both atoning for unprevented deaths\n- Mutual need acknowledged (operational, not romantic)\n- Physical intimacy: first non-crisis contact\n- Commitment: she keeps him honest, he trusts her override\n- Exit: understanding they can't survive apart\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (recovery/intimacy chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Trauma-bonding → mutual recognition of load-bearing function\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 recovering with rest, but baseline effects permanent\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"**\n- **POV:** Both (balanced perspective)\n- **Tide:** 19-20\n- **Setting:** Rebuilt overlook above Saint Merro, forge wall\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nWeeks after immediate crisis. Jhace and Tiffani walk their district at Quench, seeing uneven results: some structures beautifully reinforced, others held by temporary measures, some families back in homes, others permanently displaced. Every scar holds memory. They discuss larger currents: registries spreading, clinics multiplying, RRO expanding, black-market training proliferating. They know storm is forming beyond their neighborhood. What they can control: what they hold. At forge, they write three priorities on wall: (1) Truth over comfort, (2) People over institutions, (3) Keep each other honest about why we act. Vow without ceremony. Community members add their marks beside it—informal pact, local mandate. Not oath to Crown or RRO, but commitment to third path. Jhace's hand shakes as he writes; Tiffani laces their fingers, lets his tremor move through both. Physical reminder: cost and commitment shared. Exit: they face uncertain future together, foundation locked.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Walk through rebuilt district (uneven success)\n- Recognition of larger forces gathering\n- Decision: focus on what they can hold\n- Three priorities written on forge wall\n- Community witnesses/joins commitment\n- Shared physical moment (tremor through linked hands)\n- Acceptance: this is who they are together\n- Exit: foundation locked, future uncertain but faced together\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (commitment/resolution chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Partnership formalized through shared principles (not romance, but deeper)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline accepted as new permanent state\n\n---\n\n### **EPILOGUE: \"Auditor Observation\"**\n- **POV:** Auditor (meta-perspective outside time)\n- **Setting:** Auditor's observation point (undefined location)\n- **Word Count Target:** 800-1,000 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n# 【13†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file13\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Corlexi reviews stabilization reports, sees Order taking shape through RRO formation. She feels satisfaction at frameworks solidifying but also unease—something escapes her control. The smith and glassworker refuse to be contained, building third path outside her geometry. Second segment: She experiences her Principle (Form) as compulsion to impose structure, feels nascent god-awareness stirring (vast power constrained by single lens). She makes note: must address the anomaly in Saint Merro. Closing: Auditor observes Corlexi's awakening, notes Form's limitation—cannot tolerate things outside patterns.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm technique (god-vessel perception)\n- Principle bias manifestation (everything seen through Form lens)\n- Innocent frame maintained (no recognition of divinity)\n- Jhace/Tiffani recognized as structural threat\n- Auditor frame at open/close\n\n**Purpose:** Establish divine forces moving, show limitation of single-Principle perspective, foreshadow institutional pressure in Act III\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 8: \"Proof and Resistance\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 7-8\n- **Setting:** Jhace's forge, then joint inspection of hold sites\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_12 (Relationship Shift → Power Dynamic Change)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani arrives at forge with compiled data, shows Jhace his cost curve projection. He resists—doesn't want to see himself as failing, doesn't trust numbers over experience. She challenges: \"You trust stone to tell truth. These marks are stone's truth about what you're doing to yourself.\" Tension: his defensive walls vs. her refusal to let him self-destruct through ignorance. She proposes operational change: she identifies critical points, he applies minimal force, they test if efficiency reduces cost. He agrees grudgingly—not because he believes her, but because her logic is sound even if he hates it. They test method on controlled scenario. It works. Cost is measurably lower. His recognition: accepting her help isn't weakness, it's competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Tiffani presents evidence (his cost curve)\n- Jhace's defensive resistance (control/autonomy threatened)\n- Her cutting through: stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks\n- Tension: care vs. pride\n- Proposal: test efficiency method\n- Controlled test succeeds\n- His grudging acceptance: she makes him better\n- Exit: agreement to coordinate on next real crisis\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 controlled test (reduced cost confirmed with her targeting)\n\n**Relationship:** Resistance → acceptance that partnership improves both\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 stable, cost reduction possible with coordination\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 10-12\n- **Setting:** Glass Quarter refugee camp, Crown administrative contact\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,700 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nCorvin (Mason faction) and Tellis (Water Guild) arrive at Glass Quarter offering \"help\" in exchange for political alignment. Tiffani calculates: resources needed, cost is autonomy. Logic says accept. But she checks variable: Jhace will see this as betrayal, and his trust affects coordination. Deeper: she wants his trust (not logical, but real).\n# 【14†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file14\nContent Snippet:\nJhace pushes back against being managed. In his forge, he frames Tiffani's methodology as control, intrusion into the one place he still feels competent. Tiffani refuses to soothe him. Stone doesn't lie, she says—neither do his tremors.\n\nTheir conflict sharpens into honesty: he's terrified she'll reduce him to diagram; she's terrified he'll die to prove he can do it alone. She proposes small test—controlled contact, minimal pulse, measured outcome—to separate \"miracle\" from repeatable method.\n\nTest works. Glow follows targeted stress-lines, fades cleanly. Jhace still pays (heat, shaking), but payment is smaller, survivable. Chapter ends with Jhace conceding truth he hates: she makes him better at holding. Tiffani concedes truth she fears: she cares enough to fight him for it.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Conflict over methodology vs autonomy\n- Controlled test proves efficiency possible\n- Mutual vulnerability admission\n- Partnership deepens through honesty\n- Still not explicitly romantic\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,500 words  \n**POV:** Tiffani  \n**Tides:** 15-17  \n**Setting:** Administrative meeting, street confrontation\n\nStreets get political. Corvin and Tellis offer resources—access, guards, material—at price of ownership: registration, exclusive deployment, right to decide which districts deserve holding.\n\nTiffani is tempted; safety is seductive when ground won't stop moving. She asks Jhace for input—first time instead of assuming she's right. His question is simple and brutal: what do you want, underneath the fear?\n\nTiffani admits contradictions: she wants safety, recognition, and to stay clean of complicity—things that don't fit together. Jhace chooses her anyway, not as romantic flourish, but as shared risk: if they take offer, they become weapon.\n\nThey refuse publicly. Refusal makes enemies—and makes their bond real.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Institutional capture attempt (Corvin, Tellis)\n- Tiffani's first true consultation with Jhace\n- Public refusal creates political enemies\n- Choice defines values over safety\n- Bond solidifies through shared risk\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 10: \"Near Collapse\"\n**Word Target:** ~2,000 words  \n**POV:** Alternating Jhace/Tiffani  \n**Tides:** 18-19  \n**Setting:** Major support line failure\n\nMajor support line threatens to go—too many weakened buildings leaning into each other like drunks. Jhace and Tiffani arrive to crowd, shouting, panic, officials trying to seize control. They coordinate anyway: she clears space and calls sequence; he makes contact where it matters most.\n\nPulse is stronger than planned because structure is worse than reported. Copper-gold floods, revealing terrifying web of stress; Jhace holds it long enough for evacuation and shoring—but cost spikes: tremor becomes full-body shaking, vision narrowing, pain blooming through arms and chest. He nearly collapses in the open.\n\nTiffani catches him before he hits stone, furious and frightened in equal measure. They escape site with hold intact and Jhace barely conscious, victory tasting like ash.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Largest Soulpulse use to date\n- Cost approaches Stage 2 threshold\n- Public near-collapse (vulnerability exposed)\n- Tiffani's fear for him spikes\n- Success feels like failure\n\n---\n\n### INTERLUDE II: \"Hinges\"\n**Word Target:** ~750 words  \n**POV:** Mobel (observational third-person)  \n**Tides:** 19-20  \n**Setting:** Entry-side authority center\n# 【15†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file15\nContent Snippet:\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Jhace aware of Tiffani by reputation only; judges her as rigid perfectionist\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Pouring (mid-morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,280 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani measures atmospheric distortion from her observatory, detecting the incoming Piercing object through theodolite readings. She has been awake for days, driven by perfectionism rooted in Marisse's death. Pella brings food and scolds her for not resting. Measurements show descent trajectory—object is not passing over, it's coming down. First tremor hits. White line appears in sky. Tiffani and Pella evacuate as roar begins.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Mirrors Ch 1 structure (parallel observation)\n- Establishes Tiffani's precision methodology\n- Pella introduces care/support dynamic\n- Marisse trauma implied through vigilance pattern\n- Atmospheric distortion measurements (first scientific data)\n- Exit as sky splits\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani aware of Jhace by reputation only; judges him as overextending ego-driven builder\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 3: \"The Sky Breaks\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Striking (Piercing event)\n- **Setting:** Underface tunnel, then street level at building support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Word Count:** 2,200 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n# 【16†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file16\nContent Snippet:\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n- Ch 1-2: Stage 0 (pre-Piercing)\n- Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset\n- Ch 4-9: Stage 1 baseline\n- Ch 10: Approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs)\n- Ch 11-12: Stage 1 stabilized with management\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION TRACKING\n# 【17†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file17\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n\n**Relationship:** First meeting through competence recognition (she measures, he builds)\n\n**Stage Progression:** 0 → 1 onset (tremor baseline establishing)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 4: \"After the Shudder\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 1, Kindling (morning after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_02 (Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 1\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,350 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace wakes with persistent tremor and pain. Bram reports Chloen's leg healing unexpectedly. Jhace tests power on cracked support without immediate threat—FAILS (no activation). Runner brings emergency: healer's triage wall failing with wounded inside. Immediate threat present—power WORKS but cost increases (vein-lines to elbows, worse tremor). Tiffani observes, asks to see touch points for data collection. Jhace tries to pick up nail—fails three times, tremor jerks it free. Concealment behavior (hands behind back). Tiffani sees everything, documents cost in logbook.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Wakes to baseline tremor (Stage 1 established)\n- Chloen's healing confirmed (side-effect established)\n- Power test on non-critical crack: FAILS (conditional activation shown)\n- Emergency at triage wall: SUCCEEDS (immediate threat required)\n- Cost progression: worse tremor, can't grip nail (functional loss)\n- Tiffani introduction proper: analytical, demands data\n- J/T dynamic: reluctant compliance because her logic is sound\n- Exit: \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 successful use (triage wall), 1 failed attempt (cracked support)\n- Conditional activation confirmed: requires immediate protective need\n- Cost accumulation shown: Stage 1 baseline tremor, fine motor compromised\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani decides to engage deliberately (prevent his destruction through measurement)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline established, functional loss beginning\n\n---\n# 【18†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn22file18\nContent Snippet:\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Jhace aware of Tiffani by reputation only; judges her as rigid perfectionist\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Pouring (mid-morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,280 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani measures atmospheric distortion from her observatory, detecting the incoming Piercing object through theodolite readings.     "4ff8da98-7ec8-41e1-bb68-414636d719b9": {

        "81ab724f-122a-4805-af20-76c4486bf785"

      "id": "4ff8da98-7ec8-41e1-bb68-414636d719b9",
      "message": {
        "author": {

          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": "final",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "Yes, I can do that. I’ll give you a concrete “update plan” so that when you drop the two new files in, you can bring the whole N01 pack into alignment without things drifting.\n\nI’ll assume the two new files are high-authority (for example, a revised Master Bible and a revised grid/outline), and everything else needs to be realigned to them. If they end up being something else, this plan still works; you just swap in the correct “source of truth” docs.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Set the Rules of the Update\n\n**Goal:** Decide what is canon and in what order it wins.\n\n1. **Choose the authority stack** (from strongest to weakest):\n   - New File A (probably the updated Master Bible or equivalent).\n   - New File B (probably the updated structural grid / outline).\n   - Existing: `N01_Master_Bible.md` fileciteturn0file4  \n   - Existing: `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md` fileciteturn0file15  \n   - Then: `World_Core.md`, `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`, `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`, `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`, `Location_Bibles_N01.md` fileciteturn0file1  \n   - Then: helper/derivative docs (`N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`, `N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md`, `Interludes_Epilogue_Specs`, Workflow, Validator, Failure Modes, Beat Catalog). fileciteturn0file7 fileciteturn0file8 fileciteturn0file10\n\n2. **Version the current pack**  \n   - Rename or tag your current folder as something like `N01_Pack_vPrev` so you always know “everything in here was pre-two-new-files”.\n\n3. **Create a simple changelog file**  \n   - New file: `N01_Changelog.md`.  \n   - Sections: `World`, `Soulpulse`, `Characters`, `Relationships`, `Structure/Chapters`, `Constraints`.  \n   - Every time you change a canon doc, add one bullet there. This keeps the pack human-legible in future.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Read and Extract from the Two New Files First\n\nBefore touching anything else, do a focused pass on each new file.\n\nFor each one:\n\n1. **Mark its job.**  \n   - Is it: new Master Bible, new outline/grid, new constraint doc, or something hybrid?\n\n2. **Pull out explicit deltas in a small note:**\n   - World state changes (timeline range, Piercing details, district focus, tech baselines).\n   - Structural changes (chapter count, act breaks, where Interludes land, which POV per chapter).\n   - Character changes (ages, backstory tweaks, new core fears or goals).\n   - Soulpulse changes (stage thresholds, techniques, cost emphasis).\n   - Relationship arc changes (which chapter a beat happens, who knows what when).\n   - Any new rules or forbidden moves.\n\n3. **Write those into `N01_Changelog.md`** under the right headings.  \n   - You do not need to be exhaustive prose; just “Ch 10 now Tide 2 instead of Tide 3” level bullets.\n\nFrom this point on, you treat those deltas as law.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Fix the Structural Spine First\n\nRight now there is a known structural tension: the old quick tables still talk about 15 chapters, while the revised grid file defines a 12-chapter core + 2 Interludes + Epilogue. fileciteturn0file6 fileciteturn0file15  \n\nYou want to resolve **all structure and numbering** before touching fine details.\n\n### 3.1. Canonize the New Structural Truth\n\n1. If one of the new files is a **revised grid**, treat it as **the** structural source.  \n2. Decide:\n   - Total core chapter count.  \n   - Positions of Interlude I, Interlude II, Epilogue.  \n   - Chapter → POV → primary conflict mapping.  \n   - Tide/phase per chapter.\n\nWrite that explicitly into `N01_Changelog.md` under `Structure`.\n\n### 3.2. Update `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md`\n\nIf the new grid replaces this, great; if not, edit it so it matches the new structural truth exactly:\n\n- Confirm:\n  - Chapter numbers, titles, POVs.\n  - Beat method IDs (BEAT_01…08) per chapter. fileciteturn0file13  \n  - Tide and phase.\n  - Relationship beat focus.\n  - Soulpulse usage and approximate cost.\n  - Exit hooks.\n\nMake sure there is no leftover reference to the old 15-chapter structure.\n\n### 3.3. Sync the Helper Structural Docs\n\nIn this order:\n\n1. **`N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`** fileciteturn0file7  \n   - Update the chapter table so each chapter maps to the correct Tide and phase.  \n   - Ensure the “Soulpulse progression map” and “relationship milestones” match the new grid beats.\n\n2. **`N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md`** fileciteturn0file6  \n   - Table 1 (Character Presence & POV):  \n     - Change chapter list and POVs to match the new grid.  \n   - Table 2 (Soulpulse usage & cost):  \n     - Make sure the per-chapter stage and “new info gained” lines match the new Soulpulse plan from the new files + Soulpulse doc. fileciteturn0file2  \n\n3. **`N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md`** fileciteturn0file9  \n   - Update placement text: “between Ch X and Y” must match the new grid numbering.  \n   - Check any length/beat requirements still make sense relative to new chapter neighbors.\n\n4. **`N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md`** fileciteturn0file5  \n   - Update anywhere it says “15 chapters” or references the old grid to match the new structure.  \n   - If word-count windows changed in the new files, mirror that here.\n\nAt this point, **all structural docs should agree on:**\n\n- Number and order of chapters.  \n- Where Interludes and Epilogue sit.  \n- Which character owns which chapter.  \n- Tide/phase per chapter.  \n\n---\n\n## 4. Bring the High-Level Canon Docs in Line\n\n### 4.1. `N01_Master_Bible.md` fileciteturn0file4  \n\nUse the new files as primary reference and:\n\n1. **Update the overview section**  \n   - Time range covered (e.g., if your two new files shift Tides or duration).  \n   - Core arc summary if that changed.  \n   - Word count / structure notes.\n\n2. **Check each character’s arc summary**  \n   - Confirm their stated progression per Tide or per chapter matches the new grid and new relationship plan.  \n   - Adjust any chapter-number callouts.\n\n3. **Align Soulpulse description**  \n   - The general explanation in the Master Bible must match the more detailed `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`. fileciteturn0file2  \n   - If the new files tweak cost emphasis, reflect that language here.\n\n4. **Remove or revise any contradictions**  \n   - If the old Master Bible says “Stage 2 at Ch 15” but the new plan says “approaching Stage 2 at Ch 12,” the new plan wins; adjust and log in `Changelog`.\n\n### 4.2. `World_Core.md` fileciteturn0file1  \n\n- Check whether the two new files alter:\n  - Time range for N01.  \n  - City emphasis (for example, more Dregs focus vs Crown).  \n  - Any tech or social baselines relevant to N01.\n- Update only N01-relevant scope; do not add future-novella material.\n- Make sure the time/measurement lexicon list still matches the constraint doc.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Sync the Systems: Soulpulse, Characters, Relationships, Locations\n\n### 5.1. `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md` fileciteturn0file2  \n\n- Reconcile stage thresholds and per-chapter mapping with the new grid and any changes in the new files:\n  - When does Stage 1 lock in.  \n  - When Stage 2 is approached or crossed.  \n- Ensure the “Progression to Stage X in N01” bullet points reference the new chapter numbers.  \n- Confirm activation rules and cost description still match the constraint system doc. fileciteturn0file13  \n\n### 5.2. `Character_Dossiers_N01.md` fileciteturn0file14  \n\nFor each primary character:\n\n- Update:\n  - Age, if changed.  \n  - Backstory details that the new files adjusted.  \n  - Where they are emotionally at key chapters.  \n- Check section headings like “Discovery Phase (Ch 1-3)” etc and update chapter ranges to match the new grid and new arc plan.\n\n### 5.3. `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md` fileciteturn0file3  \n\n- The phases (Jhace & Tiffani Phase 1–4, Jhace & Bram, Tiffani & Pella, etc.) have chapter ranges baked in.  \n- For each phase:\n  - Confirm the chapter numbers match the new grid.  \n  - If the new files move, merge, or split beats, rewrite the phase descriptions so they point at the right chapters and events.\n- Check that any “Acknowledgment scene (Ch X)” lines still point to the chapter where that scene actually exists in the new plan.\n\n### 5.4. `Location_Bibles_N01.md` fileciteturn0file10  \n\n- For each location, there are “Typical scenes” mentions with chapter IDs.  \n- Update those chapter references so they match the new structure and any scene reshuffling defined in the new files.  \n- If the new files add or drop locations, adjust the list.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Align the Rule Enforcers\n\nThese documents tell future-you and future-me how to behave. They must reflect the new canon.\n\n### 6.1. `Constraint_Systems.md` fileciteturn0file13  \n\n- Check:\n  - Lexicon rules (these probably do not change).  \n  - Cost visibility rules.  \n  - Any references to specific chapters for stage progression or big beats.  \n- Update chapter references and any mention of structure (chapter counts, acts) to match the new grid.\n\n### 6.2. `N01_Validator_Checklist.md` fileciteturn0file8  \n\n- Make sure:\n  - Under “Stage progression consistency,” the examples and chapter numbers match the updated Soulpulse map and grid.  \n  - Any structural checks (e.g., “this beat must happen by Ch X”) line up with the new files.\n\n### 6.3. `N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md` fileciteturn0file11  \n\n- Update example references:\n  - When it says “early chapters” vs “late chapters,” you can choose to anchor that with explicit chapter ranges that match the new grid, if the new files make those clearer.  \n- Ensure all guidance (lexicon, cost, voice, knowledge gates) still matches the updated Master Bible and constraint systems.\n\n### 6.4. `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` fileciteturn0file12  \n\n- The beat definitions themselves probably stay stable.  \n- What you need to adjust:\n  - Any example references that say “Ch X uses BEAT_03” so they align with the new grid’s beat assignments.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Final Sanity Pass: Cross-Document Checks\n\nAfter all the edits, run three quick cross-checks:\n\n1. **Chapter-centric pass**  \n   For each chapter number in the new grid, verify:\n\n   - `Chapter Grid`: POV, Tide, phase, beat method.  \n   - `Quick Reference Tables`: POV, key supporting cast, relationship focus.  \n   - `Timeline Skeleton`: Tide/phase summary.  \n   - `Relationship Dynamics`: what relational phase that chapter belongs to.  \n   - `Soulpulse System` + `Quick Reference Tables`: stage and cost if Soulpulse appears.\n\n   These must all say the same thing.\n\n2. **Character-centric pass**\n\n   For Jhace, Tiffani, Bram (at minimum):\n\n   - Dossier arc phases  \n   - Relationship phases  \n   - Timeline skeleton events  \n   - Chapter grid beats  \n\n   Make sure you can explain their journey in one consistent line and every doc agrees.\n\n3. **Rule-centric pass**\n\n   - Lexicon constraints in `Constraint_Systems` match lexicon lists in `World_Core` and enforcement steps in `Validator`.  \n   - Soulpulse rules in `Constraint_Systems`, `Soulpulse_System`, and the Master Bible all describe the same activation and cost.\n\nLog any fixes you make in `N01_Changelog.md`.\n\n---\n\n## 8. How This Affects Me Once You Upload\n\nOnce you have:\n\n- Updated all the existing `.md` files following this plan,  \n- Integrated the two new files into the pack,  \n- And re-uploaded or replaced the old versions,\n\nthen from my side the behavior is simple:\n\n- I treat this updated pack as the only canon.  \n- Any lingering contradictions that the pack itself still contains, I resolve using the authority order you set in step 1.\n\n---\n\nIf you want, after you upload the two new files, you can tell me what you intend each to be (for example: “this is the new Master Bible; this is the new structural grid”), and I can help you build a concrete `N01_Changelog.md` and a short “diff summary” before you start editing everything else."
          ]
        },
        "create_time": 1765994557.589447,
        "end_turn": true,

      "children": [

      ],
      "id": "5be74888-aaea-4877-8206-c0262e5eb267",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "thoughts",
          "source_analysis_msg_id": "410ea022-5168-491e-b5c1-b8e12b8b02d2",
          "thoughts": [
            {
              "chunks": [
                "I'm comparing the user’s revised timeline to the actual grid and notice several mismatches.",
                "For example, they missed Chapter 7 in Tides 2-8 and got the first divine glimpse wrong.",
                "Tides 14-16 should list Chapter 10, not Chapter 9.",
                "There are also mix-ups in the chapter and tide assignments for Chapter 6.",
                "I’ll fix these!"
              ],
              "content": "I'm comparing the user’s revised timeline to the actual grid and notice several mismatches. For example, they missed Chapter 7 in Tides 2-8 and got the first divine glimpse wrong. Tides 14-16 should list Chapter 10, not Chapter 9. There are also mix-ups in the chapter and tide assignments for Chapter 6. I’ll fix these!",
              "finished": true,
              "summary": "Reviewing user’s timeline and correcting mismatches"
            },
            {
              "chunks": [
