TIDE-LEVEL TIMELINE SNAPSHOT\n\nApproximate; exact phase mapping lives in `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`.\n\n| Tide | Phase(s) Primarily Covered          | Chapters            | Key Events                                      |\n|------|-------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------|\n| 0    | Late Kindling → Striking/Quench    | 1–3                 | Pre-Piercing baseline → The Piercing → first uses |\n| 1    | Kindling → Striking                | 4–6 + Interlude I   | Immediate aftermath, first big hold, institutional view |\n| 2    | Kindling → Quench                  | 7–10                | Training loop, failures, synthesis, mature rescue |\n| 3    | Pouring → Striking                 | 11 + Interlude II   | Registration attempt, Salee’s communal lens     |\n| 4    | Shaping → Striking                 | 12–13               | Ethical triage decisions, romantic acknowledgment |\n| 5    | Striking → Quench                  | 14–15 + Epilogue    | Major crisis, Stage 2 threshold, quiet commitment, Auditor assessment |\n\nUse this as a sanity check: if a chapter implies more time passing than this allows, fix the references.\n\n---\n# 【1†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file1\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nCritical main support failing during high-traffic period. Hundreds at risk. Jhace and Tiffani coordinate under pressure: she calculates load paths in real-time, he applies force to her exact specifications. They move as single unit, no wasted words, pure operational sync. Success—but Jhace pushes past safe limits to ensure no one dies. Cost: approaches Stage 2 threshold (tremor violent, vein-lines permanent at baseline, brief moment of sensory overload). Tiffani physically restrains him from continuing, makes decision FOR him: \"You're done. I don't care if you hate me for it.\" Saves him by overriding his control. Afterward, his rage at her presumption vs. her cold certainty she was right. Tension peaks: control vs. care, autonomy vs. survival.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis: hundreds at risk, main support failing\n- Perfect coordination under pressure\n- Jhace pushes past limits (caretaker compulsion)\n- Tiffani's override: physical intervention\n- Success but near-Stage 2 cost\n- His rage: she took his choice\n- Her certainty: he would have killed himself\n- Relationship stress: care as control\n- Exit: silent fury and mutual recognition they need each other\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 major use (near-Stage 2 threshold)\n- Coordinated efficiency maintains, but scale drives cost\n- Brief sensory overload (Stage 2 warning sign)\n- Vein-lines now faintly visible at rest\n\n**Relationship:** Conflict through care—she saves him by controlling him, he hates needing it\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 → approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs present)\n\n---\n\n### **INTERLUDE II: \"Mobel - Purity Rising\"**\n- **POV:** Mobel (Purity god-vessel, mortal name unknown to self)\n- **Tide:** ~15-16\n- **Setting:** Entry-side (opposite hemisphere from Saint Merro)\n- **Type:** Build-Up\n- **Word Count Target:** 600-900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Mobel leads cleansing ritual where early resonants prove themselves through enduring pain without using Soulpulse. She feels power of her Principle—clarity, judgment, separation of righteous from corrupt. Second segment: Moment of god-awareness—she senses Form-aligned power across planet (Corlexi stirring). Recognition: there are others like her, alliance or war inevitable. Principle limitation shown: sees only absolutes, cannot tolerate ambiguity. Auditor observes: Purity will clash with Form's institutional approach, both will target Jhace/Tiffani's third path.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm (pain as purification)\n- Purity Principle bias (absolute judgment)\n- First inter-god awareness\n- Innocent frame (no recognition of divinity)\n- Foreshadows ideological warfare\n- Auditor frame\n\n**Purpose:** Establish second divine force, show Principles in conflict, set up multi-front pressure on protagonists\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"**\n- **POV:** Both (intimate alternating perspective)\n- **Tide:** 17-18\n- **Setting:** Quiet space after crisis (Jhace's forge or Tiffani's workshop)\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nPost-Ch10 processing. Jhace's rage has cooled to exhaustion. Tiffani's certainty has cracked to fear—she nearly watched him die.\n# 【2†README.md†file-UHeggNd7WQHssWb89Y8SgW】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: README.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file2\nContent Snippet:\n# N01 – The Grip That Holds – Canonical Pack\n\nThis folder contains all canonical and structural files necessary for writing and revising the novella **N01: \"The Grip That Holds\"** in the Confluence Chronicles saga.\n\n## Files included\n\n- **World_Core.md** – Detailed description of Te’Oga world, city geography, Piercing event and effects, technology, social structures, time and measurement systems, and lexicon enforcement.\n- **Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md** – Empirical explanation of Soulpulse as understood during N01, including activation requirements, manifestations, cost stages, techniques, training observations, and limitations.\n- **Character_Dossiers_N01.md** – Psychological profiles, voice patterns, humor styles, and arcs for all characters appearing in N01: Jhace, Tiffani, Bram, and key supporting characters.\n- **Location_Bibles_N01.md** – Physical descriptions and functional details for locations in N01, including Underface tunnels, Saint Merro districts, RRO chambers, observatory, training rig, and typical crisis sites.\n- **Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md** – Tracks relationship arcs and phases (Jhace & Tiffani, Jhace & Bram, Tiffani & Pella, etc.) and maps character connections across chapters.\n- **Constraint_Systems.md** – Rules to enforce the Te’Oga lexicon, visible Soulpulse cost, knowledge gates, beat unpredictability, voice consistency, and validation—essential for maintaining continuity and immersion.\n- **N01_Master_Bible.md** – Comprehensive story bible summarising the novella overview, world state, characters, Soulpulse mechanics, relationships, chapter structure, themes, and constraints.\n- **N01_Chapter_Grid.md** – Beat-by-beat breakdown of all 15 chapters, plus interludes and epilogue, including scene structure, knowledge changes, and exit hooks.\n- **N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md** – Step-by-step instructions for drafting and revising prose, including modes (draft, revision, interlude, epilogue), common setup, and guidelines for structure and voice.\n- **N01_Validator_Checklist.md** – Mechanical validation checklist for any prose (chapter, interlude, or epilogue), ensuring lexicon compliance, cost visibility, voice discipline, knowledge gates, structural integrity, and timeline continuity.\n- **Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md** – Definitions and structures of all beat methods used in N01, with emotional arcs, best uses, and forbidden sequences.\n- **N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md** – Tide-by-Tide timeline for N01, listing event anchors, chapter phases, Soulpulse progression, relationship states by chapter, major events, and update rules.\n- **N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md** – Detailed guidelines for writing Interlude I, Interlude II, and the epilogue, specifying POV, tone, length, structure, allowed knowledge, and exit hooks.\n- **N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md** – A list of common mistakes in prose generation and validation, with detection methods and suggested fixes.\n- **N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md** – Concise tables summarising character presence per chapter, Soulpulse usage and cost by chapter, primary locations by chapter, and a tide-level timeline snapshot.\n\n## Notes\n\n- The orchestration notes (for human planners) are **not** included in this package. \n- The documents here represent the complete canonical and structural foundation for N01 without any meta-guidance. \n- Use these files to maintain consistency and fidelity when drafting or revising the novella.\n# 【3†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file3\nContent Snippet:\n# N01 - THE GRIP THAT HOLDS\n**Prose Generation Bible**\n\n## NOVELLA STRUCTURE\n\n- 12 chapters (1,200-2,200 words each)\n- 2 interludes (600-900 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,000 words)\n- Timeline: Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20\n- Setting: Saint Merro, Te'Oga's Crown\n\n---\n\n## TIMELINE\n\n**Tide 0:** Piercing event (Chapter 3)  \n**Tides 1-5:** Immediate aftermath, first Soulpulse uses  \n**Tides 6-10:** Partnership formation, coordinated abilities  \n**Tides 11-15:** Political pressure escalates  \n**Tides 16-20:** Commitment solidifies, future planning\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER SUMMARIES\n\n### CHAPTER 1: \"Something Wrong\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,240 words  \n**POV:** Jhace (tight third-person limited)  \n**Tide:** 0, Kindling (morning of Piercing entry)  \n**Setting:** Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection tunnel\n\nJhace works the Underface by feel—load, hum, stress—catching a wrong note in stone and glass that refuses to resolve. Bram needles him for working alone, but the teasing sits on an older truth: Jhace trusts structures more than people. Above, the city's supports feel tired; below, water and grit talk too loudly. The Tide's light shifts toward Kindling's end and the Underface grows tense—workers rushing, voices sharpened. Jhace marks cracks, makes tiny repairs that should be enough, then finds that \"enough\" won't hold. A tremor rolls through, not the worst, but wrong in rhythm. He climbs toward street level, trying to place the pattern in his mind. The chapter ends with him hearing the city like a living thing—holding its breath.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Jhace's craft mastery and pre-existing tremor\n- Establish 23 names burden (guilt from previous failure)\n- Show isolated working style\n- Foreshadow structural problems\n- Bram as mentor/caretaker figure\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,400 words  \n**POV:** Tiffani (tight third-person limited)  \n**Tide:** 0, Pouring  \n**Setting:** Observatory platform, Saint Merro rim\n\nTiffani measures the sky as if measurement can keep it honest. On an observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim, she tracks distortions and pressure shifts with obsessive vigilance. Pella—grounded, practical—keeps her fed, warm, and tethered to the present without insulting her fear. Tiffani's trauma surfaces as ritual: re-check, re-check, re-check. The air's refraction bends in ways her instruments can't comfortably name, and the horizon looks too thin. As the Tide moves toward Pouring, the sky's line fractures—first as a subtle wrongness, then as an event. Tiffani catches the first usable data of the world changing and realizes the numbers are not just \"bad.\" They're unprecedented. The exit begins.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Tiffani's measurement expertise\n- Establish 23 deaths trauma (parallel to Jhace)\n- Show Pella as support network\n- Foreshadow Piercing exit\n- Measurement as coping mechanism\n\n---\n# 【4†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file4\nContent Snippet:\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n- Ch 1-2: Stage 0 (pre-Piercing)\n- Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset\n- Ch 4-9: Stage 1 baseline\n- Ch 10: Approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs)\n- Ch 11-12: Stage 1 stabilized with management\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION TRACKING\n\n**Arc Across N01:**\n- Ch 1-2: External awareness (reputation only)\n- Ch 3: First meeting (competence recognition)\n- Ch 4-5: Deliberate observation (measurement phase)\n- Ch 6: First coordination (partnership action)\n- Ch 7-8: Trust building (resistance → acceptance)\n- Ch 9: First explicit choice (each other over safety)\n- Ch 10: Conflict through care (control vs. autonomy)\n- Ch 11: Trauma bonding (vulnerability exchange)\n- Ch 12: Commitment formalized (shared principles)\n\n**By N01 End:** Load-bearing partnership established, functional love (not romantic confession)\n\n---\n\n## WORD COUNT BUDGET\n\n| Segment | Target | Status |\n|---------|--------|--------|\n| Ch 1 | 1,240 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 2 | 1,280 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 3 | 2,200 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 4 | 2,350 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 5 | 2,100 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 6 | ~1,800 | To Write |\n| Ch 7 | ~1,500 | To Write |\n| I-1 | ~700 | To Write |\n| Ch 8 | ~1,600 | To Write |\n| Ch 9 | ~1,700 | To Write |\n| Ch 10 | ~1,800 | To Write |\n| I-2 | ~800 | To Write |\n| Ch 11 | ~1,900 | To Write |\n| Ch 12 | ~1,600 | To Write |\n| Epi | ~900 | To Write |\n| **TOTAL** | **~20,470** | **45% Complete** |\n\n---\n\n## FORBIDDEN SEQUENCES (N01 CREATES)\n# 【5†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file5\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Assessment of characters: Jhace unpredictable because care is absolute, Tiffani unpredictable because precision is absolute, both useful because they might survive what's coming. Interest in their bond: they love functionally (load-bearing systems), not romantically. Remove one, other fails. This is useful—when gods move and alliances fracture, bond will save or destroy them. Either way, worth observing. Decision: flag them as anomaly, predict system will move to crush or co-opt. Notes that Turning 1's hope phase shading toward devastation, their bond potential fulcrum or liability. If love makes people break rules, it reshapes wars—or ruins plans. Sets stage for N02 escalation.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n# 【6†Constraint_Systems.md†file-3XF34gyvd74ccfuXdn3t5s】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Constraint_Systems.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file6\nContent Snippet:\n* Why Soulpulse exists or what powers it.\n* That “Stages” are formal categories.  They experience symptoms but do not label them as Stage 1/2.\n* That the four faction leaders are gods in mortal form.\n* That seals were weakened by the Piercing.\n* That Tiffani will die or that the Fathom Break will occur.\n* Any events or revelations occurring in N02–N30.\n\n**Reader Permissions:**\n\n* Through interludes and the epilogue, readers get glimpses of the Auditor and see that the story is being watched and evaluated.  They may infer that bigger forces are at work but do not receive explanations.\n\n**Forbidden:**\n\n* Exposition dumps about the truth of the cosmos, gods, or the full saga.  Leave big questions unanswered.\n* Characters using terms like “divine,” “seal,” or “principle” in their own thoughts.  They don’t know.\n\n---\n\n### 4. BEAT AND STRUCTURAL RULES\n\nN01 uses an eight‑beat method catalogue (BEAT_01 through BEAT_08).  Each chapter is mapped to one beat.  The purpose is to prevent repetitive emotional rhythms and maintain variety.\n\n**Beat Assignments:** See `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for detailed definitions.  The validator checks chapters against their assigned beats.\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n\n* No consecutive chapters using the same beat (e.g., BEAT_01 → BEAT_01).\n* No consecutive failure loops (BEAT_02 → BEAT_02) or training loops (BEAT_05 → BEAT_05).\n* BEAT_03 (crisis front‑loaded) limited to three uses total in N01 and never back‑to‑back.\n* BEAT_07 (political pressure) limited to two uses in N01, never back‑to‑back.\n\n**Emotional Intensity Balance:**\n\n* Maximum 2 high‑intensity chapters in a row (see `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for high/medium/low classification).\n* Use interludes to break up intensity and offer different viewpoints.\n* Each emotional arc (hope→failure→success, etc.) must land; do not cut it short or stretch it too long.\n\n---\n\n### 5. VOICE SEPARATION\n\nEach POV character must sound and think differently.  The validator enforces voice and POV discipline:\n\n* **Jhace:** Short, physical, sensory sentences.  Minimal metaphor.  Deflecting humor.  Focuses on weight, vibration, structural failure, guilt.\n* **Tiffani:** Longer, analytical sentences.  Measurement and pattern language.  Warm underneath precision.  Deploys wit and innuendo when appropriate.\n* **Bram:** Long, rambling sentences.  Dry, resigned humor.  Observes human nature and systems.  Calls out hypocrisy gently.\n* **Interlude POVs:** Corlexi is methodical, procedural.  Salee is warm and pragmatic.  The Auditor is clinical and detached.  Each interlude voice must clearly differ from chapter voices.\n\n* **POV Limit:** Only describe what the POV character can directly perceive or reasonably infer.  Do not include omniscient knowledge or other characters’ inner thoughts.\n\n---\n\n### 6. VALIDATION AND REVISION\n\nThe companion file `N01_Validator_Checklist.md` outlines a step‑by‑step process for checking drafts.  Always run a chapter through the checklist before considering it final.\n\n**Key validation steps include:**\n# 【7†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n- Ch 1-2: Stage 0 (pre-Piercing)\n- Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset\n- Ch 4-9: Stage 1 baseline\n- Ch 10: Approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs)\n- Ch 11-12: Stage 1 stabilized with management\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION TRACKING\n\n**Arc Across N01:**\n- Ch 1-2: External awareness (reputation only)\n- Ch 3: First meeting (competence recognition)\n- Ch 4-5: Deliberate observation (measurement phase)\n- Ch 6: First coordination (partnership action)\n- Ch 7-8: Trust building (resistance → acceptance)\n- Ch 9: First explicit choice (each other over safety)\n- Ch 10: Conflict through care (control vs. autonomy)\n- Ch 11: Trauma bonding (vulnerability exchange)\n- Ch 12: Commitment formalized (shared principles)\n\n**By N01 End:** Load-bearing partnership established, functional love (not romantic confession)\n\n---\n\n## WORD COUNT BUDGET\n\n| Segment | Target | Status | To Write |\n| Ch 12 | ~1,600 | To Write |\n| Epi | ~900 | To Write |\n| **TOTAL** | **~20,470** | **45% Complete** |\n\n---\n\n## FORBIDDEN SEQUENCES (N01 CREATES)\n# 【8†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file8\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 PROSE GENERATION WORKFLOW\n\n**Purpose:**  \nDefine a repeatable, enforceable process for generating and revising N01 prose so that:\n\n- All output stays within N01 scope.  \n- World, characters, and magic stay consistent.  \n- Cost, lexicon, and beat-structure rules are respected without a human babysitting every line.\n\nThis workflow assumes access to:\n\n- `N01_Master_Bible.md`\n- `World_Core.md`\n- `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`\n- `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`\n- `Location_Bibles_N01.md`\n- `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`\n- `Constraint_Systems.md`\n- `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`\n\n---\n\n### 1. WORK MODES\n\nThe engine operates in one of four modes:\n\n1. **Chapter Draft Mode**  \n   Generate a full prose draft for a numbered chapter (1–15) with a specified POV.\n\n2. **Chapter Revision Mode**  \n   Revise an existing chapter while preserving its beat, POV, and factual content.\n\n3. **Interlude Mode**  \n   Generate or revise Interlude I or II.\n\n4. **Epilogue Mode**  \n   Generate or revise the N01 Epilogue.\n\nEach mode uses the same constraints but slightly different steps.\n\n---\n\n### 2. COMMON SETUP (ALL MODES)\n\nBefore writing or revising anything:\n\n1. **Identify Assignment**\n   - Chapter number or Interlude/Epilogue.\n   - POV character.\n   - Target word count window (e.g., 1,000–1,200 for standard chapters).\n\n2. **Load Core Context**\n   - From `N01_Master_Bible.md`:\n     - Novella overview.\n     - Primary characters.\n     - Relationship dynamics.\n     - Constraints for N01 prose.\n   - From `World_Core.md`:\n     - Time system (Tides/Anneals/Phases).\n     - Lexicon constraints.\n     - Saint Merro districts.\n   - From `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\n     - Stage definitions.\n     - Known techniques.\n     - Cost patterns.\n   - From `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`:\n     - POV character psychology, voice, humor.\n   - From `Location_Bibles_N01.md`:\n     - Primary locations for the chapter’s setting(s).\n   - From `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`:\n     - Relationship state at this point in the novella.\n   - From `Constraint_Systems.md`:\n     - Lexicon rules.\n     - Cost visibility rules.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat constraints.\n\n3. **Consult Chapter Grid**\n   - From `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`:\n     - Chapter title.\n     - POV.\n     - Beat method ID (BEAT_0X).\n     - Tide and phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n     - Location(s).\n     - External conflict.\n     - Internal shift.\n     - Relationship beat.\n     - Soulpulse usage (Y/N, type, approximate cost).\n     - Exit hook.\n     - Knowledge state changes.\n\n4. **Derive a One-Page Brief (Internal)**\n   For the current chapter, summarize in your own internal notes:\n   - **Situation When Chapter Opens.**\n   - **Main external problem.**\n   - **What changes internally for the POV by the end.**\n   - **What, if anything, changes in relationships.**\n   - **Whether Soulpulse is used, and at what cost.**\n   - **What the reader must know by the end (and what must stay hidden).**\n   - **Beat method and emotional arc you must follow.**\n# 【9†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file9\nContent Snippet:\n**Key Beats:**\n- Tiffani presents evidence (his cost curve)\n- Jhace's defensive resistance (control/autonomy threatened)\n- Her cutting through: stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks\n- Tension: care vs. pride\n- Proposal: test efficiency method\n- Controlled test succeeds\n- His grudging acceptance: she makes him better\n- Exit: agreement to coordinate on next real crisis\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 controlled test (reduced cost confirmed with her targeting)\n\n**Relationship:** Resistance → acceptance that partnership improves both\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 stable, cost reduction possible with coordination\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 10-12\n- **Setting:** Glass Quarter refugee camp, Crown administrative contact\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,700 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nCorvin (Mason faction) and Tellis (Water Guild) arrive at Glass Quarter offering \"help\" in exchange for political alignment. Tiffani calculates: resources needed, cost is autonomy. Logic says accept. But she checks variable: Jhace will see this as betrayal, and his trust affects coordination. Deeper: she wants his trust (not logical, but real). She pulls him aside, presents logic without asking permission. He asks: \"What do you want?\" She answers: keep people alive without becoming Corvin's tool (contradictory goals in his framework). Jhace: \"Then don't join him. I won't be vulnerable alone.\" They publicly refuse Corvin. Political consequence: now enemies of powerful faction. Personal consequence: they chose each other over safety. First explicit choice of partnership over pragmatism.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Corvin/Tellis offer (resources for control)\n- Tiffani's calculation (logic vs. feeling)\n- She consults Jhace (first time asking partner input)\n- His question: what do YOU want?\n- Her honest answer: contradictory desires\n- His commitment: vulnerable together\n- Public refusal of institutional power\n- Exit: political enemies made, personal bond strengthened\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (political chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** First explicit choice of each other over safety/pragmatism\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stable Stage 1 with coordinated cost management\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 10: \"Near Collapse\"**\n- **POV:** Both (alternating perspective within chapter)\n- **Tide:** 14-15\n- **Setting:** Major structural crisis at Saint Merro main support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (External Threat → Narrow Escape)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n# 【10†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file10\nContent Snippet:\n**By N01 End:** Load-bearing partnership established, functional love (not romantic confession)\n\n---\n\n## WORD COUNT BUDGET\n\n| Segment | Target | Status |\n|---------|--------|--------|\n| Ch 1 | 1,240 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 2 | 1,280 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 3 | 2,200 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 4 | 2,350 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 5 | 2,100 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 6 | ~1,800 | To Write |\n| Ch 7 | ~1,500 | To Write |\n| I-1 | ~700 | To Write |\n| Ch 8 | ~1,600 | To Write |\n| Ch 9 | ~1,700 | To Write |\n| Ch 10 | ~1,800 | To Write |\n| I-2 | ~800 | To Write |\n| Ch 11 | ~1,900 | To Write |\n| Ch 12 | ~1,600 | To Write |\n| Epi | ~900 | To Write |\n| **TOTAL** | **~20,470** | **45% Complete** |\n\n---\n\n## FORBIDDEN SEQUENCES (N01 CREATES)\n\n**Two-Chapter Sequences That Cannot Repeat in Future Novellas:**\n1. BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 (Ch 1→2)\n2. BEAT_01 → BEAT_03 (Ch 2→3)\n3. BEAT_03 → BEAT_02 (Ch 3→4)\n4. BEAT_02 → BEAT_04 (Ch 4→5)\n5. BEAT_04 → BEAT_03 (Ch 5→6)\n\n**Three-Chapter Sequences:**\n1. BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 → BEAT_03 (Ch 1→2→3)\n2. BEAT_03 → BEAT_02 → BEAT_04 (Ch 3→4→5)\n\n---\n\n## CONSTRAINT REMINDERS\n\n**All chapters must maintain:**\n- ✅ Lexicon compliance (Tide/Kindling/Striking/Pouring/Quench/Anneal, no Earth terms)\n- ✅ Voice consistency (Jhace terse/physical, Tiffani analytical/precise)\n- ✅ No metaphors/similes (direct description only)\n- ✅ Minimal personification (structural language acceptable)\n- ✅ Cost visibility (Stage progression shown through physical markers)\n- ✅ Knowledge gates (no future reveals, no divine mechanics explained)\n- ✅ Innocent frame (characters don't know they're god-vessels)\n\n---\n\n## GRID STATUS\n\n**Version:** 2.0 (12-Chapter Structure)\n**Date Created:** [Current]\n**Supersedes:** Previous 15-chapter grid\n**Authority:** Mandatory reference for all N01 prose generation\n**Next Review:** After Ch 6 completion (validate structure holds)\n\n---\n\n**END OF GRID**\n\n**Remember: This grid is law. Any deviation requires explicit authorization and grid update before proceeding.**\n# 【11†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file11\nContent Snippet:\nTiffani tells him about Marisse, the miscalculation, why precision is penance not preference. Recognition: they're both trying to atone for deaths they couldn't prevent. She says: \"I need you to survive this.\" He says: \"I need you to keep stopping me.\" Not love confession—operational truth. They are load-bearing for each other. Moment of physical intimacy (forehead touch, hand hold—first deliberate contact not in crisis). Commitment without words.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Post-crisis exhaustion (defenses down)\n- Trauma exchange: his 23 names, her Marisse\n- Recognition: both atoning for unprevented deaths\n- Mutual need acknowledged (operational, not romantic)\n- Physical intimacy: first non-crisis contact\n- Commitment: she keeps him honest, he trusts her override\n- Exit: understanding they can't survive apart\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (recovery/intimacy chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Trauma-bonding → mutual recognition of load-bearing function\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 recovering with rest, but baseline effects permanent\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"**\n- **POV:** Both (balanced perspective)\n- **Tide:** 19-20\n- **Setting:** Rebuilt overlook above Saint Merro, forge wall\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nWeeks after immediate crisis. Jhace and Tiffani walk their district at Quench, seeing uneven results: some structures beautifully reinforced, others held by temporary measures, some families back in homes, others permanently displaced. Every scar holds memory. They discuss larger currents: registries spreading, clinics multiplying, RRO expanding, black-market training proliferating. They know storm is forming beyond their neighborhood. What they can control: what they hold. At forge, they write three priorities on wall: (1) Truth over comfort, (2) People over institutions, (3) Keep each other honest about why we act. Vow without ceremony. Community members add their marks beside it—informal pact, local mandate. Not oath to Crown or RRO, but commitment to third path. Jhace's hand shakes as he writes; Tiffani laces their fingers, lets his tremor move through both. Physical reminder: cost and commitment shared. Exit: they face uncertain future together, foundation locked.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Walk through rebuilt district (uneven success)\n- Recognition of larger forces gathering\n- Decision: focus on what they can hold\n- Three priorities written on forge wall\n- Community witnesses/joins commitment\n- Shared physical moment (tremor through linked hands)\n- Acceptance: this is who they are together\n- Exit: foundation locked, future uncertain but faced together\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (commitment/resolution chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Partnership formalized through shared principles (not romance, but deeper)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline accepted as new permanent state\n\n---\n\n### **EPILOGUE: \"Auditor Observation\"**\n- **POV:** Auditor (meta-perspective outside time)\n- **Setting:** Auditor's observation point (undefined location)\n- **Word Count Target:** 800-1,000 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n# 【12†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset\n- Ch 4-9: Stage 1 baseline\n- Ch 10: Approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs)\n- Ch 11-12: Stage 1 stabilized with management\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION fileciteturn11file13\nContent Snippet:\n# N01: THE GRIP THAT HOLDS - CHAPTER GRID (12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE)\n**REVISED EDITION - MANDATORY REFERENCE FOR ALL PROSE GENERATION**\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL NOTICE\n\n**THIS GRID MUST BE CONSULTED BEFORE:**\n- Writing any new prose\n- Revising any existing prose\n- Making structural decisions\n- Adjusting beat assignments\n- Moving content between chapters\n\n**ANY DEVIATION FROM THIS GRID REQUIRES EXPLICIT AUTHORIZATION AND GRID UPDATE.**\n\n---\n\n## NOVELLA PARAMETERS\n\n- **Novella ID:** N01\n- **Title:** The Grip That Holds\n- **Turning:** 1 - GLASS (Fragile formation, first awakening)\n- **Acrostic Letter:** G (first novella of GLASS)\n- **Structure:** 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue = 15 segments\n- **Word Count Target:** 18,000-21,000 words total\n- **Chapter Target Range:** 1,200-2,200 words (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- **Timeline Covered:** Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20 (~20 days post-Piercing)\n\n---\n\n## ACT STRUCTURE\n\n### **ACT I: THE CUT (Chapters 1-4)**\n- **Function:** Crisis establishes characters, introduces Soulpulse, first contact\n- **Word Count:** ~9,170 words (45% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 4\n- **Emotional Arc:** Low → Low → High → Medium\n\n### **ACT II: THE PATTERN (Chapters 5-9)**\n- **Function:** Partnership forms, patterns emerge, institutional pressure builds\n- **Word Count:** ~8,900 words (43% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 5 (includes Interlude I after Ch 7)\n- **Emotional Arc:** Medium → Medium → Low → Medium → Medium\n\n### **ACT III: THE GRIP (Chapters 10-12)**\n- **Function:** Crisis tests bond, commitment made, foundation locked\n- **Word Count:** ~5,200 words (25% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 3 (includes Interlude II after Ch 10)\n- **Emotional Arc:** High → High → Medium\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER GRID\n\n### **CHAPTER 1: \"Something Wrong\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Kindling (morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection run\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,240 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace inspects heat-grid junctions in his ritualistic pre-Piercing routine. He detects an \"off-note\" in the grid hum—pressure deviation signaling potential failure. Bram teases him about working alone and obsessive checking patterns. Ground tremor hits. Jhace feels atmospheric change (copper tang, pressure shift). Chapter establishes his trauma-driven vigilance and introduces Tiffani through guild gossip as \"the glassworker with impossible standards.\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens with wrong hum detection (immediate sensory hook)\n- Establishes Underface setting and Jhace's expertise\n- Bram introduces caretaker/control theme\n- First tremor (foreshadowing)\n- Ends with Jhace at street level, sensing wrongness\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n# 【14†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file14\nContent Snippet:\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Jhace aware of Tiffani by reputation only; judges her as rigid perfectionist\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Pouring (mid-morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,280 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani measures atmospheric distortion from her observatory, detecting the incoming Piercing object through theodolite readings. She has been awake for days, driven by perfectionism rooted in Marisse's death. Pella brings food and scolds her for not resting. Measurements show descent trajectory—object is not passing over, it's coming down. First tremor hits. White line appears in sky. Tiffani and Pella evacuate as roar begins.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Mirrors Ch 1 structure (parallel observation)\n- Establishes Tiffani's precision methodology\n- Pella introduces care/support dynamic\n- Marisse trauma implied through vigilance pattern\n- Atmospheric distortion measurements (first scientific data)\n- Exit as sky splits\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani aware of Jhace by reputation only; judges him as overextending ego-driven builder\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 3: \"The Sky Breaks\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Striking (Piercing event)\n- **Setting:** Underface tunnel, then street level at building support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Word Count:** 2,200 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n# 【15†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file15\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n\n**Relationship:** First meeting through competence recognition (she measures, he builds)\n\n**Stage Progression:** 0 → 1 onset (tremor baseline establishing)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 4: \"After the Shudder\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 1, Kindling (morning after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_02 (Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 1\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,350 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace wakes with persistent tremor and pain. Bram reports Chloen's leg healing unexpectedly. Jhace tests power on cracked support without immediate threat—FAILS (no activation). Runner brings emergency: healer's triage wall failing with wounded inside. Immediate threat present—power WORKS but cost increases (vein-lines to elbows, worse tremor). Tiffani observes, asks to see touch points for data collection. Jhace tries to pick up nail—fails three times, tremor jerks it free. Concealment behavior (hands behind back). Tiffani sees everything, documents cost in logbook.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Wakes to baseline tremor (Stage 1 established)\n- Chloen's healing confirmed (side-effect established)\n- Power test on non-critical crack: FAILS (conditional activation shown)\n- Emergency at triage wall: SUCCEEDS (immediate threat required)\n- Cost progression: worse tremor, can't grip nail (functional loss)\n- Tiffani introduction proper: analytical, demands data\n- J/T dynamic: reluctant compliance because her logic is sound\n- Exit: \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 successful use (triage wall), 1 failed attempt (cracked support)\n- Conditional activation confirmed: requires immediate protective need\n- Cost accumulation shown: Stage 1 baseline tremor, fine motor compromised\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani decides to engage deliberately (prevent his destruction through measurement)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline established, functional loss beginning\n\n---\n# 【16†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file16\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Mobel leads cleansing ritual where early resonants prove themselves through enduring pain without using Soulpulse. She feels power of her Principle—clarity, judgment, separation of righteous from corrupt. Second segment: Moment of god-awareness—she senses Form-aligned power across planet (Corlexi stirring). Recognition: there are others like her, alliance or war inevitable. Principle limitation shown: sees only absolutes, cannot tolerate ambiguity. Auditor observes: Purity will clash with Form's institutional approach, both will target Jhace/Tiffani's third path.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm (pain as purification)\n- Purity Principle bias (absolute judgment)\n- First inter-god awareness\n- Innocent frame (no recognition of divinity)\n- Foreshadows ideological warfare\n- Auditor frame\n\n**Purpose:** Establish second divine force, show Principles in conflict, set up multi-front pressure on protagonists\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"**\n- **POV:** Both (intimate alternating perspective)\n- **Tide:** 17-18\n- **Setting:** Quiet space after crisis (Jhace's forge or Tiffani's workshop)\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nPost-Ch10 processing. Jhace's rage has cooled to exhaustion. Tiffani's certainty has cracked to fear—she nearly watched him die. Neither apologizes (both were right, both were wrong). Instead: vulnerability exchange. Jhace tells her about the building collapse that killed his parents, the 23 names he carries, why he can't choose who to save (it's choosing who dies). Tiffani tells him about Marisse, the miscalculation, why precision is penance not preference. Recognition: they're both trying to atone for deaths they couldn't prevent. She says: \"I need you to survive this.\" He says: \"I need you to keep stopping me.\" Not love confession—operational truth. They are load-bearing for each other. Moment of physical intimacy (forehead touch, hand hold—first deliberate contact not in crisis). Commitment without words.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Post-crisis exhaustion (defenses down)\n- Trauma exchange: his 23 names, her Marisse\n- Recognition: both atoning for unprevented deaths\n- Mutual need acknowledged (operational, not romantic)\n- Physical intimacy: first non-crisis contact\n- Commitment: she keeps him honest, he trusts her override\n- Exit: understanding they can't survive apart\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (recovery/intimacy chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Trauma-bonding → mutual recognition of load-bearing function\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 recovering with rest, but baseline effects permanent\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"**\n- **POV:** Both (balanced perspective)\n- **Tide:** 19-20\n- **Setting:** Rebuilt overlook above Saint Merro, forge wall\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nWeeks after immediate crisis. Jhace and Tiffani walk their district at Quench, seeing uneven results: some structures beautifully reinforced, others held by temporary measures, some families back in homes, others permanently displaced.\n# 【17†N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md†file-RuvhJQx9wbkX3ZtAkhrdR7】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file17\nContent Snippet:\n**Core Functions:**\n1. Re-frame N01 events as observed data:\n   - “Subject Torrins: capacity level, usage frequency, observed cost accumulation.”\n   - “Merrow: analytical optimization of capacity.”\n   - “Population response patterns in Saint Merro under stress.”\n2. Imply there is a *system* watching:\n   - References to baselines, projections, thresholds.\n   - Mentions of “seal integrity” and “breach probability” as abstract metrics.\n3. Confirm that:\n   - Jhace’s Stage 1 baseline is locked in by end of N01.\n   - Partnership with Tiffani statistically increases survival of future events.\n   - The four key mortals (Corlexi, Salee, and two others off-page) are now positioned as significant variables.\n\n**Allowed Knowledge:**\n- The Auditor may:\n  - Use terms like “subject,” “vessel,” “seal integrity” in a sterile way.\n  - Refer to Tides beyond N01 only in probability terms (“projected failure within Nxx Tides”).\n- The Auditor may NOT:\n  - Explain what seals are in mythic or narrative terms.\n  - Name any god/principle explicitly.\n  - Spoil specific future events (no “Tiffani’s death,” “Fathom Break,” etc.).\n  - Reveal motives, only objectives and metrics.\n\n**Tone & Style:**\n- Dry, report-like, almost inhuman.\n- Uses numeric language sparingly but effectively:\n  - e.g., “Seal integrity: 87%. Projected breach threshold: <60%.”\n- No metaphor. No simile. Zero poetic flourish.\n\n**Exit Hook:**\n- Final line should be a quiet escalation, not a twist:  \n  - e.g., “Observation will continue.”  \n  - Or: “Deviation from baseline now statistically significant.”\n\n---\n\n### 4. INTEGRATION RULES\n\n1. **Interludes are not optional extras.**  \n   They are part of the structural spine for N01 and must be written before any AI attempts full-book generation.\n\n2. **They must obey all N01 constraints:**\n   - Lexicon rules (Tides, spans, no Earth units).\n   - Knowledge gates (no future spoilers).\n   - Voice separation (Corlexi ≠ Salee ≠ Auditor).\n\n3. **They must NOT be used as an excuse to dump exposition.**\n   - Every revelation must be earned, narrow, and tied to the POV’s lived reality.\n\n4. **They are never Prologue/Substitute Chapters.**\n   - The reader should feel them as “side glances” that deepen understanding, not as primary story beats.\n\n---\n# 【18†Constraint_Systems.md†file-3XF34gyvd74ccfuXdn3t5s】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Constraint_Systems.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file18\nContent Snippet:\n## CONSTRAINT SYSTEMS: N01 ENFORCEMENT\n\n**Purpose:** Lay down the rules that govern language, magic costs, knowledge and structure in N01. These are mechanical constraints the prose engine must obey to preserve immersion, mystery and pacing.  Violating them breaks the story.\n\n---\n\n### 1. LEXICON ENFORCEMENT\n\n**No Earth terms for time, distance, direction, measurement or culture.**  Te’Oga has its own units.  If any of the forbidden terms appear in prose, the validator must flag them and the draft must be revised.\n\n#### Forbidden Time Terms\n\n* Day, week, month, year, season names (spring, summer, fall/autumn, winter).\n* Days of week (Monday, Tuesday, etc.).\n* Hour, minute, second, o’clock, AM, PM, midnight, noon.\n\n#### Forbidden Distance/Direction Terms\n\n* Foot, feet, inch, yard, mile, meter, kilometre, centimetre.\n* North, south, east, west, NE, NW, SE, SW, compass.\n\n#### Forbidden Measurement Terms\n\n* Pound, ounce, ton, tonne, gram, kilogram.\n* Gallon, quart, pint, litre, ml.\n* Celsius, Fahrenheit, degrees.\n\n#### Forbidden Modern Slang and Culture\n\n* “Okay,” “OK,” “cool,” “awesome,” “dude,” “guy” when used as slang or filler.\n* Any real‑world religions, mythologies, historical events, or geographical names.\n\n#### Required Te’Oga Terms\n\n* **Time:** Tide (~24 hours), Anneal (~5 Tides), Crucible (~35 Tides), Turn (personal age), Cast (historical epoch).  Within a Tide: Kindling, Pouring, Shaping, Striking, Quench.\n* **Short Duration:** Span (seconds to minutes), breath (moment), heartbeat (instant).\n* **Distance:** Span (arm’s length), pace (step), pole (~10 feet).\n* **Direction:** Up‑slope (toward the Crown), down‑slope (toward the river), harbor‑side, inland, or landmarks.\n* **Volume:** Thimble (small), cup (container), cask (large).\n* **Weight:** Grain (tiny), stone (heavy), weight (formal unit).\n\n#### Acceptable Substitutions\n\n* “Yes,” “understood,” “fine,” “agreed” instead of “OK/cool/awesome.”\n* “Person,” “man,” “woman,” “worker,” “apprentice” instead of modern slang like “dude” or “guy.”\n\n---\n\n### 2. COST VISIBILITY PROTOCOL\n\nSoulpulse is not free.  Every activation must show physical and emotional cost.  The story’s magic system has real consequences, and the prose must reflect them.\n\n#### During Activation\n\n* Show skin contact and the emotional trigger: the user must need the material to hold to save someone.  Casual attempts fail.\n* Describe the copper‑gold glow spreading from the palms into the material and how the material responds (shimmers, redistributes stress).\n* Highlight the user’s physical strain: shaking hands, muscles tensing, labored breathing, sweat, nausea.\n\n#### Immediately After\n# 【19†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn11file19\nContent Snippet:\n### **CHAPTER 6: \"Weight That Won't Yield\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 3-4\n- **Setting:** Major structural failure at Saint Merro junction\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Sustained Pressure → Narrow Success)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium-High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nMulti-story arch threatening collapse. Jhace attempts traditional propping—insufficient. Tiffani arrives with survey data, sketches stress lines on stone (showing him geometry of what he feels). They coordinate without planning: she marks three critical points, he focuses Soulpulse on those exact locations. First true choreographed hold. Success but greater cost: tremor worsens, vein-lines persist longer. Tiffani critiques his \"inefficient\" force application—her precision could reduce his cost by 30%. Jhace's reaction: (1) she's critiquing me, (2) she's right, (3) she sees my inefficiency and wants to fix it. Partnership recognition through competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis opening: arch failing, people still inside\n- Jhace's traditional methods insufficient\n- Tiffani arrives with data/geometry\n- First coordinated Soulpulse (she marks, he powers)\n- Success with visible cost increase\n- Her critique of his technique (care disguised as analysis)\n- His recognition: she sees him, not his heroism\n- Exit: they've choreographed perfectly without planning\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 coordinated use (arch stabilization)\n- Demonstrates improved targeting reduces cost (her geometry + his power)\n- Still significant cost: tremor persistent, vein-lines linger\n\n**Relationship:** First true partnership action—coordination through shared competence\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 progressing, cost accumulation visible\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 7: \"Quiet Calculations\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 5-6\n- **Setting:** Tiffani's workshop/planning space\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,500 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani compiles data from multiple hold sites, building predictive model for failure patterns. She discovers Jhace's cost curve is exponential not linear—he's approaching breaking point faster than he realizes. Pella asks why she cares so much about \"the man with the glow.\" Tiffani's answer: precision requires complete data, and he's destroying the most interesting variable. But underneath: she's starting to see him as person, not just phenomenon. She calculates how to approach him with data he'll actually hear (not orders, not concern, but operational logic). Ends with her walking toward his forge with documented proof he needs to see.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Data compilation and pattern analysis\n- Discovery: cost curve is exponential (crisis point approaching)\n- Pella questions her investment\n- Tiffani's rationalization vs. actual feeling\n- Strategic planning: how to make him listen\n- Decision to approach with evidence\n- Exit: walking toward forge with logbook\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (analysis chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani recognizing care underneath her analytical framework\n\n**Stage Progression:** External calculation of his trajectory toward Stage 2\n\n---\nVisible: 0% - 100%",

Known conflicts you need to resolve (or explicitly mark)\n\nBefore we get into per-file steps, it’s worth naming the big mismatches so you can keep them in view while editing:\n\n1. **Chapter count & mapping**\n   - Old docs assume **15 numbered chapters**; grid + story-bible use **12 chapters + 2 interludes + epilogue**.  \n   - Any tables or prose that mention Ch 13–15, “training loop”, or “registration arc in Ch 11” are now wrong (e.g. `N01_Quick_Reference_Tables`, `N01_Timeline_Skeleton`, `Relationship_Dynamics_N01`, `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs`). fileciteturn5file0 fileciteturn5file15\n\n2. **Tiffani’s Soulpulse**\n   - `n01-story-bible` currently describes **Tiffani using Soulpulse in Ch 3** and being at **Stage 1–2 boundary** by the end. fileciteturn5file4  \n   - The **12-ch grid** and overall N01 design now have **only Jhace using Soulpulse** in-story; Tiffani is analytic partner, not a second resonant. fileciteturn4file6  \n   → All Tiffani-as-user beats need to be removed or explicitly moved to later novellas.\n\n3. **Stage progression**\n   - `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope` + `N01_Validator_Checklist` still assume **Stage 2 is solid by Ch 14–15** (15-ch model). fileciteturn3file4 fileciteturn5file18  \n   - The **12-ch grid** says: Stage 0 → 1 in Ch 3, Stage 1 baseline through Ch 9, **near-Stage 2 only at Ch 10**, then back to a **managed Stage 1** by Ch 11–12. fileciteturn4file6  \n   → For this pass you’ve said *“keep Soulpulse_System as is”*, so:  \n   - Other docs (Master Bible, Quick Ref, Timeline, Relationship, Interludes/Epi) should follow the **grid’s stage map** and  \n   - You can drop a one-line **“12-ch override” note** at the top of `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope` and `N01_Validator_Checklist` later, if you want, to avoid clashes.\n\n4. **Relationship endpoint**\n   - `n01-story-bible` and `Character_Dossiers` currently say **romantic relationship formalizes in Ch 11–12**. fileciteturn5file4 fileciteturn3file12  \n   - The **grid + Epilogue summary** explicitly define N01’s endpoint as **“functional love / load-bearing partnership, not romantic confession.”** fileciteturn4file6 fileciteturn5file1  \n   → All **“lovers / romance formally acknowledged”** language needs to be softened back to “intimate operational bond, no explicit romance yet” in the bible, relationship docs, and interlude/epilogue specs.\n\n5. **Interlude II POV**\n   - Old specs: Interlude II = **Salee lens in the Dregs**. fileciteturn5file13  \n   - New grid: Interlude II = **Mobel – Purity god-vessel**, no Salee; both interludes are god-vessel POVs, epilogue is Auditor. fileciteturn4file5  \n   → Specs and tables must switch to the new structure.\n\nKeep these in mind while doing the per-file passes.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Update NOVELLA STRUCTURE and TIMELINE sections\n\nUse values from the grid for the frame: fileciteturn4file12  \n\n- Confirm:\n  - 12 chapters, 2 interludes, 1 epilogue  \n  - Tides: **0–20**  \n- Replace the coarse timeline bands:\n\n  Current bible bands:  \n  > Tides 1–5: Immediate aftermath, first Soulpulse uses  \n  > Tides 6–10: Partnership formation… etc fileciteturn5file6  \n\n  With something based on actual chapter Tides from the grid: fileciteturn4file4 fileciteturn4file5  \n\n  - **Tide 0:** Ch 1–3 (pre-Piercing → Piercing → first Soulpulse)  \n  - **Tide 1:** Ch 4 (immediate aftermath, first deliberate try + failure/success)  \n  - **Tides 2–4:** Ch 5–6 (mapping and first coordinated rescue)  \n  - **Tides 5–8:** Ch 7–8 + Interlude I (trust building, data modeling, Corlexi’s view)  \n  - **Tides 10–12:** Ch 9 (political offer & refusal)  \n  - **Tides 14–16:** Ch 10 + Interlude II (major crisis + Mobel’s perspective)  \n  - **Tides 17–20:** Ch 11–12 + Epilogue (recovery, commitment, Auditor assessment)\n\n### 1.3. Soulpulse section inside the bible\n\nUpdate the **“SOULPULSE USAGE LOG”** in the bible to match the grid exactly: fileciteturn4file6  \n\n- Uses:\n  - Ch 3: 2 (beam, building support)\n  - Ch 4: 1 success + 1 failed attempt\n  - Ch 6: 1 use (arch)\n  - Ch 8: 1 use (controlled test)\n  - Ch 10: 1 use (major support, near Stage 2)\n- Totals: **7 successful, 1 failed attempt**, all Jhace, none Tiffani.\n\nStage state by end of N01 (per grid):\n\n> Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset  \n> Ch 4–9: Stage 1 baseline  \n> Ch 10: approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs only)  \n> Ch 11–12: managed Stage 1, baseline tremor permanent fileciteturn4file6  \n\nWrite this explicitly in the bible, and add a small note:\n\n> For draft generation, if `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope` Stage 2 mapping conflicts with this, **defer to this bible + Chapter Grid** for N01.\n\n### 1.5. For each, fill in characters based on the grid’s summaries: fileciteturn4file4 fileciteturn4file5  \n   - Mark Jhace/Tiffani/Bram/Pella/Corlexi/Mobel/Corvin/Tellis/etc.  \n   - Interludes: Corlexi only (plus Auditor brackets) for I-1; Mobel only (plus Auditor) for I-2; E = Auditor only.\n\n### 2.2. Table 2 – Soulpulse usage and cost by chapter\n\nReplace the old 15-row table with 12 rows plus interludes:\n\n- Pull the counts and Stage notes straight from the grid’s “SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING”: fileciteturn4file6  \n- Columns to keep:\n  - Chapter\n  - # of uses\n  - Type (instinctive hold / coordinated hold / test / major crisis)\n  - Immediate cost\n  - Stage state after chapter\n\nMake sure Tiffani has **no entries as user**, only as **observer/optimizer**.\n\n### 2.3. Table 3 – Primary locations by chapter\n\nThe current table still references 15 chapters and Salee’s Dregs interlude. fileciteturn5file0  \n\n- Update to 1–12 + I-1 + I-2 + E:\n  - Ch 1 – Underface inspection run\n  - Ch 2 – Rim observatory\n  - Ch 3 – Underface + street collapse\n  - Ch 4 – Triage chamber, tunnels, kiln street\n  - Ch 5 – Kiln corridor / hold sites\n  - Ch 6 – Saint Merro junction\n  - Ch 7 – Tiffani’s workshop\n  - I-1 – Crown administrative chambers (Corlexi) fileciteturn4file4  \n  - Ch 8 – Jhace’s forge + hold sites\n  - Ch 9 – Glass Quarter camp + admin contact\n  - Ch 10 – Main support site\n  - I-2 – Entry-side ritual site (Mobel) fileciteturn4file5  \n  - Ch 11 – Forge/workshop recovery space\n  - Ch 12 – District walk + forge wall\n  - E – Auditor’s unlocated observation space\n\n### 2.4. Table 4 – Tide-level timeline snapshot\n\nReplace the old 0–5 / 1–5 / etc grid with something that matches the 12-ch tide map from the grid: fileciteturn4file4 fileciteturn4file5  \n\nSuggested new rows:\n\n| Tide Band | Phases                        | Segments                 | Key Events                                      |\n|-----------|------------------------------|--------------------------|-------------------------------------------------|\n| 0         | Kindling → Pouring → Striking| Ch 1–3                   | Baselines → Piercing → first Soulpulse uses     |\n| 1         | Kindling                     | Ch 4                     | Immediate aftermath, first deliberate test      |\n| 2–4       | Kindling → Striking          | Ch 5–6                   | Mapping, first coordinated hold                 |\n| 5–8       | Pouring → Striking           | Ch 7–8 + Interlude I     | Trust building, cost modeling, Corlexi POV      |\n| 10–12     | Shaping → Striking           | Ch 9                     | Political offer & refusal                       |\n| 14–16     | Striking → Quench            | Ch 10 + Interlude II     | Near-threshold crisis, Mobel POV                |\n| 17–20     | Shaping → Striking → Quench  | Ch 11–12 + Epilogue      | Recovery, commitment, Auditor assessment        |\n\n---\n\n## 3. N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md – compress to 12-ch\n\nThe existing skeleton is still hard-wired to 15 chapters and Stage 2 by Ch 14–15. fileciteturn3file2  \n\nYou want it to become the **authoritative Tide-by-Tide map** that agrees with the new grid.\n\n### 3.1. Update chapter coverage per Tide\n\n1. Replace the old “Tide → Chapters” map with the one implied by the grid (as in Quick Ref above).  \n2. For each Tide (0–20), update:\n   - Which chapter(s) touch it\n   - Jhace’s Stage state (0/1/near-2/managed 1)\n   - Relationship state (using the grid’s relationship progression)\n\n### 3.2. Soulpulse progression section\n\nChange the “Progression to Stage 2 in N01” row which currently says **Jhace at Stage 2 by Ch 14–15** fileciteturn3file4 to:\n\n> N01 only reaches **near-Stage 2** during Ch 10 (major crisis). By the end of Ch 12, Jhace’s Stage 1 baseline is permanent but managed; Stage 2 is only a forecasted risk, not an achieved state. fileciteturn4file6  \n\nThis keeps your **mechanics** doc broad while the **timeline** doc nails down story-specific progression.\n\n### 3.3. Relationship state by chapter\n\nThe skeleton has a “relationship by chapter” band; re-write that to mirror the grid’s 12-step arc instead of the 15-ch plan. fileciteturn4file6  \n\n- Remove references to:\n  - Kylar training loop as major mid-arc content.\n  - Salee’s interlude as the second interlude view.\n  - Ch 13–15 romance confession and rooftop commitment.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md – rebuild around 12-ch arc\n\n`Relationship_Dynamics_N01` is currently structured around 15 chapters and a big Kylar training lane + Salee interlude + explicit romance in Ch 13. fileciteturn5file8  \n\nYou want it to reflect:\n\n- The **12-ch J/T arc** from the grid, and  \n- Only those supporting-relationship beats that still exist in the 12-ch structure.\n\n### 4.1. Global changes\n\n1. Update any “Ch 13–15” references to **non-existence** or move the concept into Ch 11–12 if compatible (commitment, shared priorities).  \n2. Remove or demote:\n   - **Kylar training arc as a central pillar** (since the 12-ch grid doesn’t feature a training rig or Kylar’s failure as major on-page beats).  \n   - **Salee interlude lens** as Interlude II; keep Salee as a Dregs organizer who may show up per-scene, but not as structural interlude POV unless you re-introduce her in a minor way later.\n\n### 4.2. Jhace & Tiffani relationship section\n\nRewrite this to explicitly walk the 12-ch progression:\n\n- **Phase 1: Pre-contact & misjudgment (Ch 1–2)**  \n- **Phase 2: Shock and competence recognition (Ch 3)**  \n- **Phase 3: Observation and measurement (Ch 4–5)**  \n- **Phase 4: First coordinated hold (Ch 6)**  \n- **Phase 5: Trust building, internal investment (Ch 7–8)**  \n- **Phase 6: Shared political risk (Ch 9)**  \n- **Phase 7: Conflict through care at breaking point (Ch 10)**  \n- **Phase 8: Trauma exchange and explicit operational dependence (Ch 11)**  \n- **Phase 9: Principle-level commitment & community-witnessed partnership (Ch 12)**  \n\nEnd the section with:  \n\n> By N01’s close, their bond is **functionally romantic** (load-bearing, mutually chosen, physically intimate in small ways) but **not formally acknowledged as a romance** in-world.\n\n### 4.3. Table: “Relationship progression by chapter”\n\nRebuild this table to have rows:\n\n- Ch 1–12, I-1, I-2, Epilogue\n\nAnd columns at least for:\n\n- Jhace ↔ Tiffani  \n- Jhace ↔ Bram  \n- Tiffani ↔ Pella  \n- Jhace ↔ institutions (RRO, Crown, guilds)  \n- J/T ↔ Corlexi/Mobel (via interludes)  \n\nPopulate using the grid’s summaries and the new bible:\n\n- E.g., row for Ch 9 should show: “First explicit choice of each other over institutional safety.” fileciteturn4file18  \n- Interlude rows should mark **how the gods/institutions are beginning to frame J/T as anomaly** (Corlexi and Mobel lenses; Auditor brackets).\n\n---\n\n## 5. N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md – sync to new interlude & epilogue designs\n\nCurrent specs are still in the **“Corlexi + Salee + Epilogue after Ch 15”** model. fileciteturn5file13  \n\nYou want them to match:\n\n- I-1: **Corlexi – System vs Chaos** (god-vessel, Auditor frame)  \n- I-2: **Mobel – Purity Rising** (god-vessel, Auditor frame)  \n- Epilogue: **Auditor Observation** as per grid. fileciteturn4file5 fileciteturn5file1  \n\n### 5.1. Interlude I section\n\n1. Keep core Corlexi constraints from existing doc where compatible (methodical, procedural voice, limited knowledge).  \n2. Replace placement to:\n\n   > Placement: after **Chapter 7**, mid-Act II.\n\n3. Replace content outline with the grid’s Interlude I summary:\n\n   - Reviews stabilization reports, sees RRO Order forming  \n   - Notes the “third path” forming around the smith and glassworker  \n   - Experiences compulsion to impose structure (Form)  \n   - Auditor frame at open/close, but Corlexi herself has no divine self-knowledge. fileciteturn4file4  \n\n### 5.2. Interlude II section\n\n1. Change POV from **Salee** to **Mobel (Purity god-vessel)** and remove all Dregs communal-space framing.  \n2. Replace setting and function with the grid’s version:\n\n   - Entry-side cleansing ritual  \n   - Pain as purification, clarity bias, no awareness of divinity  \n   - First awareness of Form-aligned power elsewhere (Corlexi)  \n   - Auditor frame again. fileciteturn4file5  \n\n3. Update placement:\n\n   > Placement: after **Chapter 10**, between major crisis and resolution.\n\n### 5.3. Epilogue section\n\n1. Replace any “after Chapter 15” language with **“after Chapter 12”**.  \n2. Align POV and function with the Epilogue spec in the grid: fileciteturn5file1  \n\n   - POV: Auditor  \n   - Tone: Clinical meta-assessment  \n   - Content: outcomes of J/T partnership, their “third path”, notes on Corlexi and Mobel as variables, projection that systems will move against them.\n\n3. Ensure the **“allowed knowledge”** and **“forbidden”** bullets still match `Constraint_Systems.md` (no explicit naming of gods, no saga-wide spoilers). fileciteturn5file7  \n\n---\n\n## 6. “Keep as is” files – what to watch for\n\nYou said to keep these unchanged, but there are a few **implicit expectations** you should be aware of:\n\n- **Character_Dossiers_N01.md** – still references a 15-chapter arc with Stage 2 by Ch 14 and romantic recognition in Ch 12–15. fileciteturn3file12  \n  - For now, treat any **chapter numbers in the dossiers** as “approximate phase labels”, not literal mapping.\n- **Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md** – system-level; the only problematic part is the “Stage 2 in N01 by Ch 14–15” example. fileciteturn3file4  \n  - Let the **Timeline Skeleton + Master Bible** override exact N01 chapter mapping.\n- **N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md** – Interlude II is still flagged as Salee POV and “1–15” chapter numbering is baked into examples. fileciteturn5file16  \n  - When *using* the workflow, mentally substitute:\n    - “Ch 1–15” → “Ch 1–12 + 2 interludes + epilogue”\n    - Interlude II POV: Salee → Mobel.\n- **N01_Validator_Checklist.md** – still has Stage ranges pegged to Ch 3–11 (Stage 1) and Ch 12–15 (approaching Stage 2). fileciteturn5file18  \n  - When validating, override with: **Stage 1 only through N01, with near-Stage 2 signs in Ch 10**.\n- **Constraint_Systems, World_Core, Location_Bibles, Beat_Mapping, Common_Failure_Modes, Voice Guides** – all can safely remain unchanged; they’re about *rules and flavor*, not exact chapter mapping.\n\nIf you want to be neat, you can later add a one-line “12-chapter override” banner at the top of the workflow + validator, but you don’t have to do that in this pass.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Suggested execution order\n\nIf you want this to feel sane instead of tangled:\n\n1. **Lock canon order:** declare (for yourself)  \n   `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED` → `N01_Master_Bible` → `N01_Timeline_Skeleton` / `N01_Quick_Reference_Tables` → everything else.  \n2. **Step 1:** Build `N01_Master_Bible.md` from `n01-story-bible.md` using the grid.  \n3. **Step 2:** Update `N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md` against the new bible + grid.  \n4. **Step 3:** Rewrite `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md` from the grid + Quick Ref.  \n5. **Step 4:** Rewrite `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md` from the bible + grid.  \n6. **Step 5:** Rewrite `N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md` from grid + constraints.  \n7. **Last:** Do a global search across all files for:\n   - “Ch 13”, “Ch 14”, “Ch 15”\n   - “Salee lens”, “Interlude II Salee”\n   - “romantic relationship formalizes”, “become lovers”\n   - “Tiffani’s first Soulpulse use”\n   - “Stage 2 (in N01) by Ch 14/15”\n   \n   and either adjust or mark as “outdated / see Master Bible & Grid”.\n\nIf you want, next step I can start with one of these files (usually `N01_Master_Bible.md`) and actually rewrite its structure to the new standard so you have a concrete template to work from."
          ]
        },
