          "result": "# 【0†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file0\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nCritical main support failing during high-traffic period. Hundreds at risk. Jhace and Tiffani coordinate under pressure: she calculates load paths in real-time, he applies force to her exact specifications. They move as single unit, no wasted words, pure operational sync. Success—but Jhace pushes past safe limits to ensure no one dies. Cost: approaches Stage 2 threshold (tremor violent, vein-lines permanent at baseline, brief moment of sensory overload). Tiffani physically restrains him from continuing, makes decision FOR him: \"You're done. I don't care if you hate me for it.\" Saves him by overriding his control. Afterward, his rage at her presumption vs. her cold certainty she was right. Tension peaks: control vs. care, autonomy vs. survival.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis: hundreds at risk, main support failing\n- Perfect coordination under pressure\n- Jhace pushes past limits (caretaker compulsion)\n- Tiffani's override: physical intervention\n- Success but near-Stage 2 cost\n- His rage: she took his choice\n- Her certainty: he would have killed himself\n- Relationship stress: care as control\n- Exit: silent fury and mutual recognition they need each other\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 major use (near-Stage 2 threshold)\n- Coordinated efficiency maintains, but scale drives cost\n- Brief sensory overload (Stage 2 warning sign)\n- Vein-lines now faintly visible at rest\n\n**Relationship:** Conflict through care—she saves him by controlling him, he hates needing it\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 → approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs present)\n\n---\n\n### **INTERLUDE II: \"Mobel - Purity Rising\"**\n- **POV:** Mobel (Purity god-vessel, mortal name unknown to self)\n- **Tide:** ~15-16\n- **Setting:** Entry-side (opposite hemisphere from Saint Merro)\n- **Type:** Build-Up\n- **Word Count Target:** 600-900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Mobel leads cleansing ritual where early resonants prove themselves through enduring pain without using Soulpulse. She feels power of her Principle—clarity, judgment, separation of righteous from corrupt. Second segment: Moment of god-awareness—she senses Form-aligned power across planet (Corlexi stirring). Recognition: there are others like her, alliance or war inevitable. Principle limitation shown: sees only absolutes, cannot tolerate ambiguity. Auditor observes: Purity will clash with Form's institutional approach, both will target Jhace/Tiffani's third path.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm (pain as purification)\n- Purity Principle bias (absolute judgment)\n- First inter-god awareness\n- Innocent frame (no recognition of divinity)\n- Foreshadows ideological warfare\n- Auditor frame\n\n**Purpose:** Establish second divine force, show Principles in conflict, set up multi-front pressure on protagonists\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"**\n- **POV:** Both (intimate alternating perspective)\n- **Tide:** 17-18\n- **Setting:** Quiet space after crisis (Jhace's forge or Tiffani's workshop)\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nPost-Ch10 processing. Jhace's rage has cooled to exhaustion. Tiffani's certainty has cracked to fear—she nearly watched him die.\n# 【1†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file1\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Corlexi reviews stabilization reports, sees Order taking shape through RRO formation. She feels satisfaction at frameworks solidifying but also unease—something escapes her control. The smith and glassworker refuse to be contained, building third path outside her geometry. Second segment: She experiences her Principle (Form) as compulsion to impose structure, feels nascent god-awareness stirring (vast power constrained by single lens). She makes note: must address the anomaly in Saint Merro. Closing: Auditor observes Corlexi's awakening, notes Form's limitation—cannot tolerate things outside patterns.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm technique (god-vessel perception)\n- Principle bias manifestation (everything seen through Form lens)\n- Innocent frame maintained (no recognition of divinity)\n- Jhace/Tiffani recognized as structural threat\n- Auditor frame at open/close\n\n**Purpose:** Establish divine forces moving, show limitation of single-Principle perspective, foreshadow institutional pressure in Act III\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 8: \"Proof and Resistance\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 7-8\n- **Setting:** Jhace's forge, then joint inspection of hold sites\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_12 (Relationship Shift → Power Dynamic Change)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani arrives at forge with compiled data, shows Jhace his cost curve projection. He resists—doesn't want to see himself as failing, doesn't trust numbers over experience. She challenges: \"You trust stone to tell truth. These marks are stone's truth about what you're doing to yourself.\" Tension: his defensive walls vs. her refusal to let him self-destruct through ignorance. She proposes operational change: she identifies critical points, he applies minimal force, they test if efficiency reduces cost. He agrees grudgingly—not because he believes her, but because her logic is sound even if he hates it. They test method on controlled scenario. It works. Cost is measurably lower. His recognition: accepting her help isn't weakness, it's competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Tiffani presents evidence (his cost curve)\n- Jhace's defensive resistance (control/autonomy threatened)\n- Her cutting through: stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks\n- Tension: care vs. pride\n- Proposal: test efficiency method\n- Controlled test succeeds\n- His grudging acceptance: she makes him better\n- Exit: agreement to coordinate on next real crisis\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 controlled test (reduced cost confirmed with her targeting)\n\n**Relationship:** Resistance → acceptance that partnership improves both\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 stable, cost reduction possible with coordination\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 10-12\n- **Setting:** Glass Quarter refugee camp, Crown administrative contact\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,700 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nCorvin (Mason faction) and Tellis (Water Guild) arrive at Glass Quarter offering \"help\" in exchange for political alignment. Tiffani calculates: resources needed, cost is autonomy. Logic says accept. But she checks variable: Jhace will see this as betrayal, and his trust affects coordination. Deeper: she wants his trust (not logical, but real).\n# 【2†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file2\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Assessment of characters: Jhace unpredictable because care is absolute, Tiffani unpredictable because precision is absolute, both useful because they might survive what's coming. Interest in their bond: they love functionally (load-bearing systems), not romantically. Remove one, other fails. This is useful—when gods move and alliances fracture, bond will save or destroy them. Either way, worth observing. Decision: flag them as anomaly, predict system will move to crush or co-opt. Notes that Turning 1's hope phase shading toward devastation, their bond potential fulcrum or liability. If love makes people break rules, it reshapes wars—or ruins plans. Sets stage for N02 escalation.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n# 【3†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file3\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Deeper: she wants his trust (not logical, but real).\n# 【5†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file5\nContent Snippet:\n### **CHAPTER 6: \"Weight That Won't Yield\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 3-4\n- **Setting:** Major structural failure at Saint Merro junction\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Sustained Pressure → Narrow Success)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium-High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nMulti-story arch threatening collapse. Jhace attempts traditional propping—insufficient. Tiffani arrives with survey data, sketches stress lines on stone (showing him geometry of what he feels). They coordinate without planning: she marks three critical points, he focuses Soulpulse on those exact locations. First true choreographed hold. Success but greater cost: tremor worsens, vein-lines persist longer. Tiffani critiques his \"inefficient\" force application—her precision could reduce his cost by 30%. Jhace's reaction: (1) she's critiquing me, (2) she's right, (3) she sees my inefficiency and wants to fix it. Partnership recognition through competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis opening: arch failing, people still inside\n- Jhace's traditional methods insufficient\n- Tiffani arrives with data/geometry\n- First coordinated Soulpulse (she marks, he powers)\n- Success with visible cost increase\n- Her critique of his technique (care disguised as analysis)\n- His recognition: she sees him, not his heroism\n- Exit: they've choreographed perfectly without planning\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 coordinated use (arch stabilization)\n- Demonstrates improved targeting reduces cost (her geometry + his power)\n- Still significant cost: tremor persistent, vein-lines linger\n\n**Relationship:** First true partnership action—coordination through shared competence\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 progressing, cost accumulation visible\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 7: \"Quiet Calculations\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 5-6\n- **Setting:** Tiffani's workshop/planning space\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,500 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani compiles data from multiple hold sites, building predictive model for failure patterns. She discovers Jhace's cost curve is exponential not linear—he's approaching breaking point faster than he realizes. Pella asks why she cares so much about \"the man with the glow.\" Tiffani's answer: precision requires complete data, and he's destroying the most interesting variable. But underneath: she's starting to see him as person, not just phenomenon. She calculates how to approach him with data he'll actually hear (not orders, not concern, but operational logic). Ends with her walking toward his forge with documented proof he needs to see.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Data compilation and pattern analysis\n- Discovery: cost curve is exponential (crisis point approaching)\n- Pella questions her investment\n- Tiffani's rationalization vs. actual feeling\n- Strategic planning: how to make him listen\n- Decision to approach with evidence\n- Exit: walking toward forge with logbook\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (analysis chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani recognizing care underneath her analytical framework\n\n**Stage Progression:** External calculation of his trajectory toward Stage 2\n\n---\n# 【6†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file6\nContent Snippet:\n**Relationship:** First true partnership action—coordination through shared competence\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 progressing, cost accumulation visible\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 7: \"Quiet Calculations\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 5-6\n- **Setting:** Tiffani's workshop/planning space\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,500 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani compiles data from multiple hold sites, building predictive model for failure patterns. Success but greater cost: tremor worsens, vein-lines persist longer. Tiffani critiques his \"inefficient\" force application—her precision could reduce his cost by 30%. Jhace's reaction: (1) she's critiquing me, (2) she's right, (3) she sees my inefficiency and wants to fix it. Partnership recognition through competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis opening: arch failing, people still inside\n- Jhace's traditional methods insufficient\n- Tiffani arrives with data/geometry\n- First coordinated Soulpulse (she marks, he powers)\n- Success with visible cost increase\n- Her critique of his technique (care disguised as analysis)\n- His recognition: she sees him, not his heroism\n- Exit: they've choreographed perfectly without planning\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 coordinated use (arch stabilization)\n- Demonstrates improved targeting reduces cost (her geometry + his power)\n- Still significant cost: tremor persistent, vein-lines linger\n\n**Relationship:** First true partnership action—coordination through shared competence\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 progressing, cost accumulation visible\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 7: \"Quiet Calculations\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 5-6\n- **Setting:** Tiffani's workshop/planning space\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,500 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani compiles data from multiple hold sites, building predictive model for failure patterns. She discovers Jhace's cost curve is exponential not linear—he's approaching breaking point faster than he realizes. Pella asks why she cares so much about \"the man with the glow.\" Tiffani's answer: precision requires complete data, and he's destroying the most interesting variable. But underneath: she's starting to see him as person, not just phenomenon. She calculates how to approach him with data he'll actually hear (not orders, not concern, but operational logic). Ends with her walking toward his forge with documented proof he needs to see.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Data compilation and pattern analysis\n- Discovery: cost curve is exponential (crisis point approaching)\n- Pella questions her investment\n- Tiffani's rationalization vs. actual feeling\n- Strategic planning: how to make him listen\n- Decision to approach with evidence\n- Exit: walking toward forge with logbook\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (analysis chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani recognizing care underneath her analytical framework\n\n**Stage Progression:** External calculation of his trajectory toward Stage 2\n\n---\n# 【8†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file10\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Mobel leads cleansing ritual where early resonants prove themselves through enduring pain without using Soulpulse. She feels power of her Principle—clarity, judgment, separation of righteous from corrupt. Second segment: Moment of god-awareness—she senses Form-aligned power across planet (Corlexi stirring). Recognition: there are others like her, alliance or war inevitable. Principle limitation shown: sees only absolutes, cannot tolerate ambiguity. Auditor observes: Purity will clash with Form's institutional approach, both will target Jhace/Tiffani's third path.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm (pain as purification)\n- Purity Principle bias (absolute judgment)\n- First inter-god awareness\n- Innocent frame (no recognition of divinity)\n- Foreshadows ideological warfare\n- Auditor frame\n\n**Purpose:** Establish second divine force, show Principles in conflict, set up multi-front pressure on protagonists\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"**\n- **POV:** Both (intimate alternating perspective)\n- **Tide:** 17-18\n- **Setting:** Quiet space after crisis (Jhace's forge or Tiffani's workshop)\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nPost-Ch10 processing. Jhace's rage has cooled to exhaustion. Tiffani's certainty has cracked to fear—she nearly watched him die. Neither apologizes (both were right, both were wrong). Instead: vulnerability exchange. Jhace tells her about the building collapse that killed his parents, the 23 names he carries, why he can't choose who to save (it's choosing who dies). Tiffani tells him about Marisse, the miscalculation, why precision is penance not preference. Recognition: they're both trying to atone for deaths they couldn't prevent. She says: \"I need you to survive this.\" He says: \"I need you to keep stopping me.\" Not love confession—operational truth. They are load-bearing for each other. Moment of physical intimacy (forehead touch, hand hold—first deliberate contact not in crisis). Commitment without words.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Post-crisis exhaustion (defenses down)\n- Trauma exchange: his 23 names, her Marisse\n- Recognition: both atoning for unprevented deaths\n- Mutual need acknowledged (operational, not romantic)\n- Physical intimacy: first non-crisis contact\n- Commitment: she keeps him honest, he trusts her override\n- Exit: understanding they can't survive apart\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (recovery/intimacy chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Trauma-bonding → mutual recognition of load-bearing function\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 recovering with rest, but baseline effects permanent\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"**\n- **POV:** Both (balanced perspective)\n- **Tide:** 19-20\n- **Setting:** Rebuilt overlook above Saint Merro, forge wall\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nWeeks after immediate crisis. Jhace and Tiffani walk their district at Quench, seeing uneven results: some structures beautifully reinforced, others held by temporary measures, some families back in homes, others permanently displaced.\n# 【11†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file11\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nCritical main support failing during high-traffic period. Hundreds at risk. Jhace and Tiffani coordinate under pressure: she calculates load paths in real-time, he applies force to her exact specifications. They move as single unit, no wasted words, pure operational sync. Success—but Jhace pushes past safe limits to ensure no one dies. Cost: approaches Stage 2 threshold (tremor violent, vein-lines permanent at baseline, brief moment of sensory overload). Tiffani physically restrains him from continuing, makes decision FOR him: \"You're done. I don't care if you hate me for it.\" Saves him by overriding his control. Afterward, his rage at her presumption vs. her cold certainty she was right. Tension peaks: control vs. care, autonomy vs. survival.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis: hundreds at risk, main support failing\n- Perfect coordination under pressure\n- Jhace pushes past limits (caretaker compulsion)\n- Tiffani's override: physical intervention\n- Success but near-Stage 2 cost\n- His rage: she took his choice\n- Her certainty: he would have killed himself\n- Relationship stress: care as control\n- Exit: silent fury and mutual recognition they need each other\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 major use (near-Stage 2 threshold)\n- Coordinated efficiency maintains, but scale drives cost\n- Brief sensory overload (Stage 2 warning sign)\n- Vein-lines now faintly visible at rest\n\n**Relationship:** Conflict through care—she saves him by controlling him, he hates needing it\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 → approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs present)\n\n---\n\n### **INTERLUDE II: \"Mobel - Purity Rising\"**\n- **POV:** Mobel (Purity god-vessel, mortal name unknown to self)\n- **Tide:** ~15-16\n- **Setting:** Entry-side (opposite hemisphere from Saint Merro)\n- **Type:** Build-Up\n- **Word Count Target:** 600-900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Mobel leads cleansing ritual where early resonants prove themselves through enduring pain without using Soulpulse. She feels power of her Principle—clarity, judgment, separation of righteous from corrupt. Second segment: Moment of god-awareness—she senses Form-aligned power across planet (Corlexi stirring). Recognition: there are others like her, alliance or war inevitable. Principle limitation shown: sees only absolutes, cannot tolerate ambiguity. Auditor observes: Purity will clash with Form's institutional approach, both will target Jhace/Tiffani's third path.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm (pain as purification)\n- Purity Principle bias (absolute judgment)\n- First inter-god awareness\n- Innocent frame (no recognition of divinity)\n- Foreshadows ideological warfare\n- Auditor frame\n\n**Purpose:** Establish second divine force, show Principles in conflict, set up multi-front pressure on protagonists\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"**\n- **POV:** Both (intimate alternating perspective)\n- **Tide:** 17-18\n- **Setting:** Quiet space after crisis (Jhace's forge or Tiffani's workshop)\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nPost-Ch10 processing. Jhace's rage has cooled to exhaustion. Tiffani's certainty has cracked to fear—she nearly watched him die.\n# 【12†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file12\nContent Snippet:\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Jhace aware of Tiffani by reputation only; judges her as rigid perfectionist\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Pouring (mid-morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,280 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani measures atmospheric distortion from her observatory, detecting the incoming Piercing object through theodolite readings. She has been awake for days, driven by perfectionism rooted in Marisse's death. Pella brings food and scolds her for not resting. Measurements show descent trajectory—object is not passing over, it's coming down. First tremor hits. White line appears in sky. Tiffani and Pella evacuate as roar begins.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Mirrors Ch 1 structure (parallel observation)\n- Establishes Tiffani's precision methodology\n- Pella introduces care/support dynamic\n- Marisse trauma implied through vigilance pattern\n- Atmospheric distortion measurements (first scientific data)\n- Exit as sky splits\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani aware of Jhace by reputation only; judges him as overextending ego-driven builder\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 3: \"The Sky Breaks\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Striking (Piercing event)\n- **Setting:** Underface tunnel, then street level at building support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Word Count:** 2,200 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n# 【13†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file13\nContent Snippet:\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n- Ch 1-2: Stage 0 (pre-Piercing)\n- Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset\n- Ch 4-9: Stage 1 baseline\n- Ch 10: Approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs)\n- Ch 11-12: Stage 1 stabilized with management\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION TRACKING\n# 【14†N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md†file-RuvhJQx9wbkX3ZtAkhrdR7】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file14\nContent Snippet:\n**Placement:**  \n- Between Chapter 11 and Chapter 12.  \n- Reader must have just experienced:  \n  - The RRO confrontation over registration and control.  \n  - Jhace and Tiffani rejecting centralized assignment in favor of their own judgment.\n\n**POV & Camera:**\n- Third-person limited, anchored to **Salee**.\n- No omniscient commentary, no divine angle.\n\n**Setting:**\n- Communal food/meeting space in the Dregs.\n- Evening/Quench phase after a day of severe strain.\n\n**Length Target:**\n- 800–1,200 words.\n\n**Beat Shape:**\n- Structural Beat Type: **“Connection as Infrastructure”** (quiet mirror of Jhace’s structural work).\n- Emotional arc:  \n  - Weariness → quiet satisfaction → sober realism.  \n  - Not “hopeful optimism” but “we are doing the work and people are still alive.”\n\n**Core Functions:**\n1. Show the crisis from the bottom:\n   - Families clustered around improvised meals.\n   - Shared childcare, bed-space rotations, watch schedules.\n   - Barter and labor exchange as survival tools.\n2. Establish Salee’s worldview:\n   - People save people; relationships are the infrastructure that doesn’t collapse.\n   - Coordination is horizontal, not hierarchical.\n   - Deep suspicion of institutions that only show up when it’s politically convenient.\n3. Contrast with RRO:\n   - She hears about (or briefly interacts with) RRO edicts.\n   - See them as “far away” and slow compared to what the Dregs need.\n   - She is not anti-structure: she’s building a different kind of structure.\n4. Connect to J/T indirectly:\n   - News that “there are a couple up-slope who hold buildings with their bare hands” reaches her.\n   - She is more interested in whether they’ll work *with* her network, not for any office.\n\n**Allowed Knowledge:**\n- Salee may:\n  - Know roughly that unusual abilities exist.\n  - Hear rumors about Jhace/Tiffani’s work as anonymous heroes.\n- She may NOT:\n  - Know their names or detailed power mechanics.\n  - Recognize them as long-term linchpins of the world.\n  - Have any direct awareness of cosmic-scale stakes.\n\n**Tone & Voice:**\n- Warm, practical, unsentimental.\n- Uses humor to keep people moving:  \n  - e.g., joking about “promotions” meaning “you get to cook instead of dig latrines today.”\n- Never airy or idealistic; everything is grounded in logistics and bodies.\n\n**Exit Hook:**\n- Ends with an action, not a speech:  \n  - e.g., Salee standing to go negotiate a food redistribution or shelter swap.\n- The last feeling should be: “this is what real work looks like from the ground.”\n\n---\n\n### 3. EPILOGUE — AUDITOR\n\n**Placement:**  \n- After Chapter 15, after both Interludes.  \n- Final text object in N01.\n\n**POV & Camera:**\n- Claustrophically controlled perspective labeled as **The Auditor**.\n- Voice is clinical, observational, almost report-like.\n- Absolutely no emotional self-exposure.\n\n**Setting:**\n- Abstract / unlocated.  \n  - No specific physical room, city, or body described.\n  - Focus on data, trends, and projections.\n\n**Length Target:**\n- 600–800 words.\n\n**Beat Shape:**\n- Structural Beat Type: **“Meta Assessment”** (post-season scouting report).\n- Emotional arc:  \n  - Detachment throughout.  \n  - Subtle tilt from “baseline” to “increased interest.”\n# 【15†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file15\nContent Snippet:\n**Relationship:** First true partnership action—coordination through shared competence\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 progressing, cost accumulation visible\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 7: \"Quiet Calculations\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 5-6\n- **Setting:** Tiffani's workshop/planning space\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,500 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani compiles data from multiple hold sites, building predictive model for failure patterns. She discovers Jhace's cost curve is exponential not linear—he's approaching breaking point faster than he realizes. Pella asks why she cares so much about \"the man with the glow.\" Tiffani's answer: precision requires complete data, and he's destroying the most interesting variable. But underneath: she's starting to see him as person, not just phenomenon. She calculates how to approach him with data he'll actually hear (not orders, not concern, but operational logic). Ends with her walking toward his forge with documented proof he needs to see.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Data compilation and pattern analysis\n- Discovery: cost curve is exponential (crisis point approaching)\n- Pella questions her investment\n- Tiffani's rationalization vs. actual feeling\n- Strategic planning: how to make him listen\n- Decision to approach with evidence\n- Exit: walking toward forge with logbook\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (analysis chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani recognizing care underneath her analytical framework\n\n**Stage Progression:** External calculation of his trajectory toward Stage 2\n\n---\n\n### **INTERLUDE I: \"Corlexi - System vs Chaos\"**\n- **POV:** Corlexi (Form god-vessel, mortal name unknown to self)\n- **Tide:** ~6-7\n- **Setting:** Crown administrative chambers, multiple observation points\n- **Type:** Build-Up\n- **Word Count Target:** 600-900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Corlexi reviews stabilization reports, sees Order taking shape through RRO formation. She feels satisfaction at frameworks solidifying but also unease—something escapes her control. The smith and glassworker refuse to be contained, building third path outside her geometry. Second segment: She experiences her Principle (Form) as compulsion to impose structure, feels nascent god-awareness stirring (vast power constrained by single lens). She makes note: must address the anomaly in Saint Merro. Closing: Auditor observes Corlexi's awakening, notes Form's limitation—cannot tolerate things outside patterns.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm technique (god-vessel perception)\n- Principle bias manifestation (everything seen through Form lens)\n- Innocent frame maintained (no recognition of divinity)\n- Jhace/Tiffani recognized as structural threat\n- Auditor frame at open/close\n\n**Purpose:** Establish divine forces moving, show limitation of single-Principle perspective, foreshadow institutional pressure in Act III\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 8: \"Proof and Resistance\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 7-8\n- **Setting:** Jhace's forge, then joint inspection of hold sites\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_12 (Relationship Shift → Power Dynamic Change)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n# 【16†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file16\nContent Snippet:\nOn entry-side, Mobel lives inside pain. Shard in his leg becomes teacher: every throb is lesson in separation—what should remain and what must be cut away. He views world through sharpening lens, judging without intending to: weak structures, weak people, weak doctrine. He doesn't name it anything special; he names it clarity.\n\nIn moment of stillness, he becomes aware that something else is moving—another pressure, another intent—not here, but across world's curve. Awareness is not conversation, just clash of directional certainty.\n\nMobel decides that whatever is happening, it must be cleansed into an answer. Interlude ends with him stepping toward authority, not as servant, but as blade seeking handle.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Mobel's cleansing bias (Purity-aligned, unconscious)\n- Pain as teacher metaphor\n- No awareness of being unusual\n- Foreshadow ideological collision\n- Awareness of \"other pressure\" (Corlexi, unknowingly)\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,700 words  \n**POV:** Jhace  \n**Tide:** 20  \n**Setting:** Recovery space, forge\n\nAfter near-collapse, relationship stops pretending it's only work. Jhace's recovery is ugly: shaking, nausea, pain-laced sleep, fear that next pulse will take something permanent. Tiffani is forced into caretaking without losing her edge; Pella and Bram support without crowding.\n\nIn quiet, Jhace admits he can't keep doing this without her—without the method, and without human anchor. Tiffani admits she can't keep treating him as data source without becoming monster.\n\nThey become lovers not as reward, but as pact: if they're going to risk bodies and reputations, they won't do it half-alive. They set rules for cost management and agree to face political consequences together.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Romantic relationship formalizes\n- Practical rather than sentimental\n- Cost management rules established\n- Commitment through shared risk\n- Support network maintains boundaries\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,800 words  \n**POV:** Alternating Jhace/Tiffani  \n**Tide:** 20  \n**Setting:** Planning space, street circuits\n\nCity doesn't stabilize; it only pauses between harms. Jhace and Tiffani review maps, limits, enemies they've made. They choose path forward: keep work public enough to prevent quiet capture, but disciplined enough to survive.\n\nThey plan \"holding circuit\" with rotation, witnesses, documentation—method as protection. There is tenderness, but it's threaded through dread: another Tide of tremors could break everything again.\n\nTheir final act is not grand rescue; it's decision to keep showing up with eyes open, accepting that holding costs, and that love doesn't erase cost—it makes payment worth naming.\n\nChapter ends with them stepping back into streets as unit: not safe, not certain, but aligned.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Practical planning for future work\n- Public accountability as protection\n- Tenderness mixed with dread\n- Love doesn't eliminate cost\n- Commitment to continue despite uncertainty\n\n---\n\n### EPILOGUE\n**Word Target:** ~900 words  \n**POV:** Institutional observer (unnamed)  \n**Tide:** 20+  \n**Setting:** Administrative chamber\n\nQuiet room. File begins. Someone with institutional power—careful hands, careful language—compiles first real ledger of \"resonant stabilization.\" Names recorded alongside locations, effects, cost symptoms.\n\nReport frames phenomenon as resource and threat. Line drawn between \"public safety\" and \"unlicensed interference.\"\n\nOutside window, Saint Merro creaks; inside, policy is forged from fear.\n# 【17†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file17\nContent Snippet:\nTiffani tells him about Marisse, the miscalculation, why precision is penance not preference. Recognition: they're both trying to atone for deaths they couldn't prevent. She says: \"I need you to survive this.\" He says: \"I need you to keep stopping me.\" Not love confession—operational truth. They are load-bearing for each other. Moment of physical intimacy (forehead touch, hand hold—first deliberate contact not in crisis). Commitment without words.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Post-crisis exhaustion (defenses down)\n- Trauma exchange: his 23 names, her Marisse\n- Recognition: both atoning for unprevented deaths\n- Mutual need acknowledged (operational, not romantic)\n- Physical intimacy: first non-crisis contact\n- Commitment: she keeps him honest, he trusts her override\n- Exit: understanding they can't survive apart\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (recovery/intimacy chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Trauma-bonding → mutual recognition of load-bearing function\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 recovering with rest, but baseline effects permanent\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"**\n- **POV:** Both (balanced perspective)\n- **Tide:** 19-20\n- **Setting:** Rebuilt overlook above Saint Merro, forge wall\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nWeeks after immediate crisis. Jhace and Tiffani walk their district at Quench, seeing uneven results: some structures beautifully reinforced, others held by temporary measures, some families back in homes, others permanently displaced. Every scar holds memory. They discuss larger currents: registries spreading, clinics multiplying, RRO expanding, black-market training proliferating. They know storm is forming beyond their neighborhood. What they can control: what they hold. At forge, they write three priorities on wall: (1) Truth over comfort, (2) People over institutions, (3) Keep each other honest about why we act. Vow without ceremony. Community members add their marks beside it—informal pact, local mandate. Not oath to Crown or RRO, but commitment to third path. Jhace's hand shakes as he writes; Tiffani laces their fingers, lets his tremor move through both. Physical reminder: cost and commitment shared. Exit: they face uncertain future together, foundation locked.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Walk through rebuilt district (uneven success)\n- Recognition of larger forces gathering\n- Decision: focus on what they can hold\n- Three priorities written on forge wall\n- Community witnesses/joins commitment\n- Shared physical moment (tremor through linked hands)\n- Acceptance: this is who they are together\n- Exit: foundation locked, future uncertain but faced together\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (commitment/resolution chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Partnership formalized through shared principles (not romance, but deeper)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline accepted as new permanent state\n\n---\n\n### **EPILOGUE: \"Auditor Observation\"**\n- **POV:** Auditor (meta-perspective outside time)\n- **Setting:** Auditor's observation point (undefined location)\n- **Word Count Target:** 800-1,000 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n# 【18†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file18\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n\n**Relationship:** First meeting through competence recognition (she measures, he builds)\n\n**Stage Progression:** 0 → 1 onset (tremor baseline establishing)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 4: \"After the Shudder\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 1, Kindling (morning after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_02 (Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 1\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,350 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace wakes with persistent tremor and pain. Bram reports Chloen's leg healing unexpectedly. Jhace tests power on cracked support without immediate threat—FAILS (no activation). Runner brings emergency: healer's triage wall failing with wounded inside. Immediate threat present—power WORKS but cost increases (vein-lines to elbows, worse tremor). Tiffani observes, asks to see touch points for data collection. Jhace tries to pick up nail—fails three times, tremor jerks it free. Concealment behavior (hands behind back). Tiffani sees everything, documents cost in logbook.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Wakes to baseline tremor (Stage 1 established)\n- Chloen's healing confirmed (side-effect established)\n- Power test on non-critical crack: FAILS (conditional activation shown)\n- Emergency at triage wall: SUCCEEDS (immediate threat required)\n- Cost progression: worse tremor, can't grip nail (functional loss)\n- Tiffani introduction proper: analytical, demands data\n- J/T dynamic: reluctant compliance because her logic is sound\n- Exit: \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 successful use (triage wall), 1 failed attempt (cracked support)\n- Conditional activation confirmed: requires immediate protective need\n- Cost accumulation shown: Stage 1 baseline tremor, fine motor compromised\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani decides to engage deliberately (prevent his destruction through measurement)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline established, functional loss beginning\n\n---\n# 【19†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn17file19\nContent Snippet:\n**Key Beats:**\n- Tiffani presents evidence (his cost curve)\n- Jhace's defensive resistance (control/autonomy threatened)\n- Her cutting through: stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks\n- Tension: care vs. pride\n- Proposal: test efficiency method\n- Controlled test succeeds\n- His grudging acceptance: she makes him better\n- Exit: agreement to coordinate on next real crisis\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 controlled test (reduced cost confirmed with her targeting)\n\n**Relationship:** Resistance → acceptance that partnership improves both\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 stable, cost reduction possible with coordination\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 10-12\n- **Setting:** Glass Quarter refugee camp, Crown administrative contact\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,700 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nCorvin (Mason faction) and Tellis (Water Guild) arrive at Glass Quarter offering \"help\" in exchange for political alignment\nVisible: 0% - 98%",

            "# N01: THE GRIP THAT HOLDS - CHAPTER GRID (12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE)\n**REVISED EDITION - MANDATORY REFERENCE FOR ALL PROSE GENERATION**\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL NOTICE\n\n**THIS GRID MUST BE CONSULTED BEFORE:**\n- Writing any new prose\n- Revising any existing prose\n- Making structural decisions\n- Adjusting beat assignments\n- Moving content between chapters\n\n**ANY DEVIATION FROM THIS GRID REQUIRES EXPLICIT AUTHORIZATION AND GRID UPDATE.**\n\n---\n\n## NOVELLA PARAMETERS\n\n- **Novella ID:** N01\n- **Title:** The Grip That Holds\n- **Turning:** 1 - GLASS (Fragile formation, first awakening)\n- **Acrostic Letter:** G (first novella of GLASS)\n- **Structure:** 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue = 15 segments\n- **Word Count Target:** 18,000-21,000 words total\n- **Chapter Target Range:** 1,200-2,200 words (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- **Timeline Covered:** Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20 (~20 days post-Piercing)\n\n---\n\n## ACT STRUCTURE\n\n### **ACT I: THE CUT (Chapters 1-4)**\n- **Function:** Crisis establishes characters, introduces Soulpulse, first contact\n- **Word Count:** ~9,170 words (45% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 4\n- **Emotional Arc:** Low → Low → High → Medium\n\n### **ACT II: THE PATTERN (Chapters 5-9)**\n- **Function:** Partnership forms, patterns emerge, institutional pressure builds\n- **Word Count:** ~8,900 words (43% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 5 (includes Interlude I after Ch 7)\n- **Emotional Arc:** Medium → Medium → Low → Medium → Medium\n\n### **ACT III: THE GRIP (Chapters 10-12)**\n- **Function:** Crisis tests bond, commitment made, foundation locked\n- **Word Count:** ~5,200 words (25% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 3 (includes Interlude II after Ch 10)\n- **Emotional Arc:** High → High → Medium\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER GRID\n\n### **CHAPTER 1: \"Something Wrong\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Kindling (morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection run\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,240 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace inspects heat-grid junctions in his ritualistic pre-Piercing routine. He detects an \"off-note\" in the grid hum—pressure deviation signaling potential failure. Bram teases him about working alone and obsessive checking patterns. Ground tremor hits. Jhace feels atmospheric change (copper tang, pressure shift). Chapter establishes his trauma-driven vigilance and introduces Tiffani through guild gossip as \"the glassworker with impossible standards.\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens with wrong hum detection (immediate sensory hook)\n- Establishes Underface setting and Jhace's expertise\n- Bram introduces caretaker/control theme\n- First tremor (foreshadowing)\n- Ends with Jhace at street level, sensing wrongness\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Jhace aware of Tiffani by reputation only; judges her as rigid perfectionist\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Pouring (mid-morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,280 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani measures atmospheric distortion from her observatory, detecting the incoming Piercing object through theodolite readings. She has been awake for days, driven by perfectionism rooted in Marisse's death. Pella brings food and scolds her for not resting. Measurements show descent trajectory—object is not passing over, it's coming down. First tremor hits. White line appears in sky. Tiffani and Pella evacuate as roar begins.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Mirrors Ch 1 structure (parallel observation)\n- Establishes Tiffani's precision methodology\n- Pella introduces care/support dynamic\n- Marisse trauma implied through vigilance pattern\n- Atmospheric distortion measurements (first scientific data)\n- Exit as sky splits\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani aware of Jhace by reputation only; judges him as overextending ego-driven builder\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 3: \"The Sky Breaks\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Striking (Piercing event)\n- **Setting:** Underface tunnel, then street level at building support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Word Count:** 2,200 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits.     "672067c1-41d8-4b85-9398-62a5d01f6df8": {

          "parts": [
            "## N01_Timeline_Skeleton_12ch.md (REVISED to match the 12-ch grid)[1]\n\n```md\n# N01 TIMELINE SKELETON — 12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE (REVISED) [file:14]\n\n**Purpose:** Anchor N01 chapters + interludes + epilogue in Tides/phases, major events, Soulpulse progression, and relationship states. [file:14]\n\n## 1) High-level timeline\n- **Tide 0:** Pre-impact setup + the Piercing (Ch 1–3). [file:14]\n- **Tide 1:** Immediate aftermath and first controlled validation of activation conditions (Ch 4). [file:14]\n- **Tides 2–8:** Pattern-building and partnership method formation, with first divine/interloper pressure glimpse (Ch 5–6 + Interlude I + Ch 8). [file:14]\n- **Tides 9–13:** Off-page time (referenced only; do not dramatize unless the grid changes). [file:14]\n- **Tides 14–16:** Major crisis + near-Stage-2 warning signs + second divine/interloper glimpse (Ch 9 + Interlude II). [file:14]\n- **Tides 17–18:** Off-page recovery time. [file:14]\n- **Tides 19–20:** Commitment and third-path foundation lock (Ch 11). [file:14]\n- **Tide 20+:** Auditor epilogue assessment (Ch 12). [file:14]\n\n## 2) Chapter-by-chapter table (grid authority)\n| Segment | Tide | Phase (if specified) | POV | Title | Notes |\n|---|---:|---|---|---|---|\n| Ch 1 | 0 | Kindling | Jhace | Something Wrong | Pre-Piercing “off-note” heat-grid hum, first tremor foreshadow. [file:14] |\n| Ch 2 | 0 | Pouring | Tiffani | Glass That Listens | Atmospheric distortion measured; white line appears; evacuation begins. [file:14] |\n| Ch 3 | 0 | Striking | Jhace | The Sky Breaks | Piercing; 2 Soulpulse uses; Chloen rescue + unexplained healing; first J/T contact. [file:14] |\n| Ch 4 | 1 | Kindling | Jhace | After the Shudder | Failed non-emergency test; emergency triage wall success; Stage 1 baseline established; Tiffani starts logging cost. [file:14] |\n| Ch 5 | 2–3 | (not fixed) | Tiffani | Lines That Don’t Lie | Maps damage + residue; refuses co-option; decides to engage Jhace directly. [file:14] |\n| Ch 6 | 5–6 | (not fixed) | Tiffani | Weight That Won’t Yield | Builds cost curve model; realizes exponential trajectory; plans how to get Jhace to listen. [file:14] |\n| Interlude I | 6–7 | (not fixed) | Corlexi | Quiet Calculations | RRO/Order lens; “anomaly” awareness; innocent frame maintained. [file:14] |\n| Ch 8 | 7–8 | (not fixed) | Jhace | (Grid label: “Interlude I: System vs Chaos” content) | Tiffani presents cost curve; controlled efficiency test; reluctant acceptance. [file:14] |\n| Ch 9 | 10–12 | (not fixed) | Tiffani | Proof and Resistance | Political offer + refusal; first explicit choice of each other over safety. [file:14] |\n| Ch 10 | 14–15 | (not fixed) | Both (alternating) | Ash and Claims | Major support failure; perfect sync; Jhace overextends; Tiffani overrides. [file:14] |\n| Interlude II | 15–16 | (not fixed) | Mobel | Near Collapse | Purity lens; innocent frame; senses other powers; flags J/T as complication. [file:14] |\n| Ch 11 | 17–18 | (not fixed) | Both (intimate alternating) | (Grid label: “Interlude II: Intimacy” content) | Vulnerability exchange; trauma truths; first non-crisis contact; operational commitment. [file:14] |\n| Ch 12 | 19–20 | Quench | Both (balanced) | Holding Together | Walk the district; write three priorities; community witnesses; tremor shared through linked hands. [file:14] |\n| Epilogue | 20+ | — | Auditor | Choosing the Weight | Clinical audit; “useful anomaly” assessment; forecasts pressure in N02. [file:14] |\n\n## 3) Soulpulse progression (N01 scope)\n**Observed activation constraints:** Requires direct contact and protective desperation; casual testing fails. [file:1][file:14]  \n**Cost visibility rule:** Every use must show copper-gold manifestation + strain + aftermath (tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion). [file:1][file:8]\n\n- Ch 3: Two activations (beam rescue; then structural support), cost escalates within the same Tide. [file:14][file:1]\n- Ch 4: One failed attempt (no immediate threat) + one successful emergency hold (triage wall). [file:14][file:1]\n- Ch 5: No activation; observation and mapping only. [file:14]\n- Ch 6: One coordinated activation (arch stabilization) with Tiffani targeting. [file:14]\n- Ch 8: One controlled efficiency test activation; cost measurably reduced by method. [file:14]\n- Ch 10: One major activation; near-Stage-2 warning signs (violent tremor; vein-lines persisting; sensory overload). [file:14][file:1]\n\n## 4) Stage-state consistency (as described in prose; no numbered labels in-world)\n- **Stage 0** only before Ch 3. [file:14][file:1][file:8]\n- **Stage 1 baseline** established by Ch 4 and maintained through most of N01. [file:14][file:1]\n- **Approaching Stage 2 threshold** appears at the major crisis (grid: Ch 10). [file:14][file:1]\n- **Do not** have Stage-2 baseline behavior earlier than the grid allows. [file:14][file:8]\n\n## 5) Relationship progression anchors (J/T)\n- Reputation-only mutual judgment (Ch 1–2). [file:14]\n- Forced proximity and competence-recognition meeting (Ch 3). [file:14]\n- Measurement/observation becomes deliberate engagement (Ch 4–6). [file:14]\n- Method test: resistance → acceptance (Ch 8). [file:14]\n- Explicit partnership choice over pragmatism (Ch 9). [file:14]\n- Control vs care rupture during crisis (Ch 10). [file:14]\n- Vulnerability exchange and load-bearing intimacy (Ch 11). [file:14]\n- Community-witnessed principles and shared tremor acceptance (Ch 12). [file:14]\n- Auditor frames bond as “functional love” and “useful anomaly” (Epilogue). [file:14]\n\n## 6) Update rules\nIf any chapter’s Tide, POV, beat method, or Soulpulse usage changes, the grid must be updated first, then this skeleton updated to match. [file:14]\n```\n\n## Relationship_Dynamics_12ch.md (REVISED to match the 12-ch grid)[1]\n\n```md\n# RELATIONSHIP DYNAMICS — 12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE (REVISED) [file:14]\n\n**Purpose:** Track relationship arcs by segment so scenes don’t jump ahead, regress, or contradict the grid’s emotional and structural intent. [file:14]\n\n## 1) Jhace & Tiffani (primary arc)\n| Segment | State label | What it must look like on-page |\n|---|---|---|\n| Ch 1–2 | Mutual Judgment (Remote) | Each thinks the other’s reputation implies danger (ego vs perfectionism) before meeting. [file:14] |\n| Ch 3 | Forced Proximity | First “we survived the same collapse” contact; competence first, warmth later. [file:14] |\n| Ch 4 | Early Respect (Measured) | She logs; he resists being watched; cost visibility becomes relational pressure. [file:14][file:1] |\n| Ch 5–6 | Analytical Engagement | Tiffani commits to truth over comfort and chooses street-level data over institutional shelter. [file:14] |\n| Ch 8 | Resistance → Acceptance | Jhace hates being “managed,” but accepts method because it works and saves lives. [file:14] |\n| Ch 9 | Choice Over Safety | They refuse a power offer together, becoming political targets; partnership becomes explicit action. [file:14] |\n| Ch 10 | Crisis Sync + Rupture | They move as one under load—then she physically stops him; care-as-control conflict peaks. [file:14] |\n| Ch 11 | Trauma Bonding → Operational Commitment | They trade the “why” beneath their obsessions (23 names / Marisse); intimacy is functional. [file:14][file:13] |\n| Ch 12 | Public Principles | Forge-wall priorities; community witness; shared tremor through linked hands. [file:14] |\n| Epilogue | Externally Logged | Auditor describes bond as load-bearing and “useful anomaly” (no romance framing). [file:14] |\n\n## 2) Jhace & Bram\n- Bram is Jhace’s stabilizing witness and the person who can say “stop” even when Jhace won’t. [file:13]\n- Bram’s presence supports early characterization (Ch 1–4) and reinforces the cost-and-care theme through quiet observation. [file:14][file:13]\n\n## 3) Tiffani & Pella\n- Pella’s repeated care actions (food, ink, scolding) define Tiffani’s refusal-to-rest pattern and make “precision as empathy” readable. [file:14]\n- Pella’s questions force Tiffani to admit (to herself) that her interest in Jhace is no longer only “data.” [file:14][file:13]\n\n## 4) Institutions & factions (relationship pressure)\n- The grid establishes multiple co-option attempts (committee positions, resource trades) and makes refusal a key character action, not background. [file:14]\n- Divine/vessel interludes (Corlexi, Mobel) must preserve innocent frame while still showing that J/T’s third path registers as a threat. [file:14][file:8]\n```\n\n## N01_Master_Bible_12ch.md (REVISED to match the 12-ch grid)[1]\n\n```md\n# N01: “THE GRIP THAT HOLDS” — MASTER BIBLE (12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE) [file:14]\n\n**Purpose:** A single reference for N01 prose generation aligned to the 12-chapter grid, maintaining world lexicon, knowledge gates, voice separation, and Soulpulse cost visibility. [file:14][file:8]\n\n## Novella parameters (authoritative)\n- **ID:** N01. [file:14]\n- **Title:** The Grip That Holds. [file:14]\n- **Turning:** 1 — GLASS. [file:14]\n- **Structure:** 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue (15 segments). [file:14]\n- **Target length:** 18,000–21,000 words. [file:14]\n- **Timeline covered:** Tide 0 through Tide 20 (~20 days post-Piercing). [file:14]\n\n## World state (N01 scope)\n- Saint Merro is a terraced guild city with proto-industrial tech and a heat-grid; no electricity and lexicon rules forbidding Earth units apply. [file:3][file:8]\n- The Piercing is a sky-shearing event that damages structures, disrupts systems, and seeds the conditions under which Soulpulse begins appearing. [file:3][file:14]\n\n## Core arc (12-ch condensed)\n- Jhace discovers Soulpulse during the Piercing and learns it is contact- and protection-dependent, not a free or casual ability. [file:14][file:1]\n- Tiffani becomes the primary analyst and method-builder, and the partnership forms through competence, conflict, and hard decisions rather than romance-forward beats. [file:14][file:13]\n- The bond resolves into a third-path commitment that refuses institutional co-option and is framed (especially by the Auditor) as functional, load-bearing love. [file:14]\n\n## Characters (N01 lens)\n### Jhace Torrins\n- Jhace’s voice is terse, physical, and guilt-driven, with humor landing accidentally and genuine laughter rare but loud when it happens. [file:13][file:8]\n- His core drive is preventing preventable death (parents died in a collapse), which makes him prone to overextension and self-erasure under crisis. [file:13]\n- Soulpulse cost must remain visible: tremor, vein-lines, pain, exhaustion, and worsening fine control when pushed. [file:1][file:8]\n\n### Tiffani Merrow\n- Tiffani’s voice is analytical and precise, growing more talkative when stressed, and her humor is sharp, timed, and often used to connect or cut tension. [file:13][file:8]\n- Her pattern obsession is rooted in Marisse’s death and her fear of missing signals again. [file:13]\n- She cannot use Soulpulse in N01 but becomes the system’s interpreter and the lever that reduces Jhace’s waste through targeting and method. [file:13][file:14]\n\n### Bram Kelter\n- Bram is a steady anchor with dry, resigned humor and long, observational cadence; he warns without forcing. [file:13][file:8]\n\n## Soulpulse (N01 scope rules)\n- Soulpulse activates through skin contact + desperate protective intention; non-critical tests fail and must be shown failing when attempted. [file:1][file:14]\n- Manifestation is copper-gold glow spreading into material with visible stress-pattern effects and vein-line tracery on the user’s arms. [file:1][file:8]\n- Cost progression must be described symptomatically (no in-world “Stage” labels) and must match the grid’s escalation points. [file:1][file:8][file:14]\n\n## Knowledge gates (non-negotiable)\n- Characters do not know why Soulpulse exists, do not understand “Stages” as formal categories, and do not have divine/seal/cosmic explanations. [file:8][file:1]\n- Interludes may hint at larger forces but must keep the innocent frame (vessels don’t recognize divinity). [file:14][file:8]\n- Epilogue Auditor is clinical and does not become emotional or reveal full cosmic mechanics. [file:14][file:8]\n\n## Structure overview (by act)\n### Act I: The Cut (Ch 1–4)\n- Two parallel observation chapters (Jhace then Tiffani) lead into the Piercing and first Soulpulse uses, then immediate aftermath validation of conditions and cost. [file:14]\n\n### Act II: The Pattern (Ch 5–9 + Interlude I)\n- Tiffani maps the city’s damage and Jhace’s cost curve, refuses co-option, and forces a method shift through proof, culminating in the first explicit partnership-over-safety choice. [file:14]\n\n### Act III: The Grip (Ch 10–12 + Interlude II + Epilogue)\n- Crisis tests autonomy vs care; intimacy is earned through vulnerability exchange; commitment is formalized publicly; Auditor logs the outcome as a system-relevant anomaly. [file:14]\n\n## Constraints summary (must be enforced every draft)\n- **Lexicon purity:** Never use Earth time/distance/direction units; use Tides/Anneals/Spans and landmark-based directions only. [file:3][file:8]\n- **Cost visibility:** Every Soulpulse use must show activation conditions + during-use strain + aftermath. [file:1][file:8]\n- **Voice separation:** Jhace/Tiffani/Bram/Corlexi/Mobel/Auditor must be unmistakable in cadence and lens. [file:8][file:13][file:14]\n- **Beat compliance:** Follow the beat method assigned by the grid and avoid forbidden sequences. [file:14][file:8]\n```\n\nIf you also want the “Tier 2 minor updates” (Character Dossiers references adjusted to the 12-ch mapping, updated Prose Workflow references, and a short 12-ch README/index), say so and they can be output in the same copy/paste-ready format.\n\n"
          ]
        },

            "Make sure to include fileciteturn0file3 in your response to cite this file, or to surface it as a link.",
            "## RELATIONSHIP DYNAMICS: N01 SCOPE\n\n**Purpose:** Track how the key relationships form, develop and function during N01 \"The Grip That Holds.\"  These summaries guide prose generation by clarifying which emotional beats occur where and what level of intimacy exists at any given point.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & TIFFANI – THE CORE PARTNERSHIP\n\nThis is the emotional spine of N01.  They move from strangers with misconceptions to partners who know they can’t survive the crisis without each other.\n\n#### Phase 1: Mutual Judgment (Ch 1–3)\n\n* **Jhace’s view:** Tiffani is the perfectionist who prevents work.  Guild gossip paints her as someone who refuses commissions if specifications aren’t perfect, causing projects to stall.  He assumes that sort of person will get good workers killed by refusing to act.\n* **Tiffani’s view:** Jhace is the overextending hero who thinks only he can save people.  She believes he takes on too much out of ego.  From a distance it looks like savior complex disguised as quality control.\n* **Reality:** Both are wrong.  They haven’t met yet and are projecting their own fears.  This phase ends when the Piercing forces their paths to cross.\n\n#### Phase 2: Competence Recognition (Ch 4–7)\n\n* **First contact (Ch 3):** Tiffani sees Jhace holding a collapsing building.  Her first reaction isn’t awe but analytical curiosity.  “What did you do?” matters more than “how brave.”\n* **His recognition:** During the major hold in Ch 6, Tiffani’s critique (“You’re applying force uniformly when the load is uneven”) annoys him until he realizes she’s right.  It wasn’t judgment; it was insight that saves lives.\n* **Her recognition:** In Ch 7, she sees how exhausted and shaken he is after each use.  She realizes his obsessive vigilance mirrors her own fear of missed patterns.  They’re both serving the same moral imperative with different tools.\n* **Status at the end of Phase 2:** They respect each other professionally.  Partnership is brewing but not yet named.  Care emerges as Tiffani catches him when he collapses and he insists she rest her injured leg.\n\n#### Phase 3: Partnership Formation (Ch 8–11)\n\n* **Trust building:** They integrate their efforts.  He allows her to guide where he applies his power; she modifies her analytical approach based on what his body can handle.  They decide resource priorities together.\n* **Conflict:** They have their first methodological fight in Ch 9 over how to train Kylar.  She wants slower emotional recovery; he wants immediate practice to rebuild confidence.  Asking Kylar what he needs resolves the fight and shows they can disagree without breaking trust.\n* **Physical intimacy emerges:** Not yet sexual.  Touch now includes brushing fingers, sitting shoulder to shoulder, passing cups during tremor.  Their bodies learn to relax around each other.\n* **Political alignment:** In Ch 11 they stand together against Corlexi’s registration demand.  It’s their first united front to an external authority.\n\n#### Phase 4: Romantic Recognition (Ch 12–15)\n\n* **The shift:** They both realize the partnership is more than professional.  Small non‑work gestures accumulate into undeniable feelings.  Silence becomes comfortable rather than loaded.\n* **Acknowledgment scene (Ch 13):** After a long day, she admits “This is more than work partnership, isn’t it?”  He replies “Yeah.”  That is their declaration.  They acknowledge romantic attraction without drama.\n* **Physical escalation:** They hold each other after crises, share body heat while sitting, enjoy the closeness.  Tiffani begins making surgical sex jokes directed at him (see Character Dossiers) to lighten tension.  They avoid consummation only because they’re physically exhausted.\n* **Commitment scene (Ch 15):** They sit in a quiet space and decide to keep holding together.  “The world is broken.  We can’t fix all of it.  But we can hold something.”  “Then we hold.”  No formal vows; this promise is enough.\n* **Status at novella end:** They are a pair in all but name.  The world sees them as partners.  They remain physically restrained because of exhaustion and crisis, but their emotional commitment is clear.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & BRAM – THE STEADY ANCHOR\n\nBram is Jhace’s older colleague and confidant.  He bridges the gap between Jhace and the rest of the world,\nThe file is too long and its contents have been truncated.\n"
          ]

            "Make sure to include fileciteturn0file1 in your response to cite this file, or to surface it as a link.",
            "## WORLD OF TE'OGA: N01 SCOPE\n\n**Purpose:** This document contains world-building information relevant to N01 \"The Grip That Holds\" — the physical reality, social structures, and baseline conditions of Te'Oga as experienced by characters during the first novella.\n\n---\n\n### SAINT MERRO: CITY GEOGRAPHY\n\nSaint Merro is a major urban center built in terraced layers up a hillside basin, with the river at the base and institutional districts at the peak.\n\n**District Structure (Lowest to Highest):**\n\n**The Dregs (River Level):**\n- Poorest district, oldest construction\n- Dense population, limited resources\n- Most vulnerable to structural failure\n- Industrial zones, slum housing\n- Where refugees gather post‑Piercing\n- Salee's community organizing happens here\n\n**Underface Districts (Mid‑Level):**\n- Working‑class residential and commercial\n- Built partially into hillside, partially above ground\n- Where most guild operations exist (Jhace's forge, craft shops)\n- Heat‑grid maintenance tunnels run beneath these areas\n- Mixed construction quality: some solid, much failing\n\n**Glass Quarter (Upper‑Mid Level):**\n- Precision craft district\n- Merrow Glass & Mirrors located here\n- Observatory tower at highest point of this district\n- Better construction but still vulnerable\n- Mixed workshops and wealthier residential\n\n**The Crown (Peak):**\n- Institutional and political center\n- Best construction, most resources\n- Least affected by Piercing initially\n- Where council meetings and governance occur\n- Where Corlexi works and institutional power concentrates\n\n**The Underface Heat‑Grid:**\n- Network of maintenance tunnels beneath working districts\n- Copper piping carries geothermal heat from deeper sources\n- Critical infrastructure for city heating and some industrial processes\n- Narrow passages, vertical risers, humid, constant rumble\n- Where Jhace spends much of his working time\n\n---\n\n### THE PIERCING: WHAT HAPPENED\n\n**Tide 0, Kindling Phase:** A massive cylindrical object penetrated Te'Oga's atmosphere. Not a meteor — trajectory wrong, entry angle impossible by natural physics.\n\n**Physical Characteristics:**\n- Roughly three hundred paces in diameter\n- Extended beyond visual range vertically\n- Moving at speeds that bent light around its passage\n- Never struck surface — passed through atmosphere and continued into deep space\n\n**Immediate Effects:**\n- Atmospheric compression waves shattered structures across half the continent\n- Seismic activity triggered widespread building collapse in urban centers\n- Introduction of alien particulate matter into atmosphere (fine metallic dust, faintly luminescent, composition unknown)\n- Disruption of traditional physics at quantum level\n\n**Post‑Piercing Conditions (As Understood in N01):**\n- Structural instability across all major cities requiring constant maintenance\n- Atmospheric changes: persistent haze, altered light quality\n- Social upheaval: traditional authority structures failing during crisis\n- Emergence of Soulpulse abilities in small population subset\n\n**What People Don't Know in N01:**\n- Why the Piercing happened\n- Who or what caused it\n- Whether it will happen again\n- The connection to ancient seals or divine entities\n- The long‑term implications for reality itself\n\n---\n\n### TECHNOLOGY LEVEL\n\n**Baseline (Pre‑Piercing):**\n- Renaissance/proto‑industrial equivalent\n- Water mills and windmills for mechanical power\n- Simple steam engines (boilers, pumps, limited applications)\n- Printing presses exist but expensive\n- Basic gunpowder exists but rare, not militarily common\n- NO electricity, telegraph, or rail\n- Lighting: oil lamps, candles, some gas lamps in wealthy areas\n- Communication: runners, courier networks, signal flags from towers\n- Medicine: herbal remedies, surgical knowledge (no germ theory but practical anatomy)\n- Metallurgy: advanced (quality steel, alloys, precision work)\n\n**Post‑Piercing Changes:**\n- Infrastructure failing faster than repairs can address\n- Traditional engineering increasingly inadequate for damaged structures\n- Soulpulse emerging as potential supplement/replacement for mechanical systems\n- Social structures breaking down as guilds lose authority\n- Technology advancement stalled while society focuses on survival\n\n---\n\n### SOCIAL STRUCTURES\n\n**Guild System:**\n\nGuilds control craft knowledge, training, and trade licensing. They are protective of their knowledge and suspicious of innovation that threatens their control.\n\n**Major Guilds Relevant to N01:**\n- **Forgemasters Guild:** Metalworking, structural repair, heat management (Jhace and Bram's guild affiliation)\n- **Glassworkers Guild:** Precision crafts, optics, observation instruments (Tiffani's guild)\n- **Builders Guild:** Architecture, construction, large‑scale projects\n- **Thermal Guild:** Heat\nThe file is too long and its contents have been truncated.\n"
