|\n\n---\n\n**Scope Note:**  The dynamics described here apply only through the end of N01. Later novellas will alter, deepen, or conclude these relationships. Prose generation must not leak future knowledge or prematurely accelerate these arcs.\n# 【3†N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md†file-CAd4CywpTdBkiKM88Yt3Ti】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file3\nContent Snippet:\n| Political confrontation                                    |\n| I2 | Dregs communal space                    | Community organizing lens                                  |\n| 12 | Neglected lower districts               | Moral decision about where help goes first                 |\n| 13 | Quiet, semi-private space (forge/back room/roof) | Emotional decompression & acknowledgment            |\n| 14 | Edge of multiple districts (load-bearing node) | High-stakes collapse prevention                       |\n| 15 | Quiet private space (rooftop/back room) | Commitment scene                                           |\n| E  | Unlocated abstraction                   | Auditor report space                                       |\n\n---\n\n### 4. TIDE-LEVEL TIMELINE SNAPSHOT\n\nApproximate; exact phase mapping lives in `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`.\n\n| Tide | Phase(s) Primarily Covered          | Chapters            | Key Events                                      |\n|------|-------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------|\n| 0    | Late Kindling → Striking/Quench    | 1–3                 | Pre-Piercing baseline → The Piercing → first uses |\n| 1    | Kindling → Striking                | 4–6 + Interlude I   | Immediate aftermath, first big hold, institutional view |\n| 2    | Kindling → Quench                  | 7–10                | Training loop, failures, synthesis, mature rescue |\n| 3    | Pouring → Striking                 | 11 + Interlude II   | Registration attempt, Salee’s communal lens     |\n| 4    | Shaping → Striking                 | 12–13               | Ethical triage decisions, romantic acknowledgment |\n| 5    | Striking → Quench                  | 14–15 + Epilogue    | Major crisis, Stage 2 threshold, quiet commitment, Auditor assessment |\n\nUse this as a sanity check: if a chapter implies more time passing than this allows, fix the references.\n\n---\n# 【4†N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md†file-9HfHWbfzEZfbejJHapEEQn】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file4\nContent Snippet:\n|\n\nIf you adjust events per chapter later, update this table to keep Tides and phases in sync.\n\n---\n\n### 3. SOULPULSE PROGRESSION MAP\n\n#### Jhace’s Stage Over Time\n\n| Tide / Chapters        | Stage State                 | Notes                                         |\n|------------------------|----------------------------|----------------------------------------------|\n| Pre-Tide 0 (off-page)  | Stage 0                    | No Soulpulse, baseline humanity.             |\n| Tide 0 (Ch 3)          | Stage 0 → Stage 1 onset    | First two instinctive uses; tremor appears.  |\n| Tide 1–2 (Ch 4–10)     | Stage 1                    | Tremor intermittent → persistent; vein-lines during use. |\n| Tide 3 (Ch 11–12)      | Stage 1 high / borderline  | Increased frequency/intensity of uses.       |\n| Tide 4–5 (Ch 13–15)    | Approaching Stage 2        | Tremor constant, vein-lines faint at rest, deep exhaustion. |\n\nRules:\n\n- No Stage 2 behavior before ~Ch 12.  \n- No Stage 0 behavior after Ch 4.  \n- Stage references stay descriptive in prose (no numbered “Stage” labels in POV).\n\n---\n\n### 4. J/T RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION MAP\n\n#### Relationship State by Chapter\n\n| ID         | State Label              | Short Description                                           |\n|------------|--------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|\n| Ch 1–2     | Mutual Judgment (Remote) | Each holds negative assumptions based on guild gossip.      |\n| Ch 3       | Forced Proximity         | Both survive same event; competence glimpsed under stress. |\n| Ch 4–5     | Early Respect            | Recognize usefulness of each other’s skills.                |\n| Ch 6       | Cost Witnessed           | Tiffani sees what Soulpulse does to Jhace; critique as care.|\n| Ch 7       | Shared Fear              | Realize they’re both driven by terror of preventable deaths.|\n| Ch 8–9     | Functional Partnership   | Work and argue as a unit; survive a failure together.      |\n| Ch 10      | Integrated Team          | Trust system: Jhace executes, Tiffani optimizes.           |\n| Ch 11      | Political Alignment      | Present united front vs. RRO.                              |\n| Ch I2      | –                       | Salee views them as a pair, even if they haven’t named it. |\n| Ch 12      | Ethical Alignment        | Choose same moral priority (neglected districts).          |\n| Ch 13      | Romantic Acknowledgment  | “More than work partnership” made explicit.                |\n| Ch 14      | Almost-Intimate          | Crisis interrupts; physical closeness undeniable.          |\n| Ch 15      | Committed Pair           | Exhausted but clear: “we keep holding together.”           |\n| Epilogue   | Externally Logged        | Auditor notes partnership as relevant factor.              |\n\nAny chapter where their behavior doesn’t match this state should be revised or the map updated. The map is the source of truth.\n\n---\n\n### 5. MAJOR EVENT ANCHORS\n\nA few events that everything else orbits around:\n\n1. **Piercing (Tide 0, Ch 3)**  \n   - City-wide collapse, thousands dead.  \n   - First Soulpulse activations.  \n   - J/T lives intersect structurally (even if not fully together yet).\n# 【5†N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md†file-6ERDNSoJumE8RYbV3j8rJJ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file5\nContent Snippet:\nDeliberately pass each line of dialogue through that filter before locking the chapter.\n\n---\n\n### 4. Knowledge Gate Breaks (Characters Knowing Too Much)\n\n**Failure:**\n- Jhace internally calls his condition “Stage 2.”\n- Characters refer to “gods,” “seals,” or any series-end machinery.\n- Someone predicts a very specific future event.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Collapses the slow-burn reveal structure of the whole saga.\n- Makes the world feel rigged instead of discovered.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Search for:\n  - “stage 2 / stage three / stage four / advanced stage”\n  - “god / divine / destiny / prophecy / chosen”\n  - Concrete statements about future events (“Tiffani will never die,” etc.)\n- Check if any POV has information they could only have from outside the story.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Replace formal labels with experiential description:\n  - “Stage 2” → “The tremor never fully left now, even when he slept.”\n- Keep existential questions open:\n  - “He didn’t know what this power was, only that each use left more of him behind.”\n- Remove or soften predictions:\n  - “This feels like it will matter” → “He had no idea if any of this would matter later; right now it just hurt.”\n\n---\n\n### 5. Beat Patterning (Predictable Rhythm)\n\n**Failure:**\n- Chapters follow the same emotional structure repeatedly:\n  - e.g., “quiet → disaster → near-death → tidy win” on loop.\n- Or specific beat sequences repeat (1→3, 1→3, 1→3).\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Reader starts predicting the shape subconsciously.\n- Stakes feel fake because nothing *structural* ever surprises.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Label each chapter’s beat type using `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md`.\n- Scan sequence for:\n  - Same beat repeated back-to-back.\n  - Same pair of beats repeated multiple times in the novella.\n  - Extended runs of high-intensity beats without a breather.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Swap beat types where functionally equivalent:\n  - Convert a “Trial→Failure→Compounding→Success/Cost” into a “Crisis Front-Loaded→Rolling Complications→Partial Resolution.”\n- Insert quieter recalibration chapters between major crises.\n- Use Interludes strategically as breathers and reframing devices.\n\n---\n\n### 6. Timeline Drift\n\n**Failure:**\n- Events that should happen on Tide 0 end up implied days later.\n- Characters refer to “days ago” when only a few Tides have passed.\n- Physical recovery times stop matching the established time frame.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Breaks causal logic.\n- Makes N01 feel like a vague mush instead of a sharp sequence.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Use `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md` while revising:\n  - For each chapter, annotate: Tide, phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n- Look for:\n  - Inconsistent references (“weeks later” in a novella that spans ~5 Tides).\n  - Overly fast healing that isn’t flagged as anomalous (only Chloen’s leg is special).\n\n**Fix:**\n- Translate casual time:\n  - “Days later” → “A few Tides later.”\n  - “Weeks later” → “Several Anneals later” (only if supported by timeline skeleton; N01 is tight).\n- Explicitly anchor big beats:\n  - “By the second Tide after the Piercing…” etc.\n\n---\n# 【6†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file6\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n\n**Relationship:** First meeting through competence recognition (she measures, he builds)\n\n**Stage Progression:** 0 → 1 onset (tremor baseline establishing)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 4: \"After the Shudder\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 1, Kindling (morning after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_02 (Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 1\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,350 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace wakes with persistent tremor and pain. Bram reports Chloen's leg healing unexpectedly. Jhace tests power on cracked support without immediate threat—FAILS (no activation). Runner brings emergency: healer's triage wall failing with wounded inside. Immediate threat present—power WORKS but cost increases (vein-lines to elbows, worse tremor). Tiffani observes, asks to see touch points for data collection. Jhace tries to pick up nail—fails three times, tremor jerks it free. Concealment behavior (hands behind back). Tiffani sees everything, documents cost in logbook.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Wakes to baseline tremor (Stage 1 established)\n- Chloen's healing confirmed (side-effect established)\n- Power test on non-critical crack: FAILS (conditional activation shown)\n- Emergency at triage wall: SUCCEEDS (immediate threat required)\n- Cost progression: worse tremor, can't grip nail (functional loss)\n- Tiffani introduction proper: analytical, demands data\n- J/T dynamic: reluctant compliance because her logic is sound\n- Exit: \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 successful use (triage wall), 1 failed attempt (cracked support)\n- Conditional activation confirmed: requires immediate protective need\n- Cost accumulation shown: Stage 1 baseline tremor, fine motor compromised\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani decides to engage deliberately (prevent his destruction through measurement)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline established, functional loss beginning\n\n---\n# 【7†Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md†file-K4V1wDwsx6NHyumotwm7xD】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file7\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### JHACE & CHLOEN – RELUCTANT TEACHER & HERO WORSHIP\n\n* **Origin:** Chloen has been shadowing Jhace for three Anneals.  He refused to take apprentices, but her persistence and careful nature won him over.\n* **N01 arc:**\n  * Ch 1: She’s present for inspections and begins the story as a curious helper.\n  * Ch 3: She is pinned under a beam; Jhace’s first Soulpulse use saves her.  Her crushed leg heals inexplicably fast.\n  * Ch 4: She hero‑worships Jhace even more.  He tries to push her away but can’t bring himself to be cruel.\n  * Ch 8–10: She watches training with wide eyes.  She shows minor Soulpulse aptitude in later novellas but in N01 she’s just learning.\n* **Dynamic:** She is the possibility that his knowledge will outlast him.  He is uncomfortable with being a hero to someone; she normalizes his need to teach.\n\n---\n\n### SUPPORTING RELATIONSHIPS\n\n* **Jhace & Kylar:** Kylar is the cautionary tale.  He fails spectacularly, injuring no one but scaring everyone, and learns humility.  Their relationship is teacher/student based on mistakes.\n* **Tiffani & Salee:** Mutual respect between a systems analyst and a grassroots organizer.  Tiffani sees the beauty in Salee’s human networks.  Salee admires Tiffani’s precision and how it serves people rather than rules.\n* **Jhace & Corlexi (minor):** Conflict built on philosophy.  Corlexi represents systematic control; Jhace refuses institutional handcuffs.  They clash politely but intensely.\n\n---\n\n### RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION BY CHAPTER\n\n| Chapter | Jhace & Tiffani | Jhace & Bram | Tiffani & Pella | Jhace & Chloen | Notes |\n|--------|-----------------|--------------|-----------------|----------------|-------|\n| **Ch 1** | — (haven’t met) | Bram teases, Jhace grunts | Apprentice cares; Tiffani annoyed | Apprentice work; minimal contact | Setup: mutual misconceptions |\n| **Ch 2** | — | — | Pella nags Tiffani to eat | — | Tiffani life shown |\n| **Ch 3** | First sight (crisis) | Bram helps Jhace respond | Pella escapes observatory with Tiffani | Chloen pinned and saved; leg heals | First contact; souls align without knowing |\n| **Ch 4** | Respect forms (analysis vs. brute force) | Bram warns about cost; Jhace hides tremor | Pella assists with salvage | Chloen hero worship grows | Learning to trust instincts and analysis |\n| **Ch 5** | Tiffani commits to study Soulpulse | Bram amused by J/T dynamic | Pella supporting analysis | — | Professional curiosity deepens |\n| **Ch 6** | First integrated rescue; critique becomes collaboration | Bram sees partnership forming | Pella absent | — | Respect moves toward partnership |\n| **Interlude I** | — | — | — | — | Corlexi view; seeds conflict |\n| **Ch 7** | Training conflict begins | Bram listens but doesn’t intervene | Pella observes | — | Methodological disagreements start |\n| **Ch 8** | Kylar fails; J/T fight about teaching | Bram warns about pushing too hard | Pella present | — | Stress test for partnership |\n| **Ch 9** | Asking Kylar his needs;\n# 【8†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file8\nContent Snippet:\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n- Ch 1-2: Stage 0 (pre-Piercing)\n- Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset\n- Ch 4-9: Stage 1 baseline\n- Ch 10: Approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs)\n- Ch 11-12: Stage 1 stabilized with management\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION TRACKING\n# 【9†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file9\nContent Snippet:\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Jhace's craft mastery and pre-existing tremor\n- Establish 23 names burden (guilt from previous failure)\n- Show isolated working style\n- Foreshadow structural problems\n- Bram as mentor/caretaker figure\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,400 words  \n**POV:** Tiffani (tight third-person limited)  \n**Tide:** 0, Pouring  \n**Setting:** Observatory platform, Saint Merro rim\n\nTiffani measures the sky as if measurement can keep it honest. On an observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim, she tracks distortions and pressure shifts with obsessive vigilance. Pella—grounded, practical—keeps her fed, warm, and tethered to the present without insulting her fear. Tiffani's trauma surfaces as ritual: re-check, re-check, re-check. The air's refraction bends in ways her instruments can't comfortably name, and the horizon looks too thin. As the Tide moves toward Pouring, the sky's line fractures—first as a subtle wrongness, then as an event. Tiffani catches the first usable data of the world changing and realizes the numbers are not just \"bad.\" They're unprecedented. The exit begins.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Tiffani's measurement expertise\n- Establish 23 deaths trauma (parallel to Jhace)\n- Show Pella as support network\n- Foreshadow Piercing exit\n- Measurement as coping mechanism\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 3: \"The Sky Breaks\"\n**Word Target:** ~2,200 words  \n**POV:** Alternating Jhace/Tiffani  \n**Tide:** 0, Shaping (Piercing exit moment)  \n**Setting:** Underface tunnel, observatory platform, street-level chaos\n\nCatastrophe arrives mid-breath. In the Underface tunnel, a wall moves before Jhace can decide to move; the city becomes weight without permission. Chloen is trapped. Jhace's old ledger of names—those he couldn't hold—spikes through him as panic. He braces a beam with his body, then something answers the desperation: copper-gold glow floods his palms and runs into the structure like heat finding a seam. The beam holds—barely. He uses it again at street level, pressing both hands to a failing support while the building's stress-lines reveal themselves like veins of light. The cost hits hard: tremor intensification, pain, heat-lash up his arms, collapse to his knees.\n\nAt the observatory, Tiffani watches the exit happen—a hole appearing in reality. Glass shatters around her. She presses palms to a fracturing window pane and copper-gold light responds, threading through the cracks, holding them stable long enough for evacuation. The cost is immediate: tremor in both hands, heat burning up her forearms.\n\nIn the chaos, they meet for the first time—not as comfort, but as demand: she needs to know what happened, how, and what it cost. Their first exchange is competence colliding with exhaustion.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Piercing exit catastrophe\n- Jhace's first Soulpulse use (saves Chloen)\n- Tiffani's first Soulpulse use (stabilizes glass)\n- Visible costs establish baseline (tremor, heat, pain)\n- First meeting: competence-driven, not romantic\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 4: \"After the Shudder\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,500 words  \n**POV:** Jhace  \n**Tide:** 0, Striking (hours after Piercing)  \n**Setting:** Collapse sites, street-level triage\n# 【10†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file10\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 PROSE GENERATION WORKFLOW\n\n**Purpose:**  \nDefine a repeatable, enforceable process for generating and revising N01 prose so that:\n\n- All output stays within N01 scope.  \n- World, characters, and magic stay consistent.  \n- Cost, lexicon, and beat-structure rules are respected without a human babysitting every line.\n\nThis workflow assumes access to:\n\n- `N01_Master_Bible.md`\n- `World_Core.md`\n- `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`\n- `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`\n- `Location_Bibles_N01.md`\n- `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`\n- `Constraint_Systems.md`\n- `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`\n\n---\n\n### 1. WORK MODES\n\nThe engine operates in one of four modes:\n\n1. **Chapter Draft Mode**  \n   Generate a full prose draft for a numbered chapter (1–15) with a specified POV.\n\n2. **Chapter Revision Mode**  \n   Revise an existing chapter while preserving its beat, POV, and factual content.\n\n3. **Interlude Mode**  \n   Generate or revise Interlude I or II.\n\n4. **Epilogue Mode**  \n   Generate or revise the N01 Epilogue.\n\nEach mode uses the same constraints but slightly different steps.\n\n---\n\n### 2. COMMON SETUP (ALL MODES)\n\nBefore writing or revising anything:\n\n1. **Identify Assignment**\n   - Chapter number or Interlude/Epilogue.\n   - POV character.\n   - Target word count window (e.g., 1,000–1,200 for standard chapters).\n\n2. **Load Core Context**\n   - From `N01_Master_Bible.md`:\n     - Novella overview.\n     - Primary characters.\n     - Relationship dynamics.\n     - Constraints for N01 prose.\n   - From `World_Core.md`:\n     - Time system (Tides/Anneals/Phases).\n     - Lexicon constraints.\n     - Saint Merro districts.\n   - From `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\n     - Stage definitions.\n     - Known techniques.\n     - Cost patterns.\n   - From `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`:\n     - POV character psychology, voice, humor.\n   - From `Location_Bibles_N01.md`:\n     - Primary locations for the chapter’s setting(s).\n   - From `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`:\n     - Relationship state at this point in the novella.\n   - From `Constraint_Systems.md`:\n     - Lexicon rules.\n     - Cost visibility rules.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat constraints.\n\n3. **Consult Chapter Grid**\n   - From `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`:\n     - Chapter title.\n     - POV.\n     - Beat method ID (BEAT_0X).\n     - Tide and phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n     - Location(s).\n     - External conflict.\n     - Internal shift.\n     - Relationship beat.\n     - Soulpulse usage (Y/N, type, approximate cost).\n     - Exit hook.\n     - Knowledge state changes.\n\n4. **Derive a One-Page Brief (Internal)**\n   For the current chapter, summarize in your own internal notes:\n   - **Situation When Chapter Opens.**\n   - **Main external problem.**\n   - **What changes internally for the POV by the end.**\n   - **What, if anything, changes in relationships.**\n   - **Whether Soulpulse is used, and at what cost.**\n   - **What the reader must know by the end (and what must stay hidden).**\n   - **Beat method and emotional arc you must follow.**\n# 【11†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file11\nContent Snippet:\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Jhace aware of Tiffani by reputation only; judges her as rigid perfectionist\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Pouring (mid-morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,280 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani measures atmospheric distortion from her observatory, detecting the incoming Piercing object through theodolite readings. She has been awake for days, driven by perfectionism rooted in Marisse's death. Pella brings food and scolds her for not resting. Measurements show descent trajectory—object is not passing over, it's coming down. First tremor hits. White line appears in sky. Tiffani and Pella evacuate as roar begins.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Mirrors Ch 1 structure (parallel observation)\n- Establishes Tiffani's precision methodology\n- Pella introduces care/support dynamic\n- Marisse trauma implied through vigilance pattern\n- Atmospheric distortion measurements (first scientific data)\n- Exit as sky splits\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani aware of Jhace by reputation only; judges him as overextending ego-driven builder\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 3: \"The Sky Breaks\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Striking (Piercing event)\n- **Setting:** Underface tunnel, then street level at building support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Word Count:** 2,200 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n# (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file13\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Corlexi reviews stabilization reports, sees Order taking shape through RRO formation. She feels satisfaction at frameworks solidifying but also unease—something escapes her control. The smith and glassworker refuse to be contained, building third path outside her geometry. Second segment: She experiences her Principle (Form) as compulsion to impose structure, feels nascent god-awareness stirring (vast power constrained by single lens). She makes note: must address the anomaly in Saint Merro. Closing: Auditor observes Corlexi's awakening, notes Form's limitation—cannot tolerate things outside patterns.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm technique (god-vessel perception)\n- Principle bias manifestation (everything seen through Form lens)\n- Innocent frame maintained (no recognition of divinity)\n- Jhace/Tiffani recognized as structural threat\n- Auditor frame at open/close\n\n**Purpose:** Establish divine forces moving, show limitation of single-Principle perspective, foreshadow institutional pressure in Act III\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 8: \"Proof and Resistance\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 7-8\n- **Setting:** Jhace's forge, then joint inspection of hold sites\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_12 (Relationship Shift → Power Dynamic Change)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani arrives at forge with compiled data, shows Jhace his cost curve projection. He resists—doesn't want to see himself as failing, doesn't trust numbers over experience. She challenges: \"You trust stone to tell truth. These marks are stone's truth about what you're doing to yourself.\" Tension: his defensive walls vs. her refusal to let him self-destruct through ignorance. She proposes operational change: she identifies critical points, he applies minimal force, they test if efficiency reduces cost. He agrees grudgingly—not because he believes her, but because her logic is sound even if he hates it. They test method on controlled scenario. It works. Cost is measurably lower. His recognition: accepting her help isn't weakness, it's competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Tiffani presents evidence (his cost curve)\n- Jhace's defensive resistance (control/autonomy threatened)\n- Her cutting through: stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks\n- Tension: care vs. pride\n- Proposal: test efficiency method\n- Controlled test succeeds\n- His grudging acceptance: she makes him better\n- Exit: agreement to coordinate on next real crisis\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 controlled test (reduced cost confirmed with her targeting)\n\n**Relationship:** Resistance → acceptance that partnership improves both\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 stable, cost reduction possible with coordination\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 10-12\n- **Setting:** Glass Quarter refugee camp, Crown administrative contact\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,700 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nCorvin (Mason faction) and Tellis (Water Guild) arrive at Glass Quarter offering \"help\" in exchange for political alignment. Tiffani calculates: resources needed, cost is autonomy. Logic says accept. But she checks variable: Jhace will see this as betrayal, and his trust affects coordination. Deeper: she wants his trust (not logical, but real).\n# 【14†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file14\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Assessment of characters: Jhace unpredictable because care is absolute, Tiffani unpredictable because precision is absolute, both useful because they might survive what's coming. Interest in their bond: they love functionally (load-bearing systems), not romantically. Remove one, other fails. This is useful—when gods move and alliances fracture, bond will save or destroy them. Either way, worth observing. Decision: flag them as anomaly, predict system will move to crush or co-opt. Notes that Turning 1's hope phase shading toward devastation, their bond potential fulcrum or liability. If love makes people break rules, it reshapes wars—or ruins plans. Sets stage for N02 escalation.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n# 【15†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file15\nContent Snippet:\nEvery scar holds memory. They discuss larger currents: registries spreading, clinics multiplying, RRO expanding, black-market training proliferating. They know storm is forming beyond their neighborhood. What they can control: what they hold. At forge, they write three priorities on wall: (1) Truth over comfort, (2) People over institutions, (3) Keep each other honest about why we act. Vow without ceremony. Community members add their marks beside it—informal pact, local mandate. Not oath to Crown or RRO, but commitment to third path. Jhace's hand shakes as he writes; Tiffani laces their fingers, lets his tremor move through both. Physical reminder: cost and commitment shared. Exit: they face uncertain future together, foundation locked.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Walk through rebuilt district (uneven success)\n- Recognition of larger forces gathering\n- Decision: focus on what they can hold\n- Three priorities written on forge wall\n- Community witnesses/joins commitment\n- Shared physical moment (tremor through linked hands)\n- Acceptance: this is who they are together\n- Exit: foundation locked, future uncertain but faced together\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (commitment/resolution chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Partnership formalized through shared principles (not romance, but deeper)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline accepted as new permanent state\n\n---\n\n### **EPILOGUE: \"Auditor Observation\"**\n- **POV:** Auditor (meta-perspective outside time)\n- **Setting:** Auditor's observation point (undefined location)\n- **Word Count Target:** 800-1,000 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Assessment of characters: Jhace unpredictable because care is absolute, Tiffani unpredictable because precision is absolute, both useful because they might survive what's coming. Interest in their bond: they love functionally (load-bearing systems), not romantically. Remove one, other fails. This is useful—when gods move and alliances fracture, bond will save or destroy them. Either way, worth observing. Decision: flag them as anomaly, predict system will move to crush or co-opt. Notes that Turning 1's hope phase shading toward devastation, their bond potential fulcrum or liability. If love makes people break rules, it reshapes wars—or ruins plans. Sets stage for N02 escalation.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n# 【16†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file16\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Assessment of characters: Jhace unpredictable because care is absolute, Tiffani unpredictable because precision is absolute, both useful because they might survive what's coming. Interest in their bond: they love functionally (load-bearing systems), not romantically. Remove one, other fails. This is useful—when gods move and alliances fracture, bond will save or destroy them. Either way, worth observing. Decision: flag them as anomaly, predict system will move to crush or co-opt. Notes that Turning 1's hope phase shading toward devastation, their bond potential fulcrum or liability. If love makes people break rules, it reshapes wars—or ruins plans. Sets stage for N02 escalation.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n# 【17†Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md†file-K4V1wDwsx6NHyumotwm7xD】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file17\nContent Snippet:\n* **Ch 1–6:** Bram teases Jhace for being obsessive (“You’re going to die down here checking seams”), and he tells jokes about resting being rehearsal for death.  He tries to get Jhace to slow down.\n* **Ch 7–11:** Bram watches Jhace trust Tiffani.  He encourages the partnership and notes its importance.  He also warns both of them about codependency.\n* **Ch 14–15:** Bram is present during the big crisis.  He sees the toll on Jhace and Tiffani and continues to offer humor and support.  He continues to worry they’ll burn out together.  He respects their choice but keeps showing up to keep them tethered.\n\n---\n\n### TIFFANI & PELLA – MENTOR & STUDENT\n\n* **Origin:** Pella was assigned to Tiffani as an apprentice.  Warned Tiffani was impossible, she discovered the opposite: Tiffani is demanding but patient, and she explains the reasons behind precision.\n* **Nurture:** Pella brings Tiffani food and nags about rest.  Tiffani allows this nagging because she recognizes it as care.  Pella picks up Tiffani’s measurement habits and learns to ask good questions.\n* **Scenes:** Ch 2 (Pella feeds Tiffani), Ch 5 (assisting with analysis), Ch 9–10 (present in training discussions), Ch 14–15 (behind the scenes worrying about Tiffani’s exhaustion).\n* **Outcome:** Pella becomes the next generation of pattern readers.  She survives this story and picks up Tiffani’s observational work after N01.\n\n---\n\n### JHACE & CHLOEN – RELUCTANT TEACHER & HERO WORSHIP\n\n* **Origin:** Chloen has been shadowing Jhace for three Anneals.  He refused to take apprentices, but her persistence and careful nature won him over.\n* **N01 arc:**\n  * Ch 1: She’s present for inspections and begins the story as a curious helper.\n  * Ch 3: She is pinned under a beam; Jhace’s first Soulpulse use saves her.  Her crushed leg heals inexplicably fast.\n  * Ch 4: She hero‑worships Jhace even more.  He tries to push her away but can’t bring himself to be cruel.\n  * Ch 8–10: She watches training with wide eyes.  She shows minor Soulpulse aptitude in later novellas but in N01 she’s just learning.\n* **Dynamic:** She is the possibility that his knowledge will outlast him.  He is uncomfortable with being a hero to someone; she normalizes his need to teach.\n\n---\n\n### SUPPORTING RELATIONSHIPS\n\n* **Jhace & Kylar:** Kylar is the cautionary tale.  He fails spectacularly, injuring no one but scaring everyone, and learns humility.  Their relationship is teacher/student based on mistakes.\n* **Tiffani & Salee:** Mutual respect between a systems analyst and a grassroots organizer.  Tiffani sees the beauty in Salee’s human networks.  Salee admires Tiffani’s precision and how it serves people rather than rules.\n* **Jhace & Corlexi (minor):** Conflict built on philosophy.  Corlexi represents systematic control; Jhace refuses institutional handcuffs.  They clash politely but intensely.\n\n---\n\n### RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION BY CHAPTER\n# 【18†Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md†file-XguHey3FbLfoV7Zzuy7eRk】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file18\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### INTERACTION WITH MATERIALS\n\n**Most Responsive:**\n- Metal (especially iron, copper, steel)\n- Stone (structural grades)\n- Wood (structural timber)\n\n**Less Responsive:**\n- Glass (brittle, resists holding)\n- Ceramics (similar to glass)\n- Composites (mixed materials confuse effect)\n\n**Living Tissue:**\n- Responds but unpredictably\n- Healing observed but mechanism unknown\n- Risk of unintended effects\n- Not explored systematically in N01\n\n---\n\n### LIMITATIONS DISCOVERED IN N01\n\n**Cannot:**\n- Affect material without direct contact\n- Maintain effect after breaking contact\n- Hold indefinitely without cost\n- Use casually or frivolously\n- Teach effectively to others\n- Predict exact cost before use\n- Activate on command without emotional engagement\n\n**Can:**\n- Hold structures temporarily\n- Redistribute load through material\n- Sense stress patterns in contacted material\n- Reduce immediate collapse risk\n- Buy time for evacuations\n- Accelerate healing (observed once, not understood)\n\n---\n\n### COST VISIBILITY: REQUIRED DESCRIPTIONS\n\n**Every Soulpulse use in prose must show:**\n\n**During Use:**\n- Copper‑gold glow described (brightness, spread pattern)\n- Physical strain on user (tremor, tension, vibration, breathing)\n- Material response (shimmer, stress redistribution)\n- Duration (in spans, breaths, or contextual time)\n\n**Immediately After:**\n- Tremor status (worsened, maintained, slight recovery)\n- Vein‑line visibility (bright, fading, persistent)\n- Exhaustion level (mild fatigue to near‑collapse)\n- Pain description (location, intensity, duration)\n\n**Long‑Term Tracking:**\n- Stage maintenance or progression\n- Cumulative cost across chapter\n- Recovery time required\n- Impact on daily function\n\n---\n\n### WHAT REMAINS UNKNOWN IN N01\n\n**Unanswered Questions:**\n- What causes Soulpulse capacity?\n- Why did it emerge post‑Piercing?\n- Are there theoretical limits?\n- Can capacity grow with practice?\n- What happens at Stage 3 or beyond?\n- Is cost damage permanent?\n- Can multiple users coordinate effects?\n- What is the actual mechanism?\n\n**These questions are not answered in N01.** Characters operate on empirical observation and educated guessing. Systematic understanding comes later.\n\n---\n\n**SCOPE NOTE:** This document reflects only what's discovered through direct observation and experimentation during N01. Later novellas will expand understanding of dual‑point mechanics, exponential costs, medical applications, and advanced techniques. For N01 prose generation, limit Soulpulse capabilities to what's described here.\n# 【19†Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md†file-XguHey3FbLfoV7Zzuy7eRk】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn2file19\nContent Snippet:\n## SOULPULSE: MECHANICS AS DISCOVERED IN N01\n\n**Purpose:** This document describes Soulpulse as it's understood and experienced during N01 \"The Grip That Holds.\" This is empirical knowledge built from observation and experimentation, NOT complete system understanding.\n\n---\n\n### WHAT IS SOULPULSE?\n\n**Observed Phenomenon:**\nFollowing the Piercing, certain individuals discovered they could affect physical reality through focused intention combined with direct contact. This wasn't magic in any traditional sense—no incantations, no reagents, no mystical gestures.\n\n**Common Element:**\nProtective intention crystallized through physical contact. The user must desperately need the material to hold/mend/stabilize AND must be touching it.\n\n---\n\n### ACTIVATION REQUIREMENTS (As Understood in N01)\n\n**Physical Contact:**\n- Requires skin-to-material contact (stone, metal, flesh, wood)\n- Works through thin materials (gloves reduce effectiveness)\n- Must maintain contact during active use\n- Breaking contact ends effect immediately\n\n**Emotional State:**\n- Demands genuine protective need\n- “I need this to hold so people survive” works\n- Casual desire or intellectual will alone doesn't work\n- Fear for others' lives seems to enhance activation\n- Anger or self-preservation less effective\n\n**Awareness:**\n- First activations always instinctive/desperate\n- Conscious activation requires practice\n- User must \"feel\" the material's stress/structure\n- Cannot activate if distracted or panicked\n\n---\n\n### VISUAL MANIFESTATION\n\n**Copper‑Gold Glow:**\n- Distinctive color, visible to observers\n- Spreads from point of contact into material\n- Intensity varies with effort/effect\n- Brighter during active use, dimmer in residue\n\n**Vein‑Lines:**\n- Tracery appears on user's forearms\n- Follows vein patterns but extends beyond normal anatomy\n- Faint when inactive, bright when active\n- Becomes more persistent with repeated use\n- First appears after 2–3 uses\n\n**Material Changes:**\n- Metal/stone shows faint shimmer during hold\n- Stress patterns visible as light/dark areas\n- Effect persists briefly after contact broken (seconds to spans)\n- Leaves subtle changes in material structure\n\n---\n\n### TACTILE EXPERIENCE (User Perspective)\n\n**During Activation:**\n- Intense pressure in palms (like pushing against resistance)\n- Vibration extending from hands through arms into chest\n- Sensation of being \"connected\" to material\n- Can feel stress points, structural integrity, weakness\n- Overwhelming when first experienced\n\n**Physical Sensation:**\n- Warmth spreading from palms\n- Muscle tension throughout arms and shoulders\n- Bone‑deep ache develops during sustained use\n- Breath becomes labored under heavy loads\n- Nausea if pushing too hard\n\n---\n\n### COST SYSTEM: STAGE PROGRESSION (N01 Scope)\n\nSoulpulse use carries a physical cost that escalates with frequency and intensity. Characters in N01 do not explicitly label stages; this section labels them for author reference.\n\n**Stage 0: Baseline (Pre‑Use)**\n- No tremor\n- Normal physical function\n- No visible vein‑lines\n- No chronic symptoms\n\n**Stage 1: Minor Tremor (After 2–3 Uses)**\n- Slight tremor in hands when attempting fine motor tasks\n- Noticeable when picking up small objects, detailed work\n- Fades partially after several spans of rest\n- Vein‑lines appear during use, fade when inactive\n- User can still function normally in non‑precision tasks\n- Most users don't progress past this if they use sparingly\nVisible: 0% - 100%",
          "summary": null,
          "tether_id": null
        },

        "24328e49-c3ca-4f02-ba44-64b20e80839a"
      ],
      "id": "c2288b86-0bf6-4283-99af-aa067fbb6f6c",
      "message": {
        "author": {

        "content": {
          "content_type": "multimodal_text",
          "parts": [
            "Make sure to include fileciteturn0file10 in your response to cite this file, or to surface it as a link.",
            "## LOCATION REFERENCE: N01 SCOPE\n\n**Purpose:** Physical descriptions, functional details, and atmospheric notes for all locations appearing in N01 \"The Grip That Holds.\"  Each section lays out what the characters and reader know about these places during the first novella and avoids any forward‑looking spoilers.\n\n---\n\n### UNDERFACE HEAT‑GRID TUNNELS\n\n**Purpose:** Maintenance access for the city’s heating infrastructure.\n\n**Physical Layout:**\n\n* A network of narrow passages beneath the working‑class districts of Saint Merro.\n* Copper pipes of varying diameters carry geothermal heat; smaller lines (thimble‑width) snake over large main lines (pole‑width).\n* Vertical risers connect deeper levels via ladders; some spaces require squeezing through tight shaft openings.\n* The tunnels are single file in most places, with slightly wider junctions for maintenance crews and tool caches.\n* Multiple access points: surface grates, doors at forge‑level basements, emergency exits into the Dregs and Underface districts.\n\n**Environmental:**\n\n* Heat: Temperature rises with depth – warm near the surface, becoming uncomfortable near deep mains.\n* Humidity: Condensation drips down walls and pipes; occasional puddles gather on the stone floor.\n* Sound: Constant low rumble from heat flow, clangs as pipes shift; conversations echo unpredictably and aren’t private.\n* Light: Limited to carried lamps or fixed lanterns at junctions; shadows dance on curved walls.\n\n**Signature Visuals:**\n\n* Copper pipes shimmering with heat distortion.\n* Chalk marks noting maintenance or structural warnings.\n* Tool racks (wrenches, hammers, spare flanges) at intersections.\n* Steam vents hissing gently along some runs.\n\n**Functional Details:**\n\n* The tunnels supply heat to homes, guild halls and industrial sites. Failures can mean freezing residents or catastrophic pressure releases.\n* Jhace’s inspection routes run through these tunnels; he feels for vibration changes and listens for subtle hum shifts to detect problems.\n* Post‑Piercing, the heat‑grid is under unusual strain – more leaks, more pressure spikes, more points of imminent failure.\n\n**Typical Scenes:**\n\n* Chapter 1 opens here with Jhace inspecting the grid and feeling something wrong.\n* Chapter 3 shows the tunnels rattling and rumbling during the Piercing.\n* Subsequent chapters (4, 6, 12) use them as shelters, shortcuts and sites of quiet reflection or crisis navigation.\n\n**Travel Time:**\n\n* From street level to inspection depth: a few spans of climbing/descending, depending on access point.\n* Cross‑district travel underground can be faster than surface routes during crises but is disorienting without experience.\n\n---\n\n### SAINT MERRO DISTRICT OVERVIEW\n\nSaint Merro is built in terraced layers up a hillside basin.  Elevation correlates roughly to status and resources.  What follows is what characters know in N01.\n\n#### The Dregs (River Level)\n\n* **Physical:** Oldest constructions, precarious buildings, narrow streets.  The river floods occasionally; many structures are warped or sinking.\n* **Environment:** Smoke, refuse and tannery smells; constant noise from industry; limited light due to tall, leaning buildings.\n* **Social:** Poor workers, refugees, marginalized communities.  Salee’s networks operate here.  Strained by post‑Piercing influxes of displaced people.\n* **Typical scenes:** Communal meals and resource pooling (Interlude II), Jhace and Tiffani choosing to help here over more “important” districts (Ch 12).\n\n#### Underface Districts (Mid‑Level)\n\n* **Physical:** Working‑class residential and commercial zones built partially into the hillside.  Mix of stone and timber.  Heat‑grid tunnels run below.\n* **Environment:** Smells of forge smoke, food cooking, humanity.  Streets are narrower than the Crown but wider than the Dregs.\n* **Social:** Guild workers and craftspeople.  Jhace’s forge and training rig are here.  Authority weakens compared to the Crown; guild disputes common.\n* **Typical scenes:** Standard work (Ch 1), training attempts (Ch 7–10), coordination during crises.\n\n#### Glass Quarter (Upper‑Mid Level)\n\n* **Physical:** District specializing in precision craft.  Better construction quality than lower levels.  The Merrow Glass & Mirrors shop and observatory sit here.\n* **Environment:** Cleaner air\nThe file is too long and its contents have been truncated.\n"
          ]
        },
