          "result": "# 【0†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file0\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Mobel leads cleansing ritual where early resonants prove themselves through enduring pain without using Soulpulse. She feels power of her Principle—clarity, judgment, separation of righteous from corrupt. Second segment: Moment of god-awareness—she senses Form-aligned power across planet (Corlexi stirring). Recognition: there are others like her, alliance or war inevitable. Principle limitation shown: sees only absolutes, cannot tolerate ambiguity. Auditor observes: Purity will clash with Form's institutional approach, both will target Jhace/Tiffani's third path.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm (pain as purification)\n- Purity Principle bias (absolute judgment)\n- First inter-god awareness\n- Innocent frame (no recognition of divinity)\n- Foreshadows ideological warfare\n- Auditor frame\n\n**Purpose:** Establish second divine force, show Principles in conflict, set up multi-front pressure on protagonists\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"**\n- **POV:** Both (intimate alternating perspective)\n- **Tide:** 17-18\n- **Setting:** Quiet space after crisis (Jhace's forge or Tiffani's workshop)\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nPost-Ch10 processing. Jhace's rage has cooled to exhaustion. Tiffani's certainty has cracked to fear—she nearly watched him die. Neither apologizes (both were right, both were wrong). Instead: vulnerability exchange. Jhace tells her about the building collapse that killed his parents, the 23 names he carries, why he can't choose who to save (it's choosing who dies). Tiffani tells him about Marisse, the miscalculation, why precision is penance not preference. Recognition: they're both trying to atone for deaths they couldn't prevent. She says: \"I need you to survive this.\" He says: \"I need you to keep stopping me.\" Not love confession—operational truth. They are load-bearing for each other. Moment of physical intimacy (forehead touch, hand hold—first deliberate contact not in crisis). Commitment without words.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Post-crisis exhaustion (defenses down)\n- Trauma exchange: his 23 names, her Marisse\n- Recognition: both atoning for unprevented deaths\n- Mutual need acknowledged (operational, not romantic)\n- Physical intimacy: first non-crisis contact\n- Commitment: she keeps him honest, he trusts her override\n- Exit: understanding they can't survive apart\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (recovery/intimacy chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Trauma-bonding → mutual recognition of load-bearing function\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 recovering with rest, but baseline effects permanent\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"**\n- **POV:** Both (balanced perspective)\n- **Tide:** 19-20\n- **Setting:** Rebuilt overlook above Saint Merro, forge wall\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nWeeks after immediate crisis. Jhace and Tiffani walk their district at Quench, seeing uneven results: some structures beautifully reinforced, others held by temporary measures, some families back in homes, others permanently displaced.\n# 【1†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file1\nContent Snippet:\n### **CHAPTER 6: \"Weight That Won't Yield\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 3-4\n- **Setting:** Major structural failure at Saint Merro junction\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Sustained Pressure → Narrow Success)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium-High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nMulti-story arch threatening collapse. Jhace attempts traditional propping—insufficient. Tiffani arrives with survey data, sketches stress lines on stone (showing him geometry of what he feels). They coordinate without planning: she marks three critical points, he focuses Soulpulse on those exact locations. First true choreographed hold. Success but greater cost: tremor worsens, vein-lines persist longer. Tiffani critiques his \"inefficient\" force application—her precision could reduce his cost by 30%. Jhace's reaction: (1) she's critiquing me, (2) she's right, (3) she sees my inefficiency and wants to fix it. Partnership recognition through competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis opening: arch failing, people still inside\n- Jhace's traditional methods insufficient\n- Tiffani arrives with data/geometry\n- First coordinated Soulpulse (she marks, he powers)\n- Success with visible cost increase\n- Her critique of his technique (care disguised as analysis)\n- His recognition: she sees him, not his heroism\n- Exit: they've choreographed perfectly without planning\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 coordinated use (arch stabilization)\n- Demonstrates improved targeting reduces cost (her geometry + his power)\n- Still significant cost: tremor persistent, vein-lines linger\n\n**Relationship:** First true partnership action—coordination through shared competence\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 progressing, cost accumulation visible\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 7: \"Quiet Calculations\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 5-6\n- **Setting:** Tiffani's workshop/planning space\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,500 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani compiles data from multiple hold sites, building predictive model for failure patterns. She discovers Jhace's cost curve is exponential not linear—he's approaching breaking point faster than he realizes. Pella asks why she cares so much about \"the man with the glow.\" Tiffani's answer: precision requires complete data, and he's destroying the most interesting variable. But underneath: she's starting to see him as person, not just phenomenon. She calculates how to approach him with data he'll actually hear (not orders, not concern, but operational logic). Ends with her walking toward his forge with documented proof he needs to see.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Data compilation and pattern analysis\n- Discovery: cost curve is exponential (crisis point approaching)\n- Pella questions her investment\n- Tiffani's rationalization vs. actual feeling\n- Strategic planning: how to make him listen\n- Decision to approach with evidence\n- Exit: walking toward forge with logbook\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (analysis chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani recognizing care underneath her analytical framework\n\n**Stage Progression:** External calculation of his trajectory toward Stage 2\n\n---\n# 【2†N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md†file-CAd4CywpTdBkiKM88Yt3Ti】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file2\nContent Snippet:\n| Political confrontation                                    |\n| I2 | Dregs communal space                    | Community organizing lens                                  |\n| 12 | Neglected lower districts               | Moral decision about where help goes first                 |\n| 13 | Quiet, semi-private space (forge/back room/roof) | Emotional decompression & acknowledgment            |\n| 14 | Edge of multiple districts (load-bearing node) | High-stakes collapse prevention                       |\n| 15 | Quiet private space (rooftop/back room) | Commitment scene                                           |\n| E  | Unlocated abstraction                   | Auditor report space                                       |\n\n---\n\n### 4. TIDE-LEVEL TIMELINE SNAPSHOT\n\nApproximate; exact phase mapping lives in `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`.\n\n| Tide | Phase(s) Primarily Covered          | Chapters            | Key Events                                      |\n|------|-------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------|\n| 0    | Late Kindling → Striking/Quench    | 1–3                 | Pre-Piercing baseline → The Piercing → first uses |\n| 1    | Kindling → Striking                | 4–6 + Interlude I   | Immediate aftermath, first big hold, institutional view |\n| 2    | Kindling → Quench                  | 7–10                | Training loop, failures, synthesis, mature rescue |\n| 3    | Pouring → Striking                 | 11 + Interlude II   | Registration attempt, Salee’s communal lens     |\n| 4    | Shaping → Striking                 | 12–13               | Ethical triage decisions, romantic acknowledgment |\n| 5    | Striking → Quench                  | 14–15 + Epilogue    | Major crisis, Stage 2 threshold, quiet commitment, Auditor assessment |\n\nUse this as a sanity check: if a chapter implies more time passing than this allows, fix the references.\n\n---\n# 【3†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file3\nContent Snippet:\n# N01 - THE GRIP THAT HOLDS\n**Prose Generation Bible**\n\n## NOVELLA STRUCTURE\n\n- 12 chapters (1,200-2,200 words each)\n- 2 interludes (600-900 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,000 words)\n- Timeline: Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20\n- Setting: Saint Merro, Te'Oga's Crown\n\n---\n\n## TIMELINE\n\n**Tide 0:** Piercing event (Chapter 3)  \n**Tides 1-5:** Immediate aftermath, first Soulpulse uses  \n**Tides 6-10:** Partnership formation, coordinated abilities  \n**Tides 11-15:** Political pressure escalates  \n**Tides 16-20:** Commitment solidifies, future planning\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER SUMMARIES\n\n### CHAPTER 1: \"Something Wrong\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,240 words  \n**POV:** Jhace (tight third-person limited)  \n**Tide:** 0, Kindling (morning of Piercing entry)  \n**Setting:** Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection tunnel\n\nJhace works the Underface by feel—load, hum, stress—catching a wrong note in stone and glass that refuses to resolve. Bram needles him for working alone, but the teasing sits on an older truth: Jhace trusts structures more than people. Above, the city's supports feel tired; below, water and grit talk too loudly. The Tide's light shifts toward Kindling's end and the Underface grows tense—workers rushing, voices sharpened. Jhace marks cracks, makes tiny repairs that should be enough, then finds that \"enough\" won't hold. A tremor rolls through, not the worst, but wrong in rhythm. He climbs toward street level, trying to place the pattern in his mind. The chapter ends with him hearing the city like a living thing—holding its breath.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Jhace's craft mastery and pre-existing tremor\n- Establish 23 names burden (guilt from previous failure)\n- Show isolated working style\n- Foreshadow structural problems\n- Bram as mentor/caretaker figure\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,400 words  \n**POV:** Tiffani (tight third-person limited)  \n**Tide:** 0, Pouring  \n**Setting:** Observatory platform, Saint Merro rim\n\nTiffani measures the sky as if measurement can keep it honest. On an observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim, she tracks distortions and pressure shifts with obsessive vigilance. Pella—grounded, practical—keeps her fed, warm, and tethered to the present without insulting her fear. Tiffani's trauma surfaces as ritual: re-check, re-check, re-check. The air's refraction bends in ways her instruments can't comfortably name, and the horizon looks too thin. As the Tide moves toward Pouring, the sky's line fractures—first as a subtle wrongness, then as an event. Tiffani catches the first usable data of the world changing and realizes the numbers are not just \"bad.\" They're unprecedented. The exit begins.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Tiffani's measurement expertise\n- Establish 23 deaths trauma (parallel to Jhace)\n- Show Pella as support network\n- Foreshadow Piercing exit\n- Measurement as coping mechanism\n\n---\n# 【4†Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md†file-XguHey3FbLfoV7Zzuy7eRk】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file4\nContent Snippet:\n---\n\n### INTERACTION WITH MATERIALS\n\n**Most Responsive:**\n- Metal (especially iron, copper, steel)\n- Stone (structural grades)\n- Wood (structural timber)\n\n**Less Responsive:**\n- Glass (brittle, resists holding)\n- Ceramics (similar to glass)\n- Composites (mixed materials confuse effect)\n\n**Living Tissue:**\n- Responds but unpredictably\n- Healing observed but mechanism unknown\n- Risk of unintended effects\n- Not explored systematically in N01\n\n---\n\n### LIMITATIONS DISCOVERED IN N01\n\n**Cannot:**\n- Affect material without direct contact\n- Maintain effect after breaking contact\n- Hold indefinitely without cost\n- Use casually or frivolously\n- Teach effectively to others\n- Predict exact cost before use\n- Activate on command without emotional engagement\n\n**Can:**\n- Hold structures temporarily\n- Redistribute load through material\n- Sense stress patterns in contacted material\n- Reduce immediate collapse risk\n- Buy time for evacuations\n- Accelerate healing (observed once, not understood)\n\n---\n\n### COST VISIBILITY: REQUIRED DESCRIPTIONS\n\n**Every Soulpulse use in prose must show:**\n\n**During Use:**\n- Copper‑gold glow described (brightness, spread pattern)\n- Physical strain on user (tremor, tension, vibration, breathing)\n- Material response (shimmer, stress redistribution)\n- Duration (in spans, breaths, or contextual time)\n\n**Immediately After:**\n- Tremor status (worsened, maintained, slight recovery)\n- Vein‑line visibility (bright, fading, persistent)\n- Exhaustion level (mild fatigue to near‑collapse)\n- Pain description (location, intensity, duration)\n\n**Long‑Term Tracking:**\n- Stage maintenance or progression\n- Cumulative cost across chapter\n- Recovery time required\n- Impact on daily function\n\n---\n\n### WHAT REMAINS UNKNOWN IN N01\n\n**Unanswered Questions:**\n- What causes Soulpulse capacity?\n- Why did it emerge post‑Piercing?\n- Are there theoretical limits?\n- Can capacity grow with practice?\n- What happens at Stage 3 or beyond?\n- Is cost damage permanent?\n- Can multiple users coordinate effects?\n- What is the actual mechanism?\n\n**These questions are not answered in N01.** Characters operate on empirical observation and educated guessing. Systematic understanding comes later.\n\n---\n\n**SCOPE NOTE:** This document reflects only what's discovered through direct observation and experimentation during N01. Later novellas will expand understanding of dual‑point mechanics, exponential costs, medical applications, and advanced techniques. For N01 prose generation, limit Soulpulse capabilities to what's described here.\n# 【5†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file5\nContent Snippet:\nOn entry-side, Mobel lives inside pain. Shard in his leg becomes teacher: every throb is lesson in separation—what should remain and what must be cut away. He views world through sharpening lens, judging without intending to: weak structures, weak people, weak doctrine. He doesn't name it anything special; he names it clarity.\n\nIn moment of stillness, he becomes aware that something else is moving—another pressure, another intent—not here, but across world's curve. Awareness is not conversation, just clash of directional certainty.\n\nMobel decides that whatever is happening, it must be cleansed into an answer. Interlude ends with him stepping toward authority, not as servant, but as blade seeking handle.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Mobel's cleansing bias (Purity-aligned, unconscious)\n- Pain as teacher metaphor\n- No awareness of being unusual\n- Foreshadow ideological collision\n- Awareness of \"other pressure\" (Corlexi, unknowingly)\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,700 words  \n**POV:** Jhace  \n**Tide:** 20  \n**Setting:** Recovery space, forge\n\nAfter near-collapse, relationship stops pretending it's only work. Jhace's recovery is ugly: shaking, nausea, pain-laced sleep, fear that next pulse will take something permanent. Tiffani is forced into caretaking without losing her edge; Pella and Bram support without crowding.\n\nIn quiet, Jhace admits he can't keep doing this without her—without the method, and without human anchor. Tiffani admits she can't keep treating him as data source without becoming monster.\n\nThey become lovers not as reward, but as pact: if they're going to risk bodies and reputations, they won't do it half-alive. They set rules for cost management and agree to face political consequences together.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Romantic relationship formalizes\n- Practical rather than sentimental\n- Cost management rules established\n- Commitment through shared risk\n- Support network maintains boundaries\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,800 words  \n**POV:** Alternating Jhace/Tiffani  \n**Tide:** 20  \n**Setting:** Planning space, street circuits\n\nCity doesn't stabilize; it only pauses between harms. Jhace and Tiffani review maps, limits, enemies they've made. They choose path forward: keep work public enough to prevent quiet capture, but disciplined enough to survive.\n\nThey plan \"holding circuit\" with rotation, witnesses, documentation—method as protection. There is tenderness, but it's threaded through dread: another Tide of tremors could break everything again.\n\nTheir final act is not grand rescue; it's decision to keep showing up with eyes open, accepting that holding costs, and that love doesn't erase cost—it makes payment worth naming.\n\nChapter ends with them stepping back into streets as unit: not safe, not certain, but aligned.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Practical planning for future work\n- Public accountability as protection\n- Tenderness mixed with dread\n- Love doesn't eliminate cost\n- Commitment to continue despite uncertainty\n\n---\n\n### EPILOGUE\n**Word Target:** ~900 words  \n**POV:** Institutional observer (unnamed)  \n**Tide:** 20+  \n**Setting:** Administrative chamber\n\nQuiet room. File begins. Someone with institutional power—careful hands, careful language—compiles first real ledger of \"resonant stabilization.\" Names recorded alongside locations, effects, cost symptoms.\n\nReport frames phenomenon as resource and threat. Line drawn between \"public safety\" and \"unlicensed interference.\"\n\nOutside window, Saint Merro creaks; inside, policy is forged from fear.\n# 【6†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file6\nContent Snippet:\n### **CHAPTER 6: \"Weight That Won't Yield\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 3-4\n- **Setting:** Major structural failure at Saint Merro junction\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Sustained Pressure → Narrow Success)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium-High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nMulti-story arch threatening collapse. Jhace attempts traditional propping—insufficient. Tiffani arrives with survey data, sketches stress lines on stone (showing him geometry of what he feels). They coordinate without planning: she marks three critical points, he focuses Soulpulse on those exact locations. First true choreographed hold. Success but greater cost: tremor worsens, vein-lines persist longer. Tiffani critiques his \"inefficient\" force application—her precision could reduce his cost by 30%. Jhace's reaction: (1) she's critiquing me, (2) she's right, (3) she sees my inefficiency and wants to fix it. Partnership recognition through competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis opening: arch failing, people still inside\n- Jhace's traditional methods insufficient\n- Tiffani arrives with data/geometry\n- First coordinated Soulpulse (she marks, he powers)\n- Success with visible cost increase\n- Her critique of his technique (care disguised as analysis)\n- His recognition: she sees him, not his heroism\n- Exit: they've choreographed perfectly without planning\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 coordinated use (arch stabilization)\n- Demonstrates improved targeting reduces cost (her geometry + his power)\n- Still significant cost: tremor persistent, vein-lines linger\n\n**Relationship:** First true partnership action—coordination through shared competence\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 progressing, cost accumulation visible\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 7: \"Quiet Calculations\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 5-6\n- **Setting:** Tiffani's workshop/planning space\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,500 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani compiles data from multiple hold sites, building predictive model for failure patterns. She discovers Jhace's cost curve is exponential not linear—he's approaching breaking point faster than he realizes. Pella asks why she cares so much about \"the man with the glow.\" Tiffani's answer: precision requires complete data, and he's destroying the most interesting variable. But underneath: she's starting to see him as person, not just phenomenon. She calculates how to approach him with data he'll actually hear (not orders, not concern, but operational logic). Ends with her walking toward his forge with documented proof he needs to see.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Data compilation and pattern analysis\n- Discovery: cost curve is exponential (crisis point approaching)\n- Pella questions her investment\n- Tiffani's rationalization vs. actual feeling\n- Strategic planning: how to make him listen\n- Decision to approach with evidence\n- Exit: walking toward forge with logbook\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (analysis chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani recognizing care underneath her analytical framework\n\n**Stage Progression:** External calculation of his trajectory toward Stage 2\n\n---\n# 【7†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file7\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n\n**Relationship:** First meeting through competence recognition (she measures, he builds)\n\n**Stage Progression:** 0 → 1 onset (tremor baseline establishing)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 4: \"After the Shudder\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 1, Kindling (morning after Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Triage chamber, Underface tunnels, kiln street\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_02 (Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 1\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Word Count:** 2,350 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace wakes with persistent tremor and pain. Bram reports Chloen's leg healing unexpectedly. Jhace tests power on cracked support without immediate threat—FAILS (no activation). Runner brings emergency: healer's triage wall failing with wounded inside. Immediate threat present—power WORKS but cost increases (vein-lines to elbows, worse tremor). Tiffani observes, asks to see touch points for data collection. Jhace tries to pick up nail—fails three times, tremor jerks it free. Concealment behavior (hands behind back). Tiffani sees everything, documents cost in logbook.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Wakes to baseline tremor (Stage 1 established)\n- Chloen's healing confirmed (side-effect established)\n- Power test on non-critical crack: FAILS (conditional activation shown)\n- Emergency at triage wall: SUCCEEDS (immediate threat required)\n- Cost progression: worse tremor, can't grip nail (functional loss)\n- Tiffani introduction proper: analytical, demands data\n- J/T dynamic: reluctant compliance because her logic is sound\n- Exit: \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 successful use (triage wall), 1 failed attempt (cracked support)\n- Conditional activation confirmed: requires immediate protective need\n- Cost accumulation shown: Stage 1 baseline tremor, fine motor compromised\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani decides to engage deliberately (prevent his destruction through measurement)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline established, functional loss beginning\n\n---\n# 【8†Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md†file-XguHey3FbLfoV7Zzuy7eRk】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file8\nContent Snippet:\n## SOULPULSE: MECHANICS AS DISCOVERED IN N01\n\n**Purpose:** This document describes Soulpulse as it's understood and experienced during N01 \"The Grip That Holds.\" This is empirical knowledge built from observation and experimentation, NOT complete system understanding.\n\n---\n\n### WHAT IS SOULPULSE?\n\n**Observed Phenomenon:**\nFollowing the Piercing, certain individuals discovered they could affect physical reality through focused intention combined with direct contact. This wasn't magic in any traditional sense—no incantations, no reagents, no mystical gestures.\n\n**Common Element:**\nProtective intention crystallized through physical contact. The user must desperately need the material to hold/mend/stabilize AND must be touching it.\n\n---\n\n### ACTIVATION REQUIREMENTS (As Understood in N01)\n\n**Physical Contact:**\n- Requires skin-to-material contact (stone, metal, flesh, wood)\n- Works through thin materials (gloves reduce effectiveness)\n- Must maintain contact during active use\n- Breaking contact ends effect immediately\n\n**Emotional State:**\n- Demands genuine protective need\n- “I need this to hold so people survive” works\n- Casual desire or intellectual will alone doesn't work\n- Fear for others' lives seems to enhance activation\n- Anger or self-preservation less effective\n\n**Awareness:**\n- First activations always instinctive/desperate\n- Conscious activation requires practice\n- User must \"feel\" the material's stress/structure\n- Cannot activate if distracted or panicked\n\n---\n\n### VISUAL MANIFESTATION\n\n**Copper‑Gold Glow:**\n- Distinctive color, visible to observers\n- Spreads from point of contact into material\n- Intensity varies with effort/effect\n- Brighter during active use, dimmer in residue\n\n**Vein‑Lines:**\n- Tracery appears on user's forearms\n- Follows vein patterns but extends beyond normal anatomy\n- Faint when inactive, bright when active\n- Becomes more persistent with repeated use\n- First appears after 2–3 uses\n\n**Material Changes:**\n- Metal/stone shows faint shimmer during hold\n- Stress patterns visible as light/dark areas\n- Effect persists briefly after contact broken (seconds to spans)\n- Leaves subtle changes in material structure\n\n---\n\n### TACTILE EXPERIENCE (User Perspective)\n\n**During Activation:**\n- Intense pressure in palms (like pushing against resistance)\n- Vibration extending from hands through arms into chest\n- Sensation of being \"connected\" to material\n- Can feel stress points, structural integrity, weakness\n- Overwhelming when first experienced\n\n**Physical Sensation:**\n- Warmth spreading from palms\n- Muscle tension throughout arms and shoulders\n- Bone‑deep ache develops during sustained use\n- Breath becomes labored under heavy loads\n- Nausea if pushing too hard\n\n---\n\n### COST SYSTEM: STAGE PROGRESSION (N01 Scope)\n\nSoulpulse use carries a physical cost that escalates with frequency and intensity. Characters in N01 do not explicitly label stages; this section labels them for author reference.\n\n**Stage 0: Baseline (Pre‑Use)**\n- No tremor\n- Normal physical function\n- No visible vein‑lines\n- No chronic symptoms\n\n**Stage 1: Minor Tremor (After 2–3 Uses)**\n- Slight tremor in hands when attempting fine motor tasks\n- Noticeable when picking up small objects, detailed work\n- Fades partially after several spans of rest\n- Vein‑lines appear during use, fade when inactive\n- User can still function normally in non‑precision tasks\n- Most users don't progress past this if they use sparingly\n# 【9†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n- Ch 1-2: Stage 0 (pre-Piercing)\n- Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset\n- Ch 4-9: Stage 1 baseline\n- Ch 10: Approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs)\n- Ch 11-12: Stage 1 stabilized with management\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP autonomy)\n- Ch 11: Trauma bonding (vulnerability exchange)\n- Ch 12: Commitment formalized (shared principles)\n\n**By N01 End:** Load-bearing partnership established, functional love (not romantic confession)\n\n---\n\n## WORD COUNT BUDGET\n\n| Segment | Target | Status |\n|---------|--------|--------|\n| Ch 1 | 1,240 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 2 | 1,280 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 3 | 2,200 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch |\n| **Ch 5** | Tiffani commits to study Soulpulse | Bram amused by J/T dynamic | Pella supporting analysis | — | Professional curiosity deepens |\n| **Ch 6** | First integrated rescue; critique becomes collaboration | Bram sees partnership forming | Pella absent | — | Respect moves toward partnership |\n| **Interlude I** | — | — | — | — | Corlexi view; seeds conflict |\n| **Ch 7** | Training conflict begins | Bram listens but doesn’t intervene | Pella observes | — | Methodological disagreements start |\n| **Ch 8** | Kylar fails; J/T fight about teaching | Bram warns about pushing too hard now public |\n| **Interlude II** | — | — | — | — | Salee lens; sees J/T from distance |\n| **Ch 12** | Ethical alignment (choose Dregs) | Bram continues warnings | — | — | Moral compass shared |\n| Later novellas will alter, deepen, or conclude these relationships. Prose generation must not leak future knowledge or prematurely accelerate these arcs.\n# 【11†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file11\nContent Snippet:\n**By N01 End:** Load-bearing partnership established, functional love (not romantic confession)\n\n---\n\n## WORD COUNT BUDGET\n\n| Segment | Target | Status |\n|---------|--------|--------|\n| Ch 1 | 1,240 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 2 | 1,280 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 3 | 2,200 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 4 | 2,350 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 5 | 2,100 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 6 | ~1,800 | To Write |\n| Ch 7 | ~1,500 | To Write |\n| I-1 | ~700 | To Write |\n| Ch 8 | ~1,600 | To Write |\n| Ch 9 | ~1,700 | To Write |\n| Ch 10 | ~1,800 | To Write |\n| I-2 | ~800 | To Write |\n| Ch 11 | ~1,900 | To Write |\n| Ch 12 | ~1,600 | To Write |\n| Epi | ~900 | To Write |\n| **TOTAL** | **~20,470** | **45% Complete** |\n\n---\n\n## FORBIDDEN SEQUENCES (N01 CREATES)\n\n**Two-Chapter Sequences That Cannot Repeat in Future Novellas:**\n1. BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 (Ch 1→2)\n2. BEAT_01 → BEAT_03 (Ch 2→3)\n3. BEAT_03 → BEAT_02 (Ch 3→4)\n4. BEAT_02 → BEAT_04 (Ch 4→5)\n5. BEAT_04 → BEAT_03 (Ch 5→6)\n\n**Three-Chapter Sequences:**\n1. BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 → BEAT_03 (Ch 1→2→3)\n2. BEAT_03 → BEAT_02 → BEAT_04 (Ch 3→4→5)\n\n---\n\n## CONSTRAINT REMINDERS\n\n**All chapters must maintain:**\n- ✅ Lexicon compliance (Tide/Kindling/Striking/Pouring/Quench/Anneal, no Earth terms)\n- ✅ Voice consistency (Jhace terse/physical, Tiffani analytical/precise)\n- ✅ No metaphors/similes (direct description only)\n- ✅ Minimal personification (structural language acceptable)\n- ✅ Cost visibility (Stage progression shown through physical markers)\n- ✅ Knowledge gates (no future reveals, no divine mechanics explained)\n- ✅ Innocent frame (characters don't know they're god-vessels)\n\n---\n\n## GRID STATUS\n\n**Version:** 2.0 (12-Chapter Structure)\n**Date Created:** [Current]\n**Supersedes:** Previous 15-chapter grid\n**Authority:** Mandatory reference for all N01 prose generation\n**Next Review:** After Ch 6 completion (validate structure holds)\n\n---\n\n**END OF GRID**\n\n**Remember: This grid is law. Any deviation requires explicit authorization and grid update before proceeding.**\n# 【12†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file12\nContent Snippet:\nEvery scar holds memory. They discuss larger currents: registries spreading, clinics multiplying, RRO expanding, black-market training proliferating. They know storm is forming beyond their neighborhood. What they can control: what they hold. At forge, they write three priorities on wall: (1) Truth over comfort, (2) People over institutions, (3) Keep each other honest about why we act. Vow without ceremony. Community members add their marks beside it—informal pact, local mandate. Not oath to Crown or RRO, but commitment to third path. Jhace's hand shakes as he writes; Tiffani laces their fingers, lets his tremor move through both. Physical reminder: cost and commitment shared. Exit: they face uncertain future together, foundation locked.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Walk through rebuilt district (uneven success)\n- Recognition of larger forces gathering\n- Decision: focus on what they can hold\n- Three priorities written on forge wall\n- Community witnesses/joins commitment\n- Shared physical moment (tremor through linked hands)\n- Acceptance: this is who they are together\n- Exit: foundation locked, future uncertain but faced together\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (commitment/resolution chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Partnership formalized through shared principles (not romance, but deeper)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline accepted as new permanent state\n\n---\n\n### **EPILOGUE: \"Auditor Observation\"**\n- **POV:** Auditor (meta-perspective outside time)\n- **Setting:** Auditor's observation point (undefined location)\n- **Word Count Target:** 800-1,000 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Assessment of characters: Jhace unpredictable because care is absolute, Tiffani unpredictable because precision is absolute, both useful because they might survive what's coming. Interest in their bond: they love functionally (load-bearing systems), not romantically. Remove one, other fails. This is useful—when gods move and alliances fracture, bond will save or destroy them. Either way, worth observing. Decision: flag them as anomaly, predict system will move to crush or co-opt. Notes that Turning 1's hope phase shading toward devastation, their bond potential fulcrum or liability. If love makes people break rules, it reshapes wars—or ruins plans. Sets stage for N02 escalation.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n# 【13†Constraint_Systems.md†file-3XF34gyvd74ccfuXdn3t5s】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Constraint_Systems.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file13\nContent Snippet:\n* Why Soulpulse exists or what powers it.\n* That “Stages” are formal categories.  They experience symptoms but do not label them as Stage 1/2.\n* That the four faction leaders are gods in mortal form.\n* That seals were weakened by the Piercing.\n* That Tiffani will die or that the Fathom Break will occur.\n* Any events or revelations occurring in N02–N30.\n\n**Reader Permissions:**\n\n* Through interludes and the epilogue, readers get glimpses of the Auditor and see that the story is being watched and evaluated.  They may infer that bigger forces are at work but do not receive explanations.\n\n**Forbidden:**\n\n* Exposition dumps about the truth of the cosmos, gods, or the full saga.  Leave big questions unanswered.\n* Characters using terms like “divine,” “seal,” or “principle” in their own thoughts.  They don’t know.\n\n---\n\n### 4. BEAT AND STRUCTURAL RULES\n\nN01 uses an eight‑beat method catalogue (BEAT_01 through BEAT_08).  Each chapter is mapped to one beat.  The purpose is to prevent repetitive emotional rhythms and maintain variety.\n\n**Beat Assignments:** See `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for detailed definitions.  The validator checks chapters against their assigned beats.\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n\n* No consecutive chapters using the same beat (e.g., BEAT_01 → BEAT_01).\n* No consecutive failure loops (BEAT_02 → BEAT_02) or training loops (BEAT_05 → BEAT_05).\n* BEAT_03 (crisis front‑loaded) limited to three uses total in N01 and never back‑to‑back.\n* BEAT_07 (political pressure) limited to two uses in N01, never back‑to‑back.\n\n**Emotional Intensity Balance:**\n\n* Maximum 2 high‑intensity chapters in a row (see `Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md` for high/medium/low classification).\n* Use interludes to break up intensity and offer different viewpoints.\n* Each emotional arc (hope→failure→success, etc.) must land; do not cut it short or stretch it too long.\n\n---\n\n### 5. VOICE SEPARATION\n\nEach POV character must sound and think differently.  The validator enforces voice and POV discipline:\n\n* **Jhace:** Short, physical, sensory sentences.  Minimal metaphor.  Deflecting humor.  Focuses on weight, vibration, structural failure, guilt.\n* **Tiffani:** Longer, analytical sentences.  Measurement and pattern language.  Warm underneath precision.  Deploys wit and innuendo when appropriate.\n* **Bram:** Long, rambling sentences.  Dry, resigned humor.  Observes human nature and systems.  Calls out hypocrisy gently.\n* **Interlude POVs:** Corlexi is methodical, procedural.  Salee is warm and pragmatic.  The Auditor is clinical and detached.  Each interlude voice must clearly differ from chapter voices.\n\n* **POV Limit:** Only describe what the POV character can directly perceive or reasonably infer.  Do not include omniscient knowledge or other characters’ inner thoughts.\n\n---\n\n### 6. VALIDATION AND REVISION\n\nThe companion file `N01_Validator_Checklist.md` outlines a step‑by‑step process for checking drafts.  Always run a chapter through the checklist before considering it final.\n\n**Key validation steps include:**\n# 【14†N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md†file-9HfHWbfzEZfbejJHapEEQn】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file14\nContent Snippet:\n|\n\nIf you adjust events per chapter later, update this table to keep Tides and phases in sync.\n\n---\n\n### 3. SOULPULSE PROGRESSION MAP\n\n#### Jhace’s Stage Over Time\n\n| Tide / Chapters        | Stage State                 | Notes                                         |\n|------------------------|----------------------------|----------------------------------------------|\n| Pre-Tide 0 (off-page)  | Stage 0                    | No Soulpulse, baseline humanity.             |\n| Tide 0 (Ch 3)          | Stage 0 → Stage 1 onset    | First two instinctive uses; tremor appears.  |\n| Tide 1–2 (Ch 4–10)     | Stage 1                    | Tremor intermittent → persistent; vein-lines during use. |\n| Tide 3 (Ch 11–12)      | Stage 1 high / borderline  | Increased frequency/intensity of uses.       |\n| Tide 4–5 (Ch 13–15)    | Approaching Stage 2        | Tremor constant, vein-lines faint at rest, deep exhaustion. |\n\nRules:\n\n- No Stage 2 behavior before ~Ch 12.  \n- No Stage 0 behavior after Ch 4.  \n- Stage references stay descriptive in prose (no numbered “Stage” labels in POV).\n\n---\n\n### 4. J/T RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION MAP\n\n#### Relationship State by Chapter\n\n| ID         | State Label              | Short Description                                           |\n|------------|--------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|\n| Ch 1–2     | Mutual Judgment (Remote) | Each holds negative assumptions based on guild gossip.      |\n| Ch 3       | Forced Proximity         | Both survive same event; competence glimpsed under stress. |\n| Ch 4–5     | Early Respect            | Recognize usefulness of each other’s skills.                |\n| Ch 6       | Cost Witnessed           | Tiffani sees what Soulpulse does to Jhace; critique as care.|\n| Ch 7       | Shared Fear              | Realize they’re both driven by terror of preventable deaths.|\n| Ch 8–9     | Functional Partnership   | Work and argue as a unit; survive a failure together.      |\n| Ch 10      | Integrated Team          | Trust system: Jhace executes, Tiffani optimizes.           |\n| Ch 11      | Political Alignment      | Present united front vs. RRO.                              |\n| Ch I2      | –                       | Salee views them as a pair, even if they haven’t named it. |\n| Ch 12      | Ethical Alignment        | Choose same moral priority (neglected districts).          |\n| Ch 13      | Romantic Acknowledgment  | “More than work partnership” made explicit.                |\n| Ch 14      | Almost-Intimate          | Crisis interrupts; physical closeness undeniable.          |\n| Ch 15      | Committed Pair           | Exhausted but clear: “we keep holding together.”           |\n| Epilogue   | Externally Logged        | Auditor notes partnership as relevant factor.              |\n\nAny chapter where their behavior doesn’t match this state should be revised or the map updated. The map is the source of truth.\n\n---\n\n### 5. MAJOR EVENT ANCHORS\n\nA few events that everything else orbits around:\n\n1. **Piercing (Tide 0, Ch 3)**  \n   - City-wide collapse, thousands dead.  \n   - First Soulpulse activations.  \n   - J/T lives intersect structurally (even if not fully together yet).\n# 【15†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file15\nContent Snippet:\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n- Ch 1-2: Stage 0 (pre-Piercing)\n- Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset\n- Ch 4-9: Stage 1 baseline\n- Ch 10: Approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs)\n- Ch 11-12: Stage 1 stabilized with management\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION TRACKING\n# 【16†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file16\nContent Snippet:\nTiffani calculates: resources needed, cost is autonomy. Logic says accept. But she checks variable: Jhace will see this as betrayal, and his trust affects coordination. Deeper: she wants his trust (not logical, but real). She pulls him aside, presents logic without asking permission. He asks: \"What do you want?\" She answers: keep people alive without becoming Corvin's tool (contradictory goals in his framework). Jhace: \"Then don't join him. I won't be vulnerable alone.\" They publicly refuse Corvin. Political consequence: now enemies of powerful faction. Personal consequence: they chose each other over safety. First explicit choice of partnership over pragmatism.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Corvin/Tellis offer (resources for control)\n- Tiffani's calculation (logic vs. feeling)\n- She consults Jhace (first time asking partner input)\n- His question: what do YOU want?\n- Her honest answer: contradictory desires\n- His commitment: vulnerable together\n- Public refusal of institutional power\n- Exit: political enemies made, personal bond strengthened\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (political chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** First explicit choice of each other over safety/pragmatism\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stable Stage 1 with coordinated cost management\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 10: \"Near Collapse\"**\n- **POV:** Both (alternating perspective within chapter)\n- **Tide:** 14-15\n- **Setting:** Major structural crisis at Saint Merro main support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (External Threat → Narrow Escape)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nCritical main support failing during high-traffic period. Hundreds at risk. Jhace and Tiffani coordinate under pressure: she calculates load paths in real-time, he applies force to her exact specifications. They move as single unit, no wasted words, pure operational sync. Success—but Jhace pushes past safe limits to ensure no one dies. Cost: approaches Stage 2 threshold (tremor violent, vein-lines permanent at baseline, brief moment of sensory overload). Tiffani physically restrains him from continuing, makes decision FOR him: \"You're done. I don't care if you hate me for it.\" Saves him by overriding his control. Afterward, his rage at her presumption vs. her cold certainty she was right. Tension peaks: control vs. care, autonomy vs. survival.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis: hundreds at risk, main support failing\n- Perfect coordination under pressure\n- Jhace pushes past limits (caretaker compulsion)\n- Tiffani's override: physical intervention\n- Success but near-Stage 2 cost\n- His rage: she took his choice\n- Her certainty: he would have killed himself\n- Relationship stress: care as control\n- Exit: silent fury and mutual recognition they need each other\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 major use (near-Stage 2 threshold)\n- Coordinated efficiency maintains, but scale drives cost\n- Brief sensory overload (Stage 2 warning sign)\n- Vein-lines now faintly visible at rest\n\n**Relationship:** Conflict through care—she saves him by controlling him, he hates needing it\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 → approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs present)\n\n---\n# 【17†N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md†file-CAd4CywpTdBkiKM88Yt3Ti】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file17\nContent Snippet:\nno direct relationships      |\n\n(Titles can change; functional roles should not.)\n\n---\n\n### 2. PRIMARY LOCATIONS BY CHAPTER\n\n| Ch | Primary Location(s)                      | Notes                                                      |\n|----|-----------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|\n| 1  | Underface heat-grid tunnels, forge area | Establish industrial vibe, Jhace’s work patterns          |\n| 2  | Merrow observatory,\n# 【18†N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md†file-6ERDNSoJumE8RYbV3j8rJJ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file18\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 — COMMON FAILURE MODES & HOW TO FIX THEM\n\n**Purpose:** Enumerate the ways a prose engine is most likely to screw up N01, and give concrete checks and fixes. This doc exists so you can catch problems fast.\n\n---\n\n### 1. Lexicon Leaks (Earth Time, Distance, Culture)\n\n**Failure:**\n- The prose slips in “day,” “week,” “hours,” “mile,” “north,” “okay,” etc.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Immediately breaks immersion.\n- Undermines all the effort spent on Tides/Anneals/Crucibles.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Simple string search over draft:\n  - Time: `day, week, month, year, hour, minute, second, morning, afternoon, evening, night` (when used as Earth time, not as generic words).\n  - Distance: `foot, feet, inch, mile, meter, kilometer`.\n  - Direction: `north, south, east, west`.\n  - Slang: `okay, ok, cool, awesome, dude, guy`.\n\n**Fix:**\n- Replace with Te’Oga terms:\n  - “day” → “Tide”\n  - “week” → “Anneal”\n  - “month” → “Crucible”\n  - “years old” → “Turns old”\n  - “hours/minutes” → “spans, breaths, heartbeats”\n  - “miles/feet” → “paces, spans, poles”\n  - “north/south” → “up-slope, downslope, toward the river, inland”\n- Adjust phrasing so it feels native, not like a search/replace hack.\n\n---\n\n### 2. Invisible Cost (Magic With No Price Tag)\n\n**Failure:**\n- Soulpulse use reads like a superhero power:\n  - Beam holds, no visible toll.\n  - User shrugs and keeps going.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Destroys the entire hard-cost system.\n- Breaks future stakes (no reason to fear Stage 2+).\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- For every Soulpulse use, ask:\n  - Do we see physical strain *during* the use?\n  - Do we see specific aftereffects (tremor, vein-lines, exhaustion, pain)?\n  - Is there any sense of cumulative damage across chapters?\n\n**Fix:**\n- Add explicit cost beats:\n  - During: shaking hands, burning forearms, laboring breath, time-span of the hold.\n  - After: dropping tools, needing to be helped to sit, failing to grip small objects, sleep that doesn’t fix it.\n- Track progression:\n  - Early chapters: tremor fades with rest.\n  - Late chapters: tremor baseline, doesn’t vanish.\n\n---\n\n### 3. Voice Flattening (Everyone Sounds the Same)\n\n**Failure:**\n- Jhace, Tiffani, Bram all speak and think with the exact same cadence and vocabulary.\n\n**Why It’s Bad:**\n- Kills character identity.\n- Makes the world feel AI-written rather than lived.\n\n**How to Detect:**\n- Strip dialogue tags from a short scene.\n- Can you still tell who said what just by the wording?\n- If not, voice is flattened.\n# 【19†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn6file19\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Assessment of characters: Jhace unpredictable because care is absolute, Tiffani unpredictable because precision is absolute, both useful because they might survive what's coming. Interest in their bond: they love functionally (load-bearing systems), not romantically. Remove one, other fails. This is useful—when gods move and alliances fracture, bond will save or destroy them. Either way, worth observing. Decision: flag them as anomaly, predict system will move to crush or co-opt. Notes that Turning 1's hope phase shading toward devastation, their bond potential fulcrum or liability. If love makes people break rules, it reshapes wars—or ruins plans. Sets stage for N02 escalation.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\nVisible: 0% - 100%",

            "Pulled **all segment titles, POVs, Tide ranges, beat methods, interlude placement, Soulpulse usage tracking, and the word-count budget** directly from your **canonical 12-ch grid** fileciteturn25file13 fileciteturn26file9, including Ch12/Epilogue specifics fileciteturn26file3 and both interludes fileciteturn25file5 fileciteturn25file4.\n\nBelow are **copy/paste-ready** replacements for the files you listed (plus the **Tier-2 addenda** you asked for).\n\n---\n\n```md\n# N01 TIMELINE SKELETON — 12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE (GRID-ALIGNED)\n\n**Authority:** `N01_Chapter_Grid.md` is law. This skeleton must match it exactly.\n\n**Purpose:** Anchor N01 chapters + interludes + epilogue in Tides/phases, major events, Soulpulse progression, and relationship states.\n\n## 1) High-level timeline (12-ch grid)\n- **Tide 0:** Pre-impact setup + the Piercing (Ch 1–3).\n- **Tide 1:** Immediate aftermath; activation-condition validation begins (Ch 4).\n- **Tides 2–4:** Post-Piercing mapping + first coordinated field hold (Ch 5–6).\n- **Tides 5–8:** Model-building + interloper pressure glimpsed; method shift forced (Ch 7 + Interlude I + Ch 8).\n- **Tide 9:** Off-page passage (referenced only).\n- **Tides 10–12:** Political co-option attempt; explicit refusal together (Ch 9).\n- **Tide 13:** Off-page passage (referenced only).\n- **Tides 14–16:** Major crisis + near-Stage-2 warning signs; second interloper glimpse (Ch 10 + Interlude II).\n- **Tides 17–18:** Aftermath processing; intimacy/vulnerability exchange; bond re-locked (Ch 11).\n- **Tides 19–20:** Public principles + third-path foundation lock (Ch 12).\n- **Tide 20+:** Auditor epilogue assessment (Epilogue).\n\n## 2) Segment table (grid authority)\n| Segment | Tide | Phase (if specified) | POV | Title | Notes |\n|---|---:|---|---|---|---|\n| Ch 1 | 0 | Kindling | Jhace | Something Wrong | Heat-grid “off-note”; Bram dynamic; first tremor foreshadow. |\n| Ch 2 | 0 | Pouring | Tiffani | Glass That Listens | Atmospheric distortion measured; white line; evacuation begins. |\n| Ch 3 | 0 | Striking | Jhace | The Sky Breaks | Piercing; 2 Soulpulse uses; Chloen rescue + unexplained healing; first J/T contact. |\n| Ch 4 | 1 | Kindling | Jhace | After the Shudder | Failed non-emergency test; triage wall success; Stage-1 baseline established; Tiffani begins logging. |\n| Ch 5 | 2–3 | (not fixed) | Tiffani | Lines That Don’t Lie | Maps damage + residue; refuses co-option; decides to engage Jhace directly. |\n| Ch 6 | 3–4 | (not fixed) | Jhace | Weight That Won’t Yield | First choreographed field hold (Tiffani targets; Jhace powers); cost rises; method need seeded. |\n| Ch 7 | 5–6 | (not fixed) | Tiffani | Quiet Calculations | Exponential cost curve discovered; approach plan; Pella pressures truth. |\n| I-1 | ~6–7 | (not fixed) | Corlexi | Corlexi — System vs Chaos | “Form” lens; institutional frame; J/T logged as anomaly; innocent frame held. |\n| Ch 8 | 7–8 | (not fixed) | Jhace | Proof and Resistance | Cost-curve confrontation; controlled efficiency test; resistance → acceptance. |\n| Ch 9 | 10–12 | (not fixed) | Tiffani | Ash and Claims | Political offer + refusal; explicit choice of each other over safety; targets created. |\n| Ch 10 | 14–15 | (not fixed) | Both (alternating) | Near Collapse | Main support failure; perfect sync; Jhace overextends; Tiffani overrides; warning signs. |\n| I-2 | ~15–16 | (not fixed) | Mobel | Mobel — Purity Rising | “Purity” lens; senses Form across the world; flags J/T as complication; innocent frame held. |\n| Ch 11 | 17–18 | (not fixed) | Both (intimate alternating) | Holding Together | Vulnerability exchange (23 names / Marisse); first deliberate non-crisis contact; operational commitment. |\n| Ch 12 | 19–20 | Quench | Both (balanced) | Choosing the Weight | Walk the district; write three priorities; community witnesses; tremor shared through linked hands. |\n| Epi | 20+ | — | Auditor | Auditor Observation | Clinical audit; “useful anomaly”; forecasts pressure in N02 without revealing mechanics. |\n\n## 3) Soulpulse progression (N01 scope)\n**Observed activation constraints:** Direct contact + protective desperation; casual testing fails.\n\n**Cost visibility rule:** Every use must show copper-gold manifestation + strain + aftermath (tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion).\n\n**Usage log (successful uses):**\n- Ch 3: 2 activations (beam rescue; building support).\n- Ch 4: 1 failed attempt (no immediate threat) + 1 successful emergency hold (triage wall).\n- Ch 6: 1 coordinated activation (arch stabilization) with Tiffani targeting.\n- Ch 8: 1 controlled efficiency test activation (measurable reduction).\n- Ch 10: 1 major activation (near-Stage-2 warning signs).\n\n**Total:** 7 successful uses + 1 failed attempt.\n\n## 4) Stage-state consistency (symptomatic only; no numbered labels in-world)\n- **Stage 0 behavior** only before Ch 3.\n- **Stage 1 baseline** established by Ch 4 and maintained through Ch 9.\n- **Approaching Stage 2 threshold** appears at Ch 10 (warning signs).\n- **Ch 11–12:** Stage 1 stabilizes with management; baseline effects remain.\n\n## 5) Relationship progression anchors (Jhace/Tiffani)\n- Reputation-only mutual judgment (Ch 1–2).\n- Competence-recognition meeting under collapse (Ch 3).\n- Deliberate observation phase (Ch 4–5).\n- First choreographed field coordination (Ch 6).\n- Trust building via proof; resistance → acceptance (Ch 7–8).\n- Explicit partnership choice over safety (Ch 9).\n- Crisis sync + rupture (care-as-control) (Ch 10).\n- Vulnerability exchange + load-bearing intimacy (Ch 11).\n- Public principles + shared tremor acceptance (Ch 12).\n- Auditor frames bond as functional love + “useful anomaly” (Epilogue).\n\n## 6) Update rule\nIf any chapter’s Tide, POV, beat method, Soulpulse usage count, or interlude placement changes, update the grid first, then update this skeleton to match.\n```\n\n---\n\n```md\n# RELATIONSHIP DYNAMICS — 12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE (GRID-ALIGNED)\n\n**Authority:** `N01_Chapter_Grid.md` is law.\n\n**Purpose:** Track relationship arcs by segment so scenes don’t jump ahead, regress, or contradict the grid’s emotional and structural intent.\n\n## 1) Jhace & Tiffani (primary arc)\n| Segment | State label | What it must look like on-page |\n|---|---|---|\n| Ch 1–2 | Mutual Judgment (Remote) | Each interprets the other’s reputation as dangerous (ego vs perfectionism) before meeting. |\n| Ch 3 | Forced Proximity (Competence First) | First contact is functional under collapse; recognition is skill-based, not warm. |\n| Ch 4–5 | Deliberate Observation | She logs conditions/cost; he resists being watched; observation becomes pressure and care. |\n| Ch 6 | First Coordination (Action) | First choreographed hold: her targeting + his contact. “Partnership” becomes behavior, not label. |\n| Ch 7–8 | Trust Building (Resistance → Acceptance) | She proves method; he hates being managed but accepts because it saves lives and reduces cost. |\n| Ch 9 | Choice Over Safety | They refuse capture/co-option together; partnership becomes explicit shared risk. |\n| Ch 10 | Crisis Sync + Rupture | They move as one under load—then she physically stops him; autonomy vs survival conflict peaks. |\n| Ch 11 | Trauma Exchange → Operational Commitment | 23 names / Marisse; first deliberate non-crisis contact; need is stated as operational truth. |\n| Ch 12 | Public Principles | Three priorities on the forge wall; community witnesses; tremor shared through linked hands. |\n| Epilogue | Externally Logged | Auditor frames bond as load-bearing and “useful anomaly” (no romance-forward framing). |\n\n## 2) Jhace & Bram\n- Bram remains Jhace’s stabilizing witness and the person who can say “stop” when Jhace cannot.\n- Bram’s presence should reinforce cost-and-care as theme via quiet observation, not speeches.\n\n## 3) Tiffani & Pella\n- Pella’s repeated care actions (food, ink, scolding) make Tiffani’s refusal-to-rest readable as pattern, not “coldness.”\n- Pella’s questions force Tiffani to admit (to herself) that Jhace is no longer only “data.”\n\n## 4) Institutions & factions (relationship pressure)\n- Co-option attempts must be on-page and force explicit choices (committee roles, resource trades, “registry” pressure).\n- Interludes (Corlexi, Mobel) must preserve innocent frame while showing that J/T’s third path registers as system-relevant.\n```\n\n---\n\n```md\n# N01: “THE GRIP THAT HOLDS” — MASTER BIBLE (12-CHAPTER GRID-ALIGNED)\n\n**Authority:** `N01_Chapter_Grid.md` is mandatory reference for all prose generation and all structural decisions.\n\n**Purpose:** Single reference for N01 prose generation aligned to the 12-chapter grid: world lexicon, knowledge gates, voice separation, and Soulpulse cost visibility.\n\n## 1) Novella parameters (authoritative)\n- **ID:** N01\n- **Title:** The Grip That Holds\n- **Turning:** 1 — GLASS\n- **Structure:** 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue = 15 segments\n- **Target length:** 18,000–21,000 words total\n- **Timeline covered:** Tide 0 through Tide 20 (plus epilogue at Tide 20+)\n\n## 2) World state (N01 scope)\n- Saint Merro is a terraced guild city with proto-industrial tech and a heat-grid; no electricity.\n- Lexicon purity rules apply to prose: no Earth time/distance/direction units.\n- The Piercing is the sky-shearing event that destabilizes the city and seeds the conditions under which Soulpulse begins appearing.\n\n## 3) Core arc (grid condensed)\n- Jhace discovers Soulpulse during the Piercing and learns it is contact- and protection-dependent, not casual or free.\n- Tiffani becomes the primary analyst and method-builder; partnership forms through competence, proof, conflict, and refusal of capture.\n- The bond resolves into a third-path commitment: public principles, community witness, and independence from institutions.\n- Auditor frames outcome as functional, load-bearing love and a “useful anomaly.”\n\n## 4) Character lens (N01)\n### Jhace Torrins\n- Voice: terse, physical, load-path thinking; humor lands accidentally; genuine laughter is rare and abrupt.\n- Drive: prevent preventable death; overextends; treats failure as moral debt.\n- Soulpulse cost must remain visible: tremor, vein-lines, pain, exhaustion, fine-control loss when pushed.\n\n### Tiffani Merrow\n- Voice: analytical, precise; gets more verbose under stress; humor is sharp and timed.\n- Drive: pattern obsession rooted in Marisse’s death; turns fear into measurement and method.\n- Cannot use Soulpulse in N01; she is the interpreter and lever that reduces Jhace’s waste through targeting and procedure.\n\n### Bram Kelter\n- Anchor presence; dry, resigned humor; warns without forcing; steady witness to Jhace’s spiral.\n\n### Interlude POVs (N01)\n- **Corlexi:** Form lens; institutional pressure; innocent frame held.\n- **Mobel:** Purity lens; ideological certainty; innocent frame held.\n- **Auditor:** clinical meta assessment; no mythic explanations; no future spoilers.\n\n## 5) Soulpulse (N01 scope rules)\n- Activates through skin contact + desperate protective intention; non-critical tests fail and must be shown failing when attempted.\n- Manifestation: copper-gold glow spreading into material; visible stress-pattern effects; vein-line tracery on user’s arms.\n- Cost progression must be described symptomatically (no in-world “Stage” labels) and must match the grid’s escalation points:\n  - Ch 3 onset; Ch 4 baseline; Ch 10 warning signs; Ch 11–12 stabilization with management.\n\n## 6) Knowledge gates (non-negotiable)\n- Characters do not know why Soulpulse exists; do not understand formal “stages”; do not have cosmic explanations.\n- Interludes hint at larger forces but keep the innocent frame (vessels do not recognize divinity).\n- Epilogue Auditor is clinical and does not reveal full mechanics or specific future events.\n\n## 7) Structure overview (by act)\n### Act I: The Cut (Ch 1–4)\n- Parallel observation chapters (Jhace then Tiffani) lead into Piercing and first Soulpulse uses, then immediate aftermath validation of conditions and cost.\n\n### Act II: The Pattern (Ch 5–9 + Interlude I after Ch 7)\n- Tiffani maps damage + cost curve; refuses co-option; method shift proved; culminates in explicit partnership-over-safety choice.\n\n### Act III: The Grip (Ch 10–12 + Interlude II after Ch 10 + Epilogue)\n- Crisis tests autonomy vs care; vulnerability exchange earns intimacy; commitment is formalized publicly; Auditor logs the outcome as system-relevant anomaly.\n\n## 8) Constraints summary (must be enforced every draft)\n- **Lexicon purity:** No Earth units; Tides/Anneals/Spans; landmark directions only.\n- **Cost visibility:** Every Soulpulse use shows activation conditions + strain + aftermath.\n- **Voice separation:** Jhace/Tiffani/Bram/Corlexi/Mobel/Auditor must be unmistakable.\n- **Beat compliance:** Follow the beat method assigned by the grid; avoid forbidden beat sequences.\n```\n\n---\n\n```md\n# N01 QUICK REFERENCE TABLES — 12-CHAPTER GRID-ALIGNED\n\n**Authority:** `N01_Chapter_Grid.md` is law. Use this file for fast sanity checks.\n\n## 1) Segment quick lookup\n| Segment | Title | POV | Tide | Beat Method | Soulpulse | Intensity | Target |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---:|---|---:|\n| Ch1 | Something Wrong | Jhace | 0 (Kindling) | BEAT_01 | 0 | Low | 1,240 |\n| Ch2 | Glass That Listens | Tiffani | 0 (Pouring) | BEAT_01 | 0 | Low | 1,280 |\n| Ch3 | The Sky Breaks | Jhace | 0 (Striking) | BEAT_03 | 2 | High | 2,200 |\n| Ch4 | After the Shudder | Jhace | 1 (Kindling) | BEAT_02 | 1 (+1 fail) | Medium | 2,350 |\n| Ch5 | Lines That Don’t Lie | Tiffani | 2–3 | BEAT_01 | 0 | Medium | 2,100 |\n| Ch6 | Weight That Won’t Yield | Jhace | 3–4 | BEAT_03 | 1 | Med-High | ~1,800 |\n| Ch7 | Quiet Calculations | Tiffani | 5–6 | BEAT_21 | 0 | Low | ~1,500 |\n| I-1 | Corlexi — System vs Chaos | Corlexi | ~6–7 | Build-up | 0 | — | ~700 |\n| Ch8 | Proof and Resistance | Jhace | 7–8 | BEAT_12 | 1 | Medium | ~1,600 |\n| Ch9 | Ash and Claims | Tiffani | 10–12 | BEAT_16 | 0 | Medium | ~1,700 |\n| Ch10 | Near Collapse | Both | 14–15 | BEAT_03 | 1 | High | ~1,800 |\n| I-2 | Mobel — Purity Rising | Mobel | ~15–16 | Build-up | 0 | — | ~800 |\n| Ch11 | Holding Together | Both | 17–18 | BEAT_17 | 0 | High | ~1,900 |\n| Ch12 | Choosing the Weight | Both | 19–20 (Quench) | BEAT_21 | 0 | Medium | ~1,600 |\n| Epi | Auditor Observation | Auditor | 20+ | Meta | 0 | — | ~900 |\n\n## 2) Soulpulse usage tracking (sanity check)\n- **Total successful uses:** 7  \n  - Ch 3: 2  \n  - Ch 4: 1  \n  - Ch 6: 1  \n  - Ch 8: 1  \n  - Ch 10: 1  \n- **Failed attempt:** Ch 4 (non-emergency test)\n\n## 3) Stage progression (symptomatic only; no in-world numbering)\n- Ch 1–2: pre-onset behavior only\n- Ch 3: onset begins\n- Ch 4–9: Stage-1 baseline established and held\n- Ch 10: approaching Stage-2 warning signs\n- Ch 11–12: Stage-1 stabilization with management; baseline effects remain\n\n## 4) Relationship progression (sanity check)\n- Ch 1–2: reputation only\n- Ch 3: first meeting (competence)\n- Ch 4–5: deliberate observation\n- Ch 6: first choreographed field coordination\n- Ch 7–8: trust building (proof → acceptance)\n- Ch 9: explicit choice over safety\n- Ch 10: conflict through care (override)\n- Ch 11: trauma exchange; operational commitment; first deliberate non-crisis contact\n- Ch 12: public principles; community witness; shared tremor\n\n## 5) Interlude placement\n- I-1 after Ch 7\n- I-2 after Ch 10\n- Epilogue after Ch 12\n\n## 6) Beat method distribution (quick check)\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10\n- BEAT_12: Ch 8\n- BEAT_16: Ch 9\n- BEAT_17: Ch 11\n- BEAT_21: Ch 7, 12\n```\n\n---\n\n```md\n# N01 INTERLUDES & EPILOGUE — SPECIFICATION (12-CHAPTER GRID-ALIGNED)\n\n**Authority:** `N01_Chapter_Grid.md` is law.\n\n**Purpose:** Define exactly what the two Interludes and Epilogue in N01 must do, what they are allowed to reveal, and how they must relate to the 12 chapters.\n\n## 1) INTERLUDE I — CORLEXI (“System vs Chaos”)\n**Placement:** After **Chapter 7** (mid-Act II).  \n**Tide:** ~6–7 (approx).  \n**POV & Camera:** Tight third, anchored to **Corlexi** only.  \n**Length Target:** 600–900 words.\n\n**Job:**\n- Show early institutional consolidation (RRO / Crown apparatus) and how crisis becomes “systems thinking.”\n- Establish Corlexi’s Form bias as compulsion toward structure and control.\n- Identify Jhace/Tiffani as an anomaly because they refuse containment and build a third path.\n- Maintain innocent frame: no self-awareness of divinity, no cosmic exposition.\n\n**Allowed Knowledge:**\n- May know reports of “copper-gold light” and “unlicensed stabilization.”\n- May propose registries/assignments.\n- May NOT explain Soulpulse mechanics or name cosmology.\n\n**Exit Hook:** A decision to move on the anomaly (registration pressure, oversight, containment planning).\n\n## 2) INTERLUDE II — MOBEL (“Purity Rising”)\n**Placement:** After **Chapter 10** (between crisis and resolution).  \n**Tide:** ~15–16 (approx).  \n**Setting:** Entry-side (opposite hemisphere from Saint Merro).  \n**POV & Camera:** Tight third, anchored to **Mobel** only.  \n**Length Target:** 600–900 words.\n\n**Job:**\n- Establish a second Principle lens (Purity) and its limitation (absolutes, intolerance for ambiguity).\n- Show Mobel sensing another force moving (Form/Corlexi) without understanding what it is.\n- Flag Jhace/Tiffani’s third path as complication to be judged, cleansed, or forced into category.\n- Maintain innocent frame: no recognition of divinity; no “lore dump.”\n\n**Exit Hook:** Mobel chooses alignment toward authority/action (movement toward shaping conflict beyond Saint Merro).\n\n## 3) EPILOGUE — AUDITOR (“Auditor Observation”)\n**Placement:** After **Chapter 12**.  \n**Tide:** 20+ (meta).  \n**POV & Camera:** Claustrophically controlled perspective labeled **The Auditor**.  \n**Setting:** Abstract / unlocated observation point.  \n**Length Target:** 800–1,000 words.\n\n**Job:**\n- Reframe N01 as logged outcomes: cost curves, bond behavior, institutional reactions.\n- Name Jhace/Tiffani as “useful anomaly” and “load-bearing partnership” in clinical terms.\n- Forecast pressure in N02 without revealing mechanics or specific future events.\n\n**Allowed Knowledge:**\n- Can use sterile labels (subject, vessel, baseline, threshold) without explanation.\n- Must NOT reveal cosmology, motivations, or concrete spoilers.\n\n**Final Note:** All three segments must preserve N01 knowledge gates and tone constraints.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Tier-2 minor updates you said “do it” to (addenda; no changes forced into the locked dossiers/workflow)\n\n```md\n# N01 README / INDEX — 12-CHAPTER CANON SET\n\n## Authority order (highest → lowest)\n1) `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`, `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`, `Constraint_Systems.md`, `World_Core.md`, `Location_Bibles_N01.md`, voice guides, validator, failure modes, workflow.\n\n## Deprecation note\nAny older 15-chapter “blueprint” documents are legacy and must not be used for structure, Tide placement, or relationship timing when drafting N01.\n```\n\n```md\n# CHARACTER DOSSIERS — 12-CHAPTER REFERENCE MAP (ADDENDUM)\n\n**Purpose:** Keep `Character_Dossiers_N01.md` untouched while giving chapter-number anchors that match the 12-ch grid.\n\n## Jhace Torrins — chapter anchors\n- Ch 1: baseline vigilance + Bram dynamic\n- Ch 3: first Soulpulse onset; Chloen rescue; collapse guilt seed\n- Ch 4: failed test + triage success; cost baseline becomes visible\n- Ch 6: first choreographed field hold with Tiffani; cost accumulation\n- Ch 8: resistance → acceptance via proof\n- Ch 10: near-threshold overextension; overridden by Tiffani\n- Ch 11: trauma exchange; operational commitment\n- Ch 12: public principles; shared tremor acceptance\n\n## Tiffani Merrow — chapter anchors\n- Ch 2: baseline precision + Pella support; Piercing detection\n- Ch 5: damage/cost mapping; refuses institutional comfort/capture\n- Ch 7: exponential cost curve; decision to confront with proof\n- Ch 9: co-option offer; chooses Jhace over safety (explicit action)\n- Ch 10–12: shared POV arc: override → vulnerability → public principles\n\n## Bram Kelter — chapter anchors\n- Ch 1–4: primary “stop” voice and witness; stabilizing presence around early cost\n- Later chapters: present as appropriate but not structurally required by the grid\n\n## Pella — chapter anchors\n- Ch 2, 5, 7, 9: ongoing care-actions that keep Tiffani human-readable under stress\n\n## Corlexi / Mobel / Auditor — anchors\n- I-1: Corlexi (Form lens) after Ch 7\n- I-2: Mobel (Purity lens) after Ch 10\n- Epilogue: Auditor after Ch 12\n```\n\n```md\n# PROSE GENERATION WORKFLOW — 12-CHAPTER PATCH (ADDENDUM)\n\n**Purpose:** Keep `N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md` intact while overriding any legacy 15-ch assumptions.\n\n## Replace any “15-chapter” assumptions with:\n- 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue = 15 segments total\n- Interlude placement: I-1 after Ch 7; I-2 after Ch 10; Epilogue after Ch 12\n- Timeline: Tide 0 → Tide 20 (+ epilogue at 20+)\n\n## Before drafting any scene:\n1) Open `N01_Chapter_Grid.md` and confirm: POV, Tide range, beat method, Soulpulse count, relationship state.\n2) Cross-check `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md` for stage-state consistency and off-page gaps (Tide 9 and Tide 13).\n3) Use `N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md` to verify you aren’t smuggling in extra Soulpulse uses or skipping relationship states.\n\n## Locked-chapter guardrail\nCh 1–5 are locked in the grid. Revisions may edit prose quality, but must not change:\n- POV, Tide, beat method, Soulpulse count, or relationship state.\n```\n\nIf you want, next I can also generate a **diff-style checklist** (“search this phrase / replace with that phrase”) specifically for updating any remaining legacy docs that still mention **Salee interlude placement** or **15-ch Tide blocks**, since those are the two most common mismatch points with the 12-ch grid fileciteturn25file12 fileciteturn25file2."
