【3†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file3\nContent Snippet:\n# N01 - THE GRIP THAT HOLDS\n**Prose Generation Bible**\n\n## NOVELLA STRUCTURE\n\n- 12 chapters (1,200-2,200 words each)\n- 2 interludes (600-900 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,000 words)\n- Timeline: Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20\n- Setting: Saint Merro, Te'Oga's Crown\n\n---\n\n## TIMELINE\n\n**Tide 0:** Piercing event (Chapter 3)  \n**Tides 1-5:** Immediate aftermath, first Soulpulse uses  \n**Tides 6-10:** Partnership formation, coordinated abilities  \n**Tides 11-15:** Political pressure escalates  \n**Tides 16-20:** Commitment solidifies, future planning\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER SUMMARIES\n\n### CHAPTER 1: \"Something Wrong\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,240 words  \n**POV:** Jhace (tight third-person limited)  \n**Tide:** 0, Kindling (morning of Piercing entry)  \n**Setting:** Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection tunnel\n\nJhace works the Underface by feel—load, hum, stress—catching a wrong note in stone and glass that refuses to resolve. Bram needles him for working alone, but the teasing sits on an older truth: Jhace trusts structures more than people. Above, the city's supports feel tired; below, water and grit talk too loudly. The Tide's light shifts toward Kindling's end and the Underface grows tense—workers rushing, voices sharpened. Jhace marks cracks, makes tiny repairs that should be enough, then finds that \"enough\" won't hold. A tremor rolls through, not the worst, but wrong in rhythm. He climbs toward street level, trying to place the pattern in his mind. The chapter ends with him hearing the city like a living thing—holding its breath.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Jhace's craft mastery and pre-existing tremor\n- Establish 23 names burden (guilt from previous failure)\n- Show isolated working style\n- Foreshadow structural problems\n- Bram as mentor/caretaker figure\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,400 words  \n**POV:** Tiffani (tight third-person limited)  \n**Tide:** 0, Pouring  \n**Setting:** Observatory platform, Saint Merro rim\n\nTiffani measures the sky as if measurement can keep it honest. On an observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim, she tracks distortions and pressure shifts with obsessive vigilance. Pella—grounded, practical—keeps her fed, warm, and tethered to the present without insulting her fear. Tiffani's trauma surfaces as ritual: re-check, re-check, re-check. The air's refraction bends in ways her instruments can't comfortably name, and the horizon looks too thin. As the Tide moves toward Pouring, the sky's line fractures—first as a subtle wrongness, then as an event. Tiffani catches the first usable data of the world changing and realizes the numbers are not just \"bad.\" They're unprecedented. The exit begins.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Tiffani's measurement expertise\n- Establish 23 deaths trauma (parallel to Jhace)\n- Show Pella as support network\n- Foreshadow Piercing exit\n- Measurement as coping mechanism\n\n---\n# 【4†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file4\nContent Snippet:\nThey refuse publicly. Refusal makes enemies—and makes their bond real.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Institutional capture attempt (Corvin, Tellis)\n- Tiffani's first true consultation with Jhace\n- Public refusal creates political enemies\n- Choice defines values over safety\n- Bond solidifies through shared risk\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 10: \"Near Collapse\"\n**Word Target:** ~2,000 words  \n**POV:** Alternating Jhace/Tiffani  \n**Tides:** 18-19  \n**Setting:** Major support line failure\n\nMajor support line threatens to go—too many weakened buildings leaning into each other like drunks. Jhace and Tiffani arrive to crowd, shouting, panic, officials trying to seize control. They coordinate anyway: she clears space and calls sequence; he makes contact where it matters most.\n\nPulse is stronger than planned because structure is worse than reported. Copper-gold floods, revealing terrifying web of stress; Jhace holds it long enough for evacuation and shoring—but cost spikes: tremor becomes full-body shaking, vision narrowing, pain blooming through arms and chest. He nearly collapses in the open.\n\nTiffani catches him before he hits stone, furious and frightened in equal measure. They escape site with hold intact and Jhace barely conscious, victory tasting like ash.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Largest Soulpulse use to date\n- Cost approaches Stage 2 threshold\n- Public near-collapse (vulnerability exposed)\n- Tiffani's fear for him spikes\n- Success feels like failure\n\n---\n\n### INTERLUDE II: \"Hinges\"\n**Word Target:** ~750 words  \n**POV:** Mobel (observational third-person)  \n**Tides:** 19-20  \n**Setting:** Entry-side authority center\n\nOn entry-side, Mobel lives inside pain. Shard in his leg becomes teacher: every throb is lesson in separation—what should remain and what must be cut away. He views world through sharpening lens, judging without intending to: weak structures, weak people, weak doctrine. He doesn't name it anything special; he names it clarity.\n\nIn moment of stillness, he becomes aware that something else is moving—another pressure, another intent—not here, but across world's curve. Awareness is not conversation, just clash of directional certainty.\n\nMobel decides that whatever is happening, it must be cleansed into an answer. Interlude ends with him stepping toward authority, not as servant, but as blade seeking handle.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Mobel's cleansing bias (Purity-aligned, unconscious)\n- Pain as teacher metaphor\n- No awareness of being unusual\n- Foreshadow ideological collision\n- Awareness of \"other pressure\" (Corlexi, unknowingly)\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,700 words  \n**POV:** Jhace  \n**Tide:** 20  \n**Setting:** Recovery space, forge\n\nAfter near-collapse, relationship stops pretending it's only work. Jhace's recovery is ugly: shaking, nausea, pain-laced sleep, fear that next pulse will take something permanent. Tiffani is forced into caretaking without losing her edge; Pella and Bram support without crowding.\n\nIn quiet, Jhace admits he can't keep doing this without her—without the method, and without human anchor. Tiffani admits she can't keep treating him as data source without becoming monster.\n\nThey become lovers not as reward, but as pact: if they're going to risk bodies and reputations, they won't do it half-alive. They set rules for cost management and agree to face political consequences together.\n# 【5†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file5\nContent Snippet:\n**Key Beats:**\n- Tiffani presents evidence (his cost curve)\n- Jhace's defensive resistance (control/autonomy threatened)\n- Her cutting through: stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks\n- Tension: care vs. pride\n- Proposal: test efficiency method\n- Controlled test succeeds\n- His grudging acceptance: she makes him better\n- Exit: agreement to coordinate on next real crisis\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 controlled test (reduced cost confirmed with her targeting)\n\n**Relationship:** Resistance → acceptance that partnership improves both\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 stable, cost reduction possible with coordination\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 10-12\n- **Setting:** Glass Quarter refugee camp, Crown administrative contact\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,700 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nCorvin (Mason faction) and Tellis (Water Guild) arrive at Glass Quarter offering \"help\" in exchange for political alignment. Tiffani calculates: resources needed, cost is autonomy. Logic says accept. But she checks variable: Jhace will see this as betrayal, and his trust affects coordination. Deeper: she wants his trust (not logical, but real). She pulls him aside, presents logic without asking permission. He asks: \"What do you want?\" She answers: keep people alive without becoming Corvin's tool (contradictory goals in his framework). Jhace: \"Then don't join him. I won't be vulnerable alone.\" They publicly refuse Corvin. Political consequence: now enemies of powerful faction. Personal consequence: they chose each other over safety. First explicit choice of partnership over pragmatism.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Corvin/Tellis offer (resources for control)\n- Tiffani's calculation (logic vs. feeling)\n- She consults Jhace (first time asking partner input)\n- His question: what do YOU want?\n- Her honest answer: contradictory desires\n- His commitment: vulnerable together\n- Public refusal of institutional power\n- Exit: political enemies made, personal bond strengthened\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (political chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** First explicit choice of each other over safety/pragmatism\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stable Stage 1 with coordinated cost management\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 10: \"Near Collapse\"**\n- **POV:** Both (alternating perspective within chapter)\n- **Tide:** 14-15\n- **Setting:** Major structural crisis at Saint Merro main support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (External Threat → Narrow Escape)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n# 【6†N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md†file-CAd4CywpTdBkiKM88Yt3Ti】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file6\nContent Snippet:\nno direct relationships      |\n\n(Titles can change; functional roles should not.)\n\n---\n\n### 2.                                 | Auditor tags Stage 1 baseline & trajectory|\n\n---\n\n### 3. File begins. Someone with institutional power—careful hands, careful language—compiles first real ledger of \"resonant stabilization.\" Names recorded alongside locations, effects, cost symptoms.\n\nReport frames phenomenon as resource and threat. Line drawn between \"public safety\" and \"unlicensed interference.\"\n\nOutside window, Saint Merro creaks; inside, policy is forged from fear.\n# 【8†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file8\nContent Snippet:\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Jhace aware of Tiffani by reputation only; judges her as rigid perfectionist\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Pouring (mid-morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,280 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani measures atmospheric distortion from her observatory, detecting the incoming Piercing object through theodolite readings. She has been awake for days, driven by perfectionism rooted in Marisse's death. Pella brings food and scolds her for not resting. Measurements show descent trajectory—object is not passing over, it's coming down. First tremor hits. White line appears in sky. Tiffani and Pella evacuate as roar begins.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Mirrors Ch 1 structure (parallel observation)\n- Establishes Tiffani's precision methodology\n- Pella introduces care/support dynamic\n- Marisse trauma implied through vigilance pattern\n- Atmospheric distortion measurements (first scientific data)\n- Exit as sky splits\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n**Relationship:** Tiffani aware of Jhace by reputation only; judges him as overextending ego-driven builder\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 3: \"The Sky Breaks\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Striking (Piercing event)\n- **Setting:** Underface tunnel, then street level at building support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Word Count:** 2,200 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nPiercing hits. Underface collapses. Chloen pinned under beam. Jhace's first Soulpulse use (desperate protective need + contact = activation). Beam lightens enough to free her; side-effect healing visible (copper-gold on bruised flesh, breaks knitting). Cost: tremor, vein-lines, pain. They escape to surface. Second Soulpulse use on failing building support (people in doorway). Jhace holds long enough for evacuation. Greater cost: worse tremor, vision narrowing, collapse. Wakes to Tiffani observing him with logbook, asking \"What did you do?\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens mid-catastrophe (wall moves before Jhace does)\n- Chloen trapped, Jhace's trauma activated (23 names callback)\n- First Soulpulse: contact + desperate need = copper-gold glow\n- Side-effect healing shown but not explained\n- Surface escape, white line visible overhead\n- Second Soulpulse on building support\n- Cost escalation: tremor → vein-lines → collapse\n- J/T meeting: analytical demand vs. exhausted answer\n\n**Soulpulse:** 2 uses (beam hold, building support)\n- Requirements confirmed: skin contact + desperate protective emotion\n- Visuals: copper-gold glow, spreads into material, traces stress lines\n- Cost: heat in palms during, tremor/vein-lines/pain/exhaustion after\n# 【9†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file9\nContent Snippet:\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n- Ch 1-2: Stage 0 (pre-Piercing)\n- Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset\n- Ch 4-9: Stage 1 baseline\n- Ch 10: Approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs)\n- Ch 11-12: Stage 1 stabilized with management\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION TRACKING\n# 【10†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file10\nContent Snippet:\nTiffani calculates: resources needed, cost is autonomy. Logic says accept. But she checks variable: Jhace will see this as betrayal, and his trust affects coordination. Deeper: she wants his trust (not logical, but real). She pulls him aside, presents logic without asking permission. He asks: \"What do you want?\" She answers: keep people alive without becoming Corvin's tool (contradictory goals in his framework). Jhace: \"Then don't join him. I won't be vulnerable alone.\" They publicly refuse Corvin. Political consequence: now enemies of powerful faction. Personal consequence: they chose each other over safety. First explicit choice of partnership over pragmatism.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Corvin/Tellis offer (resources for control)\n- Tiffani's calculation (logic vs. feeling)\n- She consults Jhace (first time asking partner input)\n- His question: what do YOU want?\n- Her honest answer: contradictory desires\n- His commitment: vulnerable together\n- Public refusal of institutional power\n- Exit: political enemies made, personal bond strengthened\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (political chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** First explicit choice of each other over safety/pragmatism\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stable Stage 1 with coordinated cost management\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 10: \"Near Collapse\"**\n- **POV:** Both (alternating perspective within chapter)\n- **Tide:** 14-15\n- **Setting:** Major structural crisis at Saint Merro main support\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (External Threat → Narrow Escape)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nCritical main support failing during high-traffic period. Hundreds at risk. Jhace and Tiffani coordinate under pressure: she calculates load paths in real-time, he applies force to her exact specifications. They move as single unit, no wasted words, pure operational sync. Success—but Jhace pushes past safe limits to ensure no one dies. Cost: approaches Stage 2 threshold (tremor violent, vein-lines permanent at baseline, brief moment of sensory overload). Tiffani physically restrains him from continuing, makes decision FOR him: \"You're done. I don't care if you hate me for it.\" Saves him by overriding his control. Afterward, his rage at her presumption vs. her cold certainty she was right. Tension peaks: control vs. care, autonomy vs. survival.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis: hundreds at risk, main support failing\n- Perfect coordination under pressure\n- Jhace pushes past limits (caretaker compulsion)\n- Tiffani's override: physical intervention\n- Success but near-Stage 2 cost\n- His rage: she took his choice\n- Her certainty: he would have killed himself\n- Relationship stress: care as control\n- Exit: silent fury and mutual recognition they need each other\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 major use (near-Stage 2 threshold)\n- Coordinated efficiency maintains, but scale drives cost\n- Brief sensory overload (Stage 2 warning sign)\n- Vein-lines now faintly visible at rest\n\n**Relationship:** Conflict through care—she saves him by controlling him, he hates needing it\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 → approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs present)\n\n---\n# 【11†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file11\nContent Snippet:\n# N01: THE GRIP THAT HOLDS - CHAPTER GRID (12-CHAPTER STRUCTURE)\n**REVISED EDITION - MANDATORY REFERENCE FOR ALL PROSE GENERATION**\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL NOTICE\n\n**THIS GRID MUST BE CONSULTED BEFORE:**\n- Writing any new prose\n- Revising any existing prose\n- Making structural decisions\n- Adjusting beat assignments\n- Moving content between chapters\n\n**ANY DEVIATION FROM THIS GRID REQUIRES EXPLICIT AUTHORIZATION AND GRID UPDATE.**\n\n---\n\n## NOVELLA PARAMETERS\n\n- **Novella ID:** N01\n- **Title:** The Grip That Holds\n- **Turning:** 1 - GLASS (Fragile formation, first awakening)\n- **Acrostic Letter:** G (first novella of GLASS)\n- **Structure:** 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue = 15 segments\n- **Word Count Target:** 18,000-21,000 words total\n- **Chapter Target Range:** 1,200-2,200 words (variance permitted for beat requirements)\n- **Timeline Covered:** Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20 (~20 days post-Piercing)\n\n---\n\n## ACT STRUCTURE\n\n### **ACT I: THE CUT (Chapters 1-4)**\n- **Function:** Crisis establishes characters, introduces Soulpulse, first contact\n- **Word Count:** ~9,170 words (45% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 4\n- **Emotional Arc:** Low → Low → High → Medium\n\n### **ACT II: THE PATTERN (Chapters 5-9)**\n- **Function:** Partnership forms, patterns emerge, institutional pressure builds\n- **Word Count:** ~8,900 words (43% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 5 (includes Interlude I after Ch 7)\n- **Emotional Arc:** Medium → Medium → Low → Medium → Medium\n\n### **ACT III: THE GRIP (Chapters 10-12)**\n- **Function:** Crisis tests bond, commitment made, foundation locked\n- **Word Count:** ~5,200 words (25% of novella)\n- **Chapters:** 3 (includes Interlude II after Ch 10)\n- **Emotional Arc:** High → High → Medium\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER GRID\n\n### **CHAPTER 1: \"Something Wrong\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 0, Kindling (morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection run\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,240 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\nJhace inspects heat-grid junctions in his ritualistic pre-Piercing routine. He detects an \"off-note\" in the grid hum—pressure deviation signaling potential failure. Bram teases him about working alone and obsessive checking patterns. Ground tremor hits. Jhace feels atmospheric change (copper tang, pressure shift). Chapter establishes his trauma-driven vigilance and introduces Tiffani through guild gossip as \"the glassworker with impossible standards.\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens with wrong hum detection (immediate sensory hook)\n- Establishes Underface setting and Jhace's expertise\n- Bram introduces caretaker/control theme\n- First tremor (foreshadowing)\n- Ends with Jhace at street level, sensing wrongness\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n# 【12†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: Jhace feels atmospheric change (copper tang, pressure shift). Chapter establishes his trauma-driven vigilance and introduces Tiffani through guild gossip as \"the glassworker with impossible standards.\"\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Opens with wrong hum detection (immediate sensory hook)\n- Establishes Underface setting and Jhace's expertise\n- Bram introduces caretaker/control theme\n- First tremor (foreshadowing)\n- Ends with Jhace at street level, sensing wrongness\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n# 【15†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file15\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Corlexi reviews stabilization reports, sees Order taking shape through RRO formation. She feels satisfaction at frameworks solidifying but also unease—something escapes her control. The smith and glassworker refuse to be contained, building third path outside her geometry. Second segment: She experiences her Principle (Form) as compulsion to impose structure, feels nascent god-awareness stirring (vast power constrained by single lens). She makes note: must address the anomaly in Saint Merro. Closing: Auditor observes Corlexi's awakening, notes Form's limitation—cannot tolerate things outside patterns.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm technique (god-vessel perception)\n- Principle bias manifestation (everything seen through Form lens)\n- Innocent frame maintained (no recognition of divinity)\n- Jhace/Tiffani recognized as structural threat\n- Auditor frame at open/close\n\n**Purpose:** Establish divine forces moving, show limitation of single-Principle perspective, foreshadow institutional pressure in Act III\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 8: \"Proof and Resistance\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 7-8\n- **Setting:** Jhace's forge, then joint inspection of hold sites\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_12 (Relationship Shift → Power Dynamic Change)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani arrives at forge with compiled data, shows Jhace his cost curve projection. He resists—doesn't want to see himself as failing, doesn't trust numbers over experience. She challenges: \"You trust stone to tell truth. These marks are stone's truth about what you're doing to yourself.\" Tension: his defensive walls vs. her refusal to let him self-destruct through ignorance. She proposes operational change: she identifies critical points, he applies minimal force, they test if efficiency reduces cost. He agrees grudgingly—not because he believes her, but because her logic is sound even if he hates it. They test method on controlled scenario. It works. Cost is measurably lower. His recognition: accepting her help isn't weakness, it's competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Tiffani presents evidence (his cost curve)\n- Jhace's defensive resistance (control/autonomy threatened)\n- Her cutting through: stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks\n- Tension: care vs. pride\n- Proposal: test efficiency method\n- Controlled test succeeds\n- His grudging acceptance: she makes him better\n- Exit: agreement to coordinate on next real crisis\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 controlled test (reduced cost confirmed with her targeting)\n\n**Relationship:** Resistance → acceptance that partnership improves both\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 stable, cost reduction possible with coordination\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 10-12\n- **Setting:** Glass Quarter refugee camp, Crown administrative contact\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,700 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nCorvin (Mason faction) and Tellis (Water Guild) arrive at Glass Quarter offering \"help\" in exchange for political alignment. Tiffani calculates: resources needed, cost is autonomy. Logic says accept. But she checks variable: Jhace will see this as betrayal, and his trust affects coordination. Deeper: she wants his trust (not logical, but real).\n# 【16†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file16\nContent Snippet:\nJhace pushes back against being managed. In his forge, he frames Tiffani's methodology as control, intrusion into the one place he still feels competent. Tiffani refuses to soothe him. Stone doesn't lie, she says—neither do his tremors.\n\nTheir conflict sharpens into honesty: he's terrified she'll reduce him to diagram; she's terrified he'll die to prove he can do it alone. She proposes small test—controlled contact, minimal pulse, measured outcome—to separate \"miracle\" from repeatable method.\n\nTest works. Glow follows targeted stress-lines, fades cleanly. Jhace still pays (heat, shaking), but payment is smaller, survivable. Chapter ends with Jhace conceding truth he hates: she makes him better at holding. Tiffani concedes truth she fears: she cares enough to fight him for it.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Conflict over methodology vs autonomy\n- Controlled test proves efficiency possible\n- Mutual vulnerability admission\n- Partnership deepens through honesty\n- Still not explicitly romantic\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,500 words  \n**POV:** Tiffani  \n**Tides:** 15-17  \n**Setting:** Administrative meeting, street confrontation\n\nStreets get political. Corvin and Tellis offer resources—access, guards, material—at price of ownership: registration, exclusive deployment, right to decide which districts deserve holding.\n\nTiffani is tempted; safety is seductive when ground won't stop moving. She asks Jhace for input—first time instead of assuming she's right. His question is simple and brutal: what do you want, underneath the fear?\n\nTiffani admits contradictions: she wants safety, recognition, and to stay clean of complicity—things that don't fit together. Jhace chooses her anyway, not as romantic flourish, but as shared risk: if they take offer, they become weapon.\n\nThey refuse publicly. Refusal makes enemies—and makes their bond real.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Institutional capture attempt (Corvin, Tellis)\n- Tiffani's first true consultation with Jhace\n- Public refusal creates political enemies\n- Choice defines values over safety\n- Bond solidifies through shared risk\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 10: \"Near Collapse\"\n**Word Target:** ~2,000 words  \n**POV:** Alternating Jhace/Tiffani  \n**Tides:** 18-19  \n**Setting:** Major support line failure\n\nMajor support line threatens to go—too many weakened buildings leaning into each other like drunks. Jhace and Tiffani arrive to crowd, shouting, panic, officials trying to seize control. They coordinate anyway: she clears space and calls sequence; he makes contact where it matters most.\n\nPulse is stronger than planned because structure is worse than reported. Copper-gold floods, revealing terrifying web of stress; Jhace holds it long enough for evacuation and shoring—but cost spikes: tremor becomes full-body shaking, vision narrowing, pain blooming through arms and chest. He nearly collapses in the open.\n\nTiffani catches him before he hits stone, furious and frightened in equal measure. They escape site with hold intact and Jhace barely conscious, victory tasting like ash.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Largest Soulpulse use to date\n- Cost approaches Stage 2 threshold\n- Public near-collapse (vulnerability exposed)\n- Tiffani's fear for him spikes\n- Success feels like failure\n\n---\n\n### INTERLUDE II: \"Hinges\"\n**Word Target:** ~750 words  \n**POV:** Mobel (observational third-person)  \n**Tides:** 19-20  \n**Setting:** Entry-side authority center\n# 【17†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file17\nContent Snippet:\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Jhace's craft mastery and pre-existing tremor\n- Establish 23 names burden (guilt from previous failure)\n- Show isolated working style\n- Foreshadow structural problems\n- Bram as mentor/caretaker figure\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,400 words  \n**POV:** Tiffani (tight third-person limited)  \n**Tide:** 0, Pouring  \n**Setting:** Observatory platform, Saint Merro rim\n\nTiffani measures the sky as if measurement can keep it honest. On an observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim, she tracks distortions and pressure shifts with obsessive vigilance. Pella—grounded, practical—keeps her fed, warm, and tethered to the present without insulting her fear. Tiffani's trauma surfaces as ritual: re-check, re-check, re-check. The air's refraction bends in ways her instruments can't comfortably name, and the horizon looks too thin. As the Tide moves toward Pouring, the sky's line fractures—first as a subtle wrongness, then as an event. Tiffani catches the first usable data of the world changing and realizes the numbers are not just \"bad.\" They're unprecedented. The exit begins.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Tiffani's measurement expertise\n- Establish 23 deaths trauma (parallel to Jhace)\n- Show Pella as support network\n- Foreshadow Piercing exit\n- Measurement as coping mechanism\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 3: \"The Sky Breaks\"\n**Word Target:** ~2,200 words  \n**POV:** Alternating Jhace/Tiffani  \n**Tide:** 0, Shaping (Piercing exit moment)  \n**Setting:** Underface tunnel, observatory platform, street-level chaos\n\nCatastrophe arrives mid-breath. In the Underface tunnel, a wall moves before Jhace can decide to move; the city becomes weight without permission. Chloen is trapped. Jhace's old ledger of names—those he couldn't hold—spikes through him as panic. He braces a beam with his body, then something answers the desperation: copper-gold glow floods his palms and runs into the structure like heat finding a seam. The beam holds—barely. He uses it again at street level, pressing both hands to a failing support while the building's stress-lines reveal themselves like veins of light. The cost hits hard: tremor intensification, pain, heat-lash up his arms, collapse to his knees.\n\nAt the observatory, Tiffani watches the exit happen—a hole appearing in reality. Glass shatters around her. She presses palms to a fracturing window pane and copper-gold light responds, threading through the cracks, holding them stable long enough for evacuation. The cost is immediate: tremor in both hands, heat burning up her forearms.\n\nIn the chaos, they meet for the first time—not as comfort, but as demand: she needs to know what happened, how, and what it cost. Their first exchange is competence colliding with exhaustion.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Piercing exit catastrophe\n- Jhace's first Soulpulse use (saves Chloen)\n- Tiffani's first Soulpulse use (stabilizes glass)\n- Visible costs establish baseline (tremor, heat, pain)\n- First meeting: competence-driven, not romantic\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 4: \"After the Shudder\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,500 words  \n**POV:** Jhace  \n**Tide:** 0, Striking (hours after Piercing)  \n**Setting:** Collapse sites, street-level triage\n# 【18†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file18\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Assessment of characters: Jhace unpredictable because care is absolute, Tiffani unpredictable because precision is absolute, both useful because they might survive what's coming. Interest in their bond: they love functionally (load-bearing systems), not romantically. Remove one, other fails. This is useful—when gods move and alliances fracture, bond will save or destroy them. Either way, worth observing. Decision: flag them as anomaly, predict system will move to crush or co-opt. Notes that Turning 1's hope phase shading toward devastation, their bond potential fulcrum or liability. If love makes people break rules, it reshapes wars—or ruins plans. Sets stage for N02 escalation.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n# 【19†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn18file19\nContent Snippet:\n### **CHAPTER 6: \"Weight That Won't Yield\"**\n- **POV:** Jhace (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 3-4\n- **Setting:** Major structural failure at Saint Merro junction\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_03 (Crisis Front-Loaded → Sustained Pressure → Narrow Success)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 3\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium-High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,800 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nMulti-story arch threatening collapse. Jhace attempts traditional propping—insufficient. Tiffani arrives with survey data, sketches stress lines on stone (showing him geometry of what he feels). They coordinate without planning: she marks three critical points, he focuses Soulpulse on those exact locations. First true choreographed hold. Success but greater cost: tremor worsens, vein-lines persist longer. Tiffani critiques his \"inefficient\" force application—her precision could reduce his cost by 30%. Jhace's reaction: (1) she's critiquing me, (2) she's right, (3) she sees my inefficiency and wants to fix it. Partnership recognition through competence.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Crisis opening: arch failing, people still inside\n- Jhace's traditional methods insufficient\n- Tiffani arrives with data/geometry\n- First coordinated Soulpulse (she marks, he powers)\n- Success with visible cost increase\n- Her critique of his technique (care disguised as analysis)\n- His recognition: she sees him, not his heroism\n- Exit: they've choreographed perfectly without planning\n\n**Soulpulse:** 1 coordinated use (arch stabilization)\n- Demonstrates improved targeting reduces cost (her geometry + his power)\n- Still significant cost: tremor persistent, vein-lines linger\n\n**Relationship:** First true partnership action—coordination through shared competence\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 progressing, cost accumulation visible\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 7: \"Quiet Calculations\"**\n- **POV:** Tiffani (tight third)\n- **Tide:** 5-6\n- **Setting:** Tiffani's workshop/planning space\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,500 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nTiffani compiles data from multiple hold sites, building predictive model for failure patterns. She discovers Jhace's cost curve is exponential not linear—he's approaching breaking point faster than he realizes. Pella asks why she cares so much about \"the man with the glow.\" Tiffani's answer: precision requires complete data, and he's destroying the most interesting variable. But underneath: she's starting to see him as person, not just phenomenon. She calculates\nVisible: 0% - 99%",

          "result": "# 【0†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file0\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Mobel leads cleansing ritual where early resonants prove themselves through enduring pain without using Soulpulse. She feels power of her Principle—clarity, judgment, separation of righteous from corrupt. Second segment: Moment of god-awareness—she senses Form-aligned power across planet (Corlexi stirring). Recognition: there are others like her, alliance or war inevitable. Principle limitation shown: sees only absolutes, cannot tolerate ambiguity. Auditor observes: Purity will clash with Form's institutional approach, both will target Jhace/Tiffani's third path.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm (pain as purification)\n- Purity Principle bias (absolute judgment)\n- First inter-god awareness\n- Innocent frame (no recognition of divinity)\n- Foreshadows ideological warfare\n- Auditor frame\n\n**Purpose:** Establish second divine force, show Principles in conflict, set up multi-front pressure on protagonists\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"**\n- **POV:** Both (intimate alternating perspective)\n- **Tide:** 17-18\n- **Setting:** Quiet space after crisis (Jhace's forge or Tiffani's workshop)\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nPost-Ch10 processing. Jhace's rage has cooled to exhaustion. Tiffani's certainty has cracked to fear—she nearly watched him die. Neither apologizes (both were right, both were wrong). Instead: vulnerability exchange. Jhace tells her about the building collapse that killed his parents, the 23 names he carries, why he can't choose who to save (it's choosing who dies). Tiffani tells him about Marisse, the miscalculation, why precision is penance not preference. Recognition: they're both trying to atone for deaths they couldn't prevent. She says: \"I need you to survive this.\" He says: \"I need you to keep stopping me.\" Not love confession—operational truth. They are load-bearing for each other. Moment of physical intimacy (forehead touch, hand hold—first deliberate contact not in crisis). Commitment without words.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Post-crisis exhaustion (defenses down)\n- Trauma exchange: his 23 names, her Marisse\n- Recognition: both atoning for unprevented deaths\n- Mutual need acknowledged (operational, not romantic)\n- Physical intimacy: first non-crisis contact\n- Commitment: she keeps him honest, he trusts her override\n- Exit: understanding they can't survive apart\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (recovery/intimacy chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Trauma-bonding → mutual recognition of load-bearing function\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 recovering with rest, but baseline effects permanent\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"**\n- **POV:** Both (balanced perspective)\n- **Tide:** 19-20\n- **Setting:** Rebuilt overlook above Saint Merro, forge wall\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nWeeks after immediate crisis. Jhace and Tiffani walk their district at Quench, seeing uneven results: some structures beautifully reinforced, others held by temporary measures, some families back in homes, others permanently displaced.\n# 【1†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md†file-PiHfF5pTdyZRbpfxaBSkbZ】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file1\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nTwo-segment structure. First segment: Mobel leads cleansing ritual where early resonants prove themselves through enduring pain without using Soulpulse. She feels power of her Principle—clarity, judgment, separation of righteous from corrupt. Second segment: Moment of god-awareness—she senses Form-aligned power across planet (Corlexi stirring). Recognition: there are others like her, alliance or war inevitable. Principle limitation shown: sees only absolutes, cannot tolerate ambiguity. Auditor observes: Purity will clash with Form's institutional approach, both will target Jhace/Tiffani's third path.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm (pain as purification)\n- Purity Principle bias (absolute judgment)\n- First inter-god awareness\n- Innocent frame (no recognition of divinity)\n- Foreshadows ideological warfare\n- Auditor frame\n\n**Purpose:** Establish second divine force, show Principles in conflict, set up multi-front pressure on protagonists\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 11: \"Holding Together\"**\n- **POV:** Both (intimate alternating perspective)\n- **Tide:** 17-18\n- **Setting:** Quiet space after crisis (Jhace's forge or Tiffani's workshop)\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** High\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nPost-Ch10 processing. Jhace's rage has cooled to exhaustion. Tiffani's certainty has cracked to fear—she nearly watched him die. Neither apologizes (both were right, both were wrong). Instead: vulnerability exchange. Jhace tells her about the building collapse that killed his parents, the 23 names he carries, why he can't choose who to save (it's choosing who dies). Tiffani tells him about Marisse, the miscalculation, why precision is penance not preference. Recognition: they're both trying to atone for deaths they couldn't prevent. She says: \"I need you to survive this.\" He says: \"I need you to keep stopping me.\" Not love confession—operational truth. They are load-bearing for each other. Moment of physical intimacy (forehead touch, hand hold—first deliberate contact not in crisis). Commitment without words.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Post-crisis exhaustion (defenses down)\n- Trauma exchange: his 23 names, her Marisse\n- Recognition: both atoning for unprevented deaths\n- Mutual need acknowledged (operational, not romantic)\n- Physical intimacy: first non-crisis contact\n- Commitment: she keeps him honest, he trusts her override\n- Exit: understanding they can't survive apart\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (recovery/intimacy chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Trauma-bonding → mutual recognition of load-bearing function\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 recovering with rest, but baseline effects permanent\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"**\n- **POV:** Both (balanced perspective)\n- **Tide:** 19-20\n- **Setting:** Rebuilt overlook above Saint Merro, forge wall\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nWeeks after immediate crisis. Jhace and Tiffani walk their district at Quench, seeing uneven results: some structures beautifully reinforced, others held by temporary measures, some families back in homes, others permanently displaced.\n# 【2†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file2\nContent Snippet:\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Assessment of characters: Jhace unpredictable because care is absolute, Tiffani unpredictable because precision is absolute, both useful because they might survive what's coming. Interest in their bond: they love functionally (load-bearing systems), not romantically. Remove one, other fails. This is useful—when gods move and alliances fracture, bond will save or destroy them. Either way, worth observing. Decision: flag them as anomaly, predict system will move to crush or co-opt. Notes that Turning 1's hope phase shading toward devastation, their bond potential fulcrum or liability. If love makes people break rules, it reshapes wars—or ruins plans. Sets stage for N02 escalation.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Meta-perspective assessment\n- Character analysis (unpredictability as threat/asset)\n- Bond recognition (functional love, not romantic)\n- Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n\n- **Jhace Solo:** 5 chapters (1, 3, 4, 6, 8)\n- **Tiffani Solo:** 4 chapters (2, 5, 7, 9)\n- **Both/Shared:** 3 chapters (10, 11, 12)\n- **Other (Interludes/Epilogue):** 3 segments\n\n**Pattern:** Opens with alternating solo POVs (establishes both independently), moves toward shared POVs as partnership deepens (reflects relationship progression)\n\n---\n\n## BEAT METHOD DISTRIBUTION\n\n**Opening Exception:** BEAT_01 → BEAT_01 permitted for Ch 1–2 only to establish parallel baseline POVs through deliberate structural mirror.\n\n**Used in N01:**\n- BEAT_01: Ch 1, 2, 5 (Saga Beat 10)\n- BEAT_02: Ch 4 (Saga Beat 1)\n- BEAT_03: Ch 3, 6, 10 (Saga Beat 3)\n- BEAT_04: Ch 8 (Saga Beat 16)\n- BEAT_07: Ch 9 (Saga Beat 26)\n- BEAT_08: Ch 7, 11, 12 (Saga Beat 17 or 21 depending on intimacy vs. reflection emphasis)\n\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n# 【3†README.md†file-UHeggNd7WQHssWb89Y8SgW】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: README.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file3\nContent Snippet:\n# N01 – The Grip That Holds – Canonical Pack\n\nThis folder contains all canonical and structural files necessary for writing and revising the novella **N01: \"The Grip That Holds\"** in the Confluence Chronicles saga.\n\n## Files included\n\n- **World_Core.md** – Detailed description of Te’Oga world, city geography, Piercing event and effects, technology, social structures, time and measurement systems, and lexicon enforcement.\n- **Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md** – Empirical explanation of Soulpulse as understood during N01, including activation requirements, manifestations, cost stages, techniques, training observations, and limitations.\n- **Character_Dossiers_N01.md** – Psychological profiles, voice patterns, humor styles, and arcs for all characters appearing in N01: Jhace, Tiffani, Bram, and key supporting characters.\n- **Location_Bibles_N01.md** – Physical descriptions and functional details for locations in N01, including Underface tunnels, Saint Merro districts, RRO chambers, observatory, training rig, and typical crisis sites.\n- **Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md** – Tracks relationship arcs and phases (Jhace & Tiffani, Jhace & Bram, Tiffani & Pella, etc.) and maps character connections across chapters.\n- **Constraint_Systems.md** – Rules to enforce the Te’Oga lexicon, visible Soulpulse cost, knowledge gates, beat unpredictability, voice consistency, and validation—essential for maintaining continuity and immersion.\n- **N01_Master_Bible.md** – Comprehensive story bible summarising the novella overview, world state, characters, Soulpulse mechanics, relationships, chapter structure, themes, and constraints.\n- **N01_Chapter_Grid.md** – Beat-by-beat breakdown of all 15 chapters, plus interludes and epilogue, including scene structure, knowledge changes, and exit hooks.\n- **N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md** – Step-by-step instructions for drafting and revising prose, including modes (draft, revision, interlude, epilogue), common setup, and guidelines for structure and voice.\n- **N01_Validator_Checklist.md** – Mechanical validation checklist for any prose (chapter, interlude, or epilogue), ensuring lexicon compliance, cost visibility, voice discipline, knowledge gates, structural integrity, and timeline continuity.\n- **Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md** – Definitions and structures of all beat methods used in N01, with emotional arcs, best uses, and forbidden sequences.\n- **N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md** – Tide-by-Tide timeline for N01, listing event anchors, chapter phases, Soulpulse progression, relationship states by chapter, major events, and update rules.\n- **N01_Interludes_Epilogue_Specs.md** – Detailed guidelines for writing Interlude I, Interlude II, and the epilogue, specifying POV, tone, length, structure, allowed knowledge, and exit hooks.\n- **N01_Common_Failure_Modes.md** – A list of common mistakes in prose generation and validation, with detection methods and suggested fixes.\n- **N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md** – Concise tables summarising character presence per chapter, Soulpulse usage and cost by chapter, primary locations by chapter, and a tide-level timeline snapshot.\n\n## Notes\n\n- The orchestration notes (for human planners) are **not** included in this package. \n- The documents here represent the complete canonical and structural foundation for N01 without any meta-guidance. \n- Use these files to maintain consistency and fidelity when drafting or revising the novella.\n# 【4†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file4\nContent Snippet:\nTiffani tells him about Marisse, the miscalculation, why precision is penance not preference. Recognition: they're both trying to atone for deaths they couldn't prevent. She says: \"I need you to survive this.\" He says: \"I need you to keep stopping me.\" Not love confession—operational truth. They are load-bearing for each other. Moment of physical intimacy (forehead touch, hand hold—first deliberate contact not in crisis). Commitment without words.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Post-crisis exhaustion (defenses down)\n- Trauma exchange: his 23 names, her Marisse\n- Recognition: both atoning for unprevented deaths\n- Mutual need acknowledged (operational, not romantic)\n- Physical intimacy: first non-crisis contact\n- Commitment: she keeps him honest, he trusts her override\n- Exit: understanding they can't survive apart\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (recovery/intimacy chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Trauma-bonding → mutual recognition of load-bearing function\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 recovering with rest, but baseline effects permanent\n\n---\n\n### **CHAPTER 12: \"Choosing the Weight\"**\n- **POV:** Both (balanced perspective)\n- **Tide:** 19-20\n- **Setting:** Rebuilt overlook above Saint Merro, forge wall\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,600 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nWeeks after immediate crisis. Jhace and Tiffani walk their district at Quench, seeing uneven results: some structures beautifully reinforced, others held by temporary measures, some families back in homes, others permanently displaced. Every scar holds memory. They discuss larger currents: registries spreading, clinics multiplying, RRO expanding, black-market training proliferating. They know storm is forming beyond their neighborhood. What they can control: what they hold. At forge, they write three priorities on wall: (1) Truth over comfort, (2) People over institutions, (3) Keep each other honest about why we act. Vow without ceremony. Community members add their marks beside it—informal pact, local mandate. Not oath to Crown or RRO, but commitment to third path. Jhace's hand shakes as he writes; Tiffani laces their fingers, lets his tremor move through both. Physical reminder: cost and commitment shared. Exit: they face uncertain future together, foundation locked.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Walk through rebuilt district (uneven success)\n- Recognition of larger forces gathering\n- Decision: focus on what they can hold\n- Three priorities written on forge wall\n- Community witnesses/joins commitment\n- Shared physical moment (tremor through linked hands)\n- Acceptance: this is who they are together\n- Exit: foundation locked, future uncertain but faced together\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (commitment/resolution chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Partnership formalized through shared principles (not romance, but deeper)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline accepted as new permanent state\n\n---\n\n### **EPILOGUE: \"Auditor Observation\"**\n- **POV:** Auditor (meta-perspective outside time)\n- **Setting:** Auditor's observation point (undefined location)\n- **Word Count Target:** 800-1,000 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n# 【5†N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md†file-8PoxydrgLxJ555cHX31TBg】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Prose_Generation_Workflow.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file5\nContent Snippet:\n## N01 PROSE GENERATION WORKFLOW\n\n**Purpose:**  \nDefine a repeatable, enforceable process for generating and revising N01 prose so that:\n\n- All output stays within N01 scope.  \n- World, characters, and magic stay consistent.  \n- Cost, lexicon, and beat-structure rules are respected without a human babysitting every line.\n\nThis workflow assumes access to:\n\n- `N01_Master_Bible.md`\n- `World_Core.md`\n- `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`\n- `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`\n- `Location_Bibles_N01.md`\n- `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`\n- `Constraint_Systems.md`\n- `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`\n\n---\n\n### 1. WORK MODES\n\nThe engine operates in one of four modes:\n\n1. **Chapter Draft Mode**  \n   Generate a full prose draft for a numbered chapter (1–15) with a specified POV.\n\n2. **Chapter Revision Mode**  \n   Revise an existing chapter while preserving its beat, POV, and factual content.\n\n3. **Interlude Mode**  \n   Generate or revise Interlude I or II.\n\n4. **Epilogue Mode**  \n   Generate or revise the N01 Epilogue.\n\nEach mode uses the same constraints but slightly different steps.\n\n---\n\n### 2. COMMON SETUP (ALL MODES)\n\nBefore writing or revising anything:\n\n1. **Identify Assignment**\n   - Chapter number or Interlude/Epilogue.\n   - POV character.\n   - Target word count window (e.g., 1,000–1,200 for standard chapters).\n\n2. **Load Core Context**\n   - From `N01_Master_Bible.md`:\n     - Novella overview.\n     - Primary characters.\n     - Relationship dynamics.\n     - Constraints for N01 prose.\n   - From `World_Core.md`:\n     - Time system (Tides/Anneals/Phases).\n     - Lexicon constraints.\n     - Saint Merro districts.\n   - From `Soulpulse_System_N01_Scope.md`:\n     - Stage definitions.\n     - Known techniques.\n     - Cost patterns.\n   - From `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`:\n     - POV character psychology, voice, humor.\n   - From `Location_Bibles_N01.md`:\n     - Primary locations for the chapter’s setting(s).\n   - From `Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md`:\n     - Relationship state at this point in the novella.\n   - From `Constraint_Systems.md`:\n     - Lexicon rules.\n     - Cost visibility rules.\n     - Knowledge gates.\n     - Beat constraints.\n\n3. **Consult Chapter Grid**\n   - From `N01_Chapter_Grid.md`:\n     - Chapter title.\n     - POV.\n     - Beat method ID (BEAT_0X).\n     - Tide and phase (Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench).\n     - Location(s).\n     - External conflict.\n     - Internal shift.\n     - Relationship beat.\n     - Soulpulse usage (Y/N, type, approximate cost).\n     - Exit hook.\n     - Knowledge state changes.\n\n4. **Derive a One-Page Brief (Internal)**\n   For the current chapter, summarize in your own internal notes:\n   - **Situation When Chapter Opens.**\n   - **Main external problem.**\n   - **What changes internally for the POV by the end.**\n   - **What, if anything, changes in relationships.**\n   - **Whether Soulpulse is used, and at what cost.**\n   - **What the reader must know by the end (and what must stay hidden).**\n   - **Beat method and emotional arc you must follow.**\n# 【6†N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md†file-CAd4CywpTdBkiKM88Yt3Ti】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Quick_Reference_Tables.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file6\nContent Snippet:\n| Political confrontation                                    |\n| I2 | Dregs communal space                    | Community organizing lens                                  |\n| 12 | Neglected lower districts               | Moral decision about where help goes first                 |\n| 13 | Quiet, semi-private space (forge/back room/roof) | Emotional decompression & acknowledgment            |\n| 14 | Edge of multiple districts (load-bearing node) | High-stakes collapse prevention                       |\n| 15 | Quiet private space (rooftop/back room) | Commitment scene                                           |\n| E  | Unlocated abstraction                   | Auditor report space                                       |\n\n---\n\n### 4. TIDE-LEVEL TIMELINE SNAPSHOT\n\nApproximate; exact phase mapping lives in `N01_Timeline_Skeleton.md`.\n\n| Tide | Phase(s) Primarily Covered          | Chapters            | Key Events                                      |\n|------|-------------------------------------|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------|\n| 0    | Late Kindling → Striking/Quench    | 1–3                 | Pre-Piercing baseline → The Piercing → first uses |\n| 1    | Kindling → Striking                | 4–6 + Interlude I   | Immediate aftermath, first big hold, institutional view |\n| 2    | Kindling → Quench                  | 7–10                | Training loop, failures, synthesis, mature rescue |\n| 3    | Pouring → Striking                 | 11 + Interlude II   | Registration attempt, Salee’s communal lens     |\n| 4    | Shaping → Striking                 | 12–13               | Ethical triage decisions, romantic acknowledgment |\n| 5    | Striking → Quench                  | 14–15 + Epilogue    | Major crisis, Stage 2 threshold, quiet commitment, Auditor assessment |\n\nUse this as a sanity check: if a chapter implies more time passing than this allows, fix the references.\n\n---\n# 【7†n01-story-bible.md†file_00000000a41471fd87cc84aa11445703】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: n01-story-bible.md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file7\nContent Snippet:\n# N01 - THE GRIP THAT HOLDS\n**Prose Generation Bible**\n\n## NOVELLA STRUCTURE\n\n- 12 chapters (1,200-2,200 words each)\n- 2 interludes (600-900 words each)\n- 1 epilogue (800-1,000 words)\n- Timeline: Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20\n- Setting: Saint Merro, Te'Oga's Crown\n\n---\n\n## TIMELINE\n\n**Tide 0:** Piercing event (Chapter 3)  \n**Tides 1-5:** Immediate aftermath, first Soulpulse uses  \n**Tides 6-10:** Partnership formation, coordinated abilities  \n**Tides 11-15:** Political pressure escalates  \n**Tides 16-20:** Commitment solidifies, future planning\n\n---\n\n## CHAPTER SUMMARIES\n\n### CHAPTER 1: \"Something Wrong\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,240 words  \n**POV:** Jhace (tight third-person limited)  \n**Tide:** 0, Kindling (morning of Piercing entry)  \n**Setting:** Underface beneath Saint Merro, heat-grid inspection tunnel\n\nJhace works the Underface by feel—load, hum, stress—catching a wrong note in stone and glass that refuses to resolve. Bram needles him for working alone, but the teasing sits on an older truth: Jhace trusts structures more than people. Above, the city's supports feel tired; below, water and grit talk too loudly. The Tide's light shifts toward Kindling's end and the Underface grows tense—workers rushing, voices sharpened. Jhace marks cracks, makes tiny repairs that should be enough, then finds that \"enough\" won't hold. A tremor rolls through, not the worst, but wrong in rhythm. He climbs toward street level, trying to place the pattern in his mind. The chapter ends with him hearing the city like a living thing—holding its breath.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Jhace's craft mastery and pre-existing tremor\n- Establish 23 names burden (guilt from previous failure)\n- Show isolated working style\n- Foreshadow structural problems\n- Bram as mentor/caretaker figure\n\n---\n\n### CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"\n**Word Target:** ~1,400 words  \n**POV:** Tiffani (tight third-person limited)  \n**Tide:** 0, Pouring  \n**Setting:** Observatory platform, Saint Merro rim\n\nTiffani measures the sky as if measurement can keep it honest. On an observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim, she tracks distortions and pressure shifts with obsessive vigilance. Pella—grounded, practical—keeps her fed, warm, and tethered to the present without insulting her fear. Tiffani's trauma surfaces as ritual: re-check, re-check, re-check. The air's refraction bends in ways her instruments can't comfortably name, and the horizon looks too thin. As the Tide moves toward Pouring, the sky's line fractures—first as a subtle wrongness, then as an event. Tiffani catches the first usable data of the world changing and realizes the numbers are not just \"bad.\" They're unprecedented. The exit begins.\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Introduce Tiffani's measurement expertise\n- Establish 23 deaths trauma (parallel to Jhace)\n- Show Pella as support network\n- Foreshadow Piercing exit\n- Measurement as coping mechanism\n\n---\n# 【8†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md†file_00000000fd0871fd84281d676acb80ef】\nContent source: Source.file\nTitle: N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED (1).md\nCitation Marker: fileciteturn12file8\nContent Snippet:\nEvery scar holds memory. They discuss larger currents: registries spreading, clinics multiplying, RRO expanding, black-market training proliferating. They know storm is forming beyond their neighborhood. What they can control: what they hold. At forge, they write three priorities on wall: (1) Truth over comfort, (2) People over institutions, (3) Keep each other honest about why we act. Vow without ceremony. Community members add their marks beside it—informal pact, local mandate. Not oath to Crown or RRO, but commitment to third path. Jhace's hand shakes as he writes; Tiffani laces their fingers, lets his tremor move through both. Physical reminder: cost and commitment shared. Exit: they face uncertain future together, foundation locked.\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Walk through rebuilt district (uneven success)\n- Recognition of larger forces gathering\n- Decision: focus on what they can hold\n- Three priorities written on forge wall\n- Community witnesses/joins commitment\n- Shared physical moment (tremor through linked hands)\n- Acceptance: this is who they are together\n- Exit: foundation locked, future uncertain but faced together\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (commitment/resolution chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** Partnership formalized through shared principles (not romance, but deeper)\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stage 1 baseline accepted as new permanent state\n\n---\n\n### **EPILOGUE: \"Auditor Observation\"**\n- **POV:** Auditor (meta-perspective outside time)\n- **Setting:** Auditor's observation point (undefined location)\n- **Word Count Target:** 800-1,000 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\nAuditor catalogs N01 outcomes: two mortals built unprecedented partnership balancing Form and Wholeness without ideology, created \"third path\" threatening all factions, refused institutional offers, committed to independence, wrote three priorities and community witnessed. Prediction of institutional response\n- Turning 1 trajectory noted\n- Setup for N02 external pressure\n- Auditor's interest: useful anomaly\n\n**Purpose:** Provide meta-context, establish stakes for next novella, show Auditor's perspective on protagonists as pieces in larger game\n\n---\n\n## INTERLUDE PLACEMENT SUMMARY\n\n- **Interlude I:** After Chapter 7 (mid-Act II, after partnership forms)\n- **Interlude II:** After Chapter 10 (between crisis and resolution, shows divine forces gathering)\n- **Positioning Logic:** Interludes provide breathing space after intense chapters, offer divine/meta perspective, foreshadow external pressures coming in N02\n\n---\n\n## POV DISTRIBUTION (12 CHAPTERS)\n# 【9†N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED fileciteturn12file10\nContent Snippet:\n**Total Unique Beats:** 6 different N01 local methods across 12 chapters\n**Repeated Beats:** \n- BEAT_01 used 3x (Ch 1, 2, 5 - opening parallel exception + later observation)\n- BEAT_03 used 3x (Ch 3, 6, 10 - crisis-heavy novella, acceptable)\n- BEAT_08 used 3x (Ch 7, 11, 12 - recovery/intimacy/commitment arc)\n\n---\n\n## EMOTIONAL INTENSITY MAP\n\n**Chapter Intensity Distribution:**\n- **High (4 chapters):** 3, 10, 11, 12\n- **Medium (6 chapters):** 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 12\n- **Low (2 chapters):** 1, 2, 7\n\n**Arc Pattern:** Low opening → High crisis → Medium stabilization → High final crisis → Medium resolution\n\n---\n\n## SOULPULSE USAGE TRACKING\n\n**Total Uses in N01:** 7 confirmed uses\n- Ch 3: 2 uses (beam, building support)\n- Ch 4: 1 use (triage wall) + 1 failed attempt\n- Ch 6: 1 use (arch coordination)\n- Ch 8: 1 use (efficiency test)\n- Ch 10: 1 use (main support, near-Stage 2)\n\n**Stage Progression:**\n- Ch 1-2: Stage 0 (pre-Piercing)\n- Ch 3: Stage 0 → 1 onset\n- Ch 4-9: Stage 1 baseline\n- Ch 10: Approaching Stage 2 threshold (warning signs)\n- Ch 11-12: Stage 1 stabilized with management\n\n---\n\n## RELATIONSHIP PROGRESSION TRACKING\n\n**Arc Across N01:**\n- Ch 1-2: External awareness (reputation only)\n- Ch 3: First meeting (competence recognition)\n- Ch 4-5: Deliberate observation (measurement phase)\n- Ch 6: First coordination (partnership action)\n- Ch 7-8: Trust building (resistance → acceptance)\n- Ch 9: First explicit choice (each other over safety)\n- Ch 10: Conflict through care (control vs. autonomy)\n- Ch 11: Trauma bonding (vulnerability exchange)\n- Ch 12: Commitment formalized (shared principles)\n\n**By N01 End:** Load-bearing partnership established, functional love (not romantic confession)\n\n---\n\n## WORD COUNT BUDGET\n\n| Segment | Target | Status |\n|---------|--------|--------|\n| Ch 1 | 1,240 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 2 | 1,280 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch 3 | 2,200 | 🔒 Locked |\n| Ch
